colspan=2 | Stacked accessories !! Boost

align=left | All balloon accessories
align=left | +30% ascent speed

align=left | All Rocket Boot derivatives
align=left | +0.7 seconds of flight time

align=left | Werewolf (buff)
align=left | +4% ascent speed

align=left | Frog Leg and its upgrades
align=left | +32% ascent speed

align=left | Infinite flight time
+36% ascent speed
Increased flight agility

All wings
The player cannot equip more than one pair of wings at a time.
All wings work in water (but doing so gives the same movement speed penalty).
Some jump-boost accessories stack with wings to boost flight (see chart above).
When a pair of wings are placed in a social slot and another is placed in an accessory slot, the full animation of the vanity wings are shown, along with any lighting and particle effects. Flight duration and other functional characteristics follow those in the non main accessory slot.
Wings can be hidden when equipped and will be hidden while not in the air, otherwise they are always visible (except when using other equipment that adds graphics to the player's back, like the Leaf Blower or Turtle Scale Mail). They fold into a low-profile image when not used.
The horizontal speed of any midair jumps (such as the Cloud in a Bottle) are greatly decreased when the player wears wings alongside them.
The fall-damage-negating effect is carried over even if the wings are placed in the sixth accessory slot in an expert or master world and then the player enters a normal world.

Specific wings
Even when dyed, the Jetpack's particle effect will give off orange light, though the particles will change color according to the dye. This also applies to the Vortex Booster (which gives off teal light). As of 1.4, wings in vanity slots no longer give off light, so invisibility based characters can use this to their advantage.
Fishron Wings work in liquids, just as they do on land (including lava and honey), with the same speed, unlike other wings. This makes them very useful for more aquatic situations like building in the Ocean.
Ghost Wings  and Spectre Wings  will only change color with the use of Black Dye, which makes them appear dark gray.

Achievements
Tips
Crafted wings can be thrown into shimmer to extract the Souls of Flight, which can be used to craft better wings.
An Ice Rod can be used to lay blocks of ice to land on midair, making it possible to remain in the air indefinitely.
The slow-fall effect of Featherfall Potion or an Umbrella stacks with wing glide, allowing for very long glides after flight time has been exhausted.
Maximum flight distance of about 1400 tiles can be achieved using the Bundle of Balloons, Frog Leg, Solar Wings or Stardust Wings, Lightning Boots or derivatives, Flying Carpet, Magiluminescence, Featherfall Potion and holding the Umbrella or Tragic Umbrella.
In Expert Mode, it is possible to fly indefinitely with Soaring Insignia equipped.
The Soaring Insignia negates the low flight time of the Jetpack, allowing the player to take full advantage of its quick vertical acceleration.
Flame Wings have the best stats of all pre-Plantera wings, except Fishron Wings.
Fledgling Wings are the only wings that can be obtained pre-Hardmode.
Obtaining wings in early Hardmode:
Harpy Wings require a bit of luck when farming Harpies. Fortunately, Wyverns spawn at the same altitude, so when a Giant Harpy Feather drops, the odds are good that 20 Souls of Flight will already have been collected. The Giant Harpy Feather can also be collected before activating Hardmode.
Angel and Demon Wings require 15 or 25 (depending on version) Soul of Light or Soul of Night, respectively. The souls can easily be acquired by staying long enough in Underground Hallow or Underground Corruption/Underground Crimson, since all enemies killed in the biome have a 20% chance to drop the respective biome's soul.
Fishron Wings can be obtained anytime in Hardmode with a 1/10 chance to drop from Duke Fishron. While they may be difficult to obtain, they have the best stats before endgame.

Trivia
Dyeing Red's Wings in combination with Hermes Boots or any of its upgrades will change the color of the smoke below the player.
Prior to , Red's Wings would allow the wearer to fly infinitely and faster than any other wings.
On old-gen console and mobile, Red's Wings and some other developer wings can only be obtained through inventory editing.
When Red's Wings are obtained in the Xbox 360 or Wii U editions, they don't disappear and can be used without any problems. The other developer wings can also be obtained, but they will not give infinite flight. When other wings are used over Red's Wings as an appearance, the ascent speed bonus disappears.
It is confirmed that the wings can be obtained on PS3 consoles.
The wings disappear as soon as the world is left, if they were previously equipped or stowed in a chest.
Ghostar's Infinity Eight's name comes from the Infinity Eight in the series of single-player action role-playing games . Their appearance resembles the sword-wings of the Avatar Skeith in its Xth Form. Its tooltip, "I am right here," is used throughout the .hack//G.U. games.
Despite being non-moving constructs of technology, Ghostar's Infinity Eight and Arkhalis' Lightwings both produce flapping noises.

History

---

# 文档：Charm of Myths

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：charm of myths
- 来源：wiki/Charm_of_Myths

The Charm of Myths is a Hardmode accessory which combines the effects of the Band of Regeneration and the Philosopher's Stone. When equipped, it regenerates  1 /  0.5 health every second and decreases the duration of the Potion Sickness debuff when consuming potions by 25%.

The effect applies the instant a recovery potion is consumed. This allows players to keep the accessory in their inventory or an accessory vanity slot and quickly equip it to heal, then swap it back for another accessory.

Potion Sickness is only reduced while the accessory is equipped. Specifically, when equipped, Potion Sickness is multiplied by 0.75, and when unequipped it is divided by 0.75.
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Equipping the Charm of Myths and the Philosopher's Stone at the same time does not cause their cooldown reductions to stack.
A potion with an already reduced cooldown (such as the Restoration Potion) will be decreased from 45 to 32.5 seconds.
The Charm of Myths' Health regeneration still applies even when afflicted with the Bleeding debuff.

History

---

# 文档：Chromatic Cloak

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：chromatic cloak
- 来源：wiki/Chromatic_Cloak

The Chromatic Cloak is a Hardmode accessory which allows a more controlled Shimmering. When equipped, touching Shimmer does not inflict the Shimmering debuff, and the player can instead start Shimmering at any time while under Shimmer by holding the  key. However, after Shimmering starts, it cannot be interrupted, not even by re-equipping the Chromatic Cloak.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
The Chromatic Cloak can be replaced with the Water Walking Boots, Obsidian Water Walking Boots, Lava Waders, or Terraspark Boots, as they have the same effect as the Chromatic Cloak, allowing the player to walk on shimmer, as well as other benefits that the Chromatic Cloak does not have. Although, the Chromatic Cloak can move through shimmer without beginning to phase though blocks, unlike the other accessories listed.

History

---

# 文档：Climbing Claws

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：climbing claws
- 来源：wiki/Climbing_Claws

Climbing Claws are an accessory that allow players to slowly slide down blocks when moving against them in midair. Jumps can also be performed off the sides of blocks. By repeatedly jumping against vertical blocks, they can be scaled. When sliding down, the slide speed can be accelerated using the  key. Climbing Claws can be found in surface chests (including Water Chests) and have a  chance of being found in Wooden Crates and , and  they can also be purchased for  from the Skeleton Merchant during a Waxing Crescent.

Climbing Claws function identically to Shoe Spikes, and when both Climbing Claws and Shoe Spikes are equipped, the player can hang from the sides of blocks, sliding down with the  key. This effect can also be achieved if the Climbing Claws and Shoe Spikes are combined into the Tiger Climbing Gear at a Tinkerer's Workshop.

Tips
Its availability very early in the game makes it a useful accessory for mobility, as it allows the player to slowly but surely climb up some walls without needing Rope.

Crafting
Used in
History

---

# 文档：Clothier Voodoo Doll

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：clothier voodoo doll
- 来源：wiki/Clothier_Voodoo_Doll

The Clothier Voodoo Doll (Not to be confused with Guide Voodoo Doll) is an accessory that allows the player, Falling Stars, and other NPCs to harm and kill the Clothier NPC, while you have the item equipped. Killing the Clothier at night this way summons Skeletron. The doll has a  chance to drop from Angry Bones, Dark Casters, and Paladin in the Dungeon.

Notes
Unlike the Guide Voodoo Doll, it does not need to be destroyed to summon the respective boss and lava cannot destroy it.

Achievements
Tips
The reusable nature of this accessory allows the player to easily make a small amount of money, as Clothier drops the Red Hat upon death, worth , and Skeletron drops , barring the value of his other drops.
However, this can only be done once per night as Clothier will not respawn until day.

Trivia
The tooltip is the same as the Guide Voodoo Doll, but without a period.
In popular culture, a  is a figurine resembling a person, which can be harmed in order to magically inflict harm on the person being mimicked.

History

---

# 文档：Cloud in a Balloon

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：cloud in a balloon
- 来源：wiki/Cloud_in_a_Balloon

The Cloud in a Balloon is an accessory that combines the functionality of the Cloud in a Bottle and the Shiny Red Balloon. It boosts jump height by 33%, and jump speed by 30%, increasing the player's first jump's height to 10.5 blocks (~75% increase), and provides a double jump that hits 9 blocks, for a total height of 19.5 blocks.

Jump height is reduced when underwater, reducing your total underwater height from 15 blocks (8 + 7 from Cloud jump) to 13 blocks (7 + 6 from Cloud jump).

Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Notes
The Cloud in a Balloon can stack with separate double-jump items, such as the Sandstorm in a Balloon and Fart in a Jar. Stacking multiple items that give the same type of double-jump (in this case, Cloud) will not increase the effects of the jump.

Tips
The Cloud in a Balloon is useful when combined with wings because it will jump-start flight and increase total flight time.
The double jump can be used to negate fall damage if timed correctly.

Gallery
History

---

# 文档：Cloud in a Bottle

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：cloud in a bottle
- 来源：wiki/Cloud_in_a_Bottle

The Cloud in a Bottle is an accessory that can be found in Gold Chests in the Underground and Cavern layers. When equipped, it allows the player an extra jump. The extra jump has a 75% jump duration compared to a normal jump, increasing the maximum height the player can reach without other accessories by 5 blocks, for a total of 11 blocks.

Crafting
Used in
Notes
With the Shiny Red Balloon equipped, jump height increases by ~75%, allowing you to ascend 19½ blocks. (10½ blocks + 9 blocks from Cloud jump).
This item can stack with the Tsunami in a Bottle, Blizzard in a Bottle, Fart in a Jar, and Sandstorm in a Bottle for up to five extra jumps.
The double-jump can be used as a fall safety device; use it shortly before hitting the ground to reset your fall damage and land safely.
Each underwater jump used when swimming with the Flipper will waste a jump provided by double-jump accessories in the order that they would be used outside of water, preventing you from using them once you break the water's surface.

Tips
If the player is not planning to craft the Bundle of Balloons, it is generally recommended to upgrade to a Fart in a Jar for increased jump height.
Shimmer can be used to uncraft the Fart in a Jar and retrieve the Cloud in a Bottle, which then can be used in the crafting of a Bundle of Balloons.

Trivia
The cloud jump goes the lowest out of all the extra jumps, but it is also arguably the most commonly found.
Redigit confirmed that the Angel Statue was originally intended as a placeholder sprite for the Cloud in a Bottle .

Gallery
History
References

---

# 文档：Cobalt Shield

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：cobalt shield
- 来源：wiki/Cobalt_Shield

The Cobalt Shield is a Shield accessory that grants the player immunity from knockback when equipped. It also provides 1 defense, which stacks with other defense-granting accessories, such as Obsidian Skulls and Shackles. It is obtained from Gold Chests found in the Dungeon, or by opening Golden Lock Boxes found in Dungeon Crates.

Crafting
Used in
Tips
The protection against knockback is particularly valuable when the player is likely to come into frequent contact with enemies and environmental hazards, including but not limited to: boss fights which spawn several enemies or projectiles, Bats in the Underground, exposed lava pools, and map-generated traps in the Cavern and Dungeon.
The knockback immunity is also of particular use when the player is on the move. For instance, farming for Fallen Stars while equipping the Hermes Boots will allow no speed interruptions caused by damage, and traversing through a Corruption chasm will prevent deadly fall damage from possible knockback caused by contact from the commonly encountered Eater of Souls.
The Cobalt Shield can synergize with the  to block attacks, granting the Striking Moment buff.
Players should upgrade it quickly with Obsidian Skull to make the Obsidian Shield as it's relatively cheap, combines the defense of both, and grants immunity to the Burning debuff.

Trivia
It is one of the 6 components of the Ankh Shield that can be obtained in Pre-Hardmode, the others being the Obsidian Skull, Nazar, Megaphone, Bezoar and Adhesive Bandage.
Even though it has the word "Cobalt" in it, and visually appears similar to other Cobalt items, it cannot be crafted from Cobalt Ore / Cobalt Bars, as this item, like certain others found in the Dungeon's Locked Gold Chests/Golden Lock Boxes, contain Eastern Asian-themed items. It additionally predates Cobalt.

History

---

# 文档：Coin Ring

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：coin ring
- 来源：wiki/Coin_Ring

The Coin Ring is a Hardmode accessory created by other accessories exclusively dropped from pirate enemies during Pirate Invasion. It causes enemies to occasionally drop 1 to 10 more coins when hit and increases the pickup range for coins - the two effects provided by the materials used to create it. This can be useful for picking up money the player would otherwise miss and making farms more efficient.

Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Notes
This accessory is probably the single most useful money-making item in the game.  In particular, it is possible to wait in safety while minions defeat  enemies just outside your shelter, and the coins dropped go directly into your inventory.  Using a swarm of particularly weak minions (like a Broken Slime Staff) against an event with no casters (The Frost Legion is particularly safe) can rake in multiple platinum coins.

You can also farm in a Solar Eclipse, but the Mothrons and Reapers can travel through walls to hit you, so it is recommended to farm before defeating Plantera.

History

---

# 文档：Compass

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：compass
- 来源：wiki/Compass

The Compass is an informational accessory that enables an on-screen display of the player's lateral position when equipped. Position is shown in the number of feet (1 foot = 1/2 tile) to the East or West of the center of the map. The center of the map is usually very close to the initial spawn point. In the , the Compass has a  chance to be dropped by Salamanders, Giant Shellys, and Crawdads, a  chance to be dropped by Piranhas, and a  chance to be dropped by Mother Slimes, Snow Flinxes, Undead Vikings, and Armored Vikings. On the , it has a  chance to be dropped by Mimics. On the , it is unobtainable (no longer drops, but still exists), and is not functional.

Crafting
Used in
Notes
Unlike the "Level" depth, which is a single horizontal row of tiles (2 feet high), the 'Center' Position is actually two columns of tiles (4 feet wide). The actual center of the map is between those two columns.
The compass can drop from Piranhas and Undead Vikings spawned from statues.

History

---

# 文档：Copper Watch

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：copper watch
- 来源：wiki/Copper_Watch

The Copper Watch is a low-tier watch. The Tin Watch is an alternate to it. When equipped in an accessory slot or simply held in the inventory, a clock will appear in the upper-right corner of the playing screen, revealing the in-game time accurate to the last hour. An hour of Terraria game time is equivalent to one minute of real-world time.

Crafting
Recipes
Used in
History

---

# 文档：Countercurse Mantra

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：countercurse mantra
- 来源：wiki/Countercurse_Mantra

The Countercurse Mantra is an accessory that combines the functions of the Megaphone and the Nazar. This item grants immunity to both the Silenced and Cursed debuffs.

It is one of the ingredients required to make the Ankh Charm, which is further required to make the Ankh Shield.

Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Trivia
A  is a religious hymn believed to have psychological or spiritual powers.

History

---

- 类别：accessories

on two Target Dummies, one being behind the player.]]

Counterweights are accessories sold by the Traveling Merchant and the Skeleton Merchant. They fire a second yoyo-type projectile after hitting an enemy with a yoyo, dealing the same damage of the yoyo and traveling towards it, then arcing over the player.

There are 6 variants, all functioning identically.

# 文档：Crimson Cloak

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：crimson cloak
- 来源：wiki/Crimson_Cloak

Capes are accessories purchased from the Traveling Merchant, usually for  each. They are considered vanity items, as they provide no stats or bonuses. The  is an anomaly; it can be sold by the Clothier for  if the player has accumulated a golf score of 2000, or by the Traveling Merchant regardless of golf score.

# 文档：Critter Shampoo

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：critter shampoo
- 来源：wiki/Critter_Shampoo

Critter Shampoo is a vanity accessory equipped in a social accessory slot that changes the color of summoned minions when paired with a dye in the corresponding dye slot. It is purchased from the Zoologist for  after completing at least 30% () of the Bestiary.

Notes
Team Dye will not be applied to minions.
Sentries, the Stardust Guardian, and the Forbidden Sign cannot be dyed with the Critter Shampoo. Instead, the Stardust Guardian and Forbidden Sign can be dyed by dyeing the pet accessory slot, while sentries cannot be dyed at all.

Tips
Trivia
The bottle seems to depict a Bunny, similar to the Guide to Critter Companionship, another item sold by the Zoologist.

History

---

# 文档：Cross Necklace

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：cross necklace
- 来源：wiki/Cross_Necklace

The Cross Necklace is a Hardmode accessory that doubles the duration of the invincibility a player is afforded after taking damage, from ~0.66 seconds to ~1.33 seconds. The Cross Necklace has a   /   chance to be dropped by Mimics.

The Cross Necklace is useful in Hardmode boss fights and any other scenario where the player is likely to be hit rapidly, such as Destroyer or Skeletron Prime.

Crafting
Used in
Notes
The Star Veil and Cross Necklace invincibility effects do not stack.
The Cross Necklace will stack with the Black Belt, Master Ninja Gear, Brain of Confusion, and the Holy Protection buff to extend the dodge invincibility period by 2/3 second to a full 2 seconds.
One can become invincible if they create an invincibility chamber using a Slime Statue and a Heart Statue wired to a 1 second timer in an enclosed room and wear a cross necklace. By having the Blue Slimes hit you constantly, the cross necklace gives you almost constant invincibility.
The invincibility is ineffective in PvP.

Trivia
This item may be a reference to the Cross Necklace in the first Castlevania game, having nearly the same image and making the player invincible for a short duration.
It bears resemblance to a Catholic .

History

---

# 文档：Depth Meter

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：depth meter
- 来源：wiki/Depth_Meter

The Depth Meter is an informational accessory that displays the player's depth. It is required to craft the GPS, which can then be used to craft the PDA and the Cell Phone. The Depth Meter is dropped by Cave Bats, Giant Bats, Jungle Bats, , and Ice Bats, with a chance of   /  . On the  versions, it also drops from Salamanders, Giant Shellys, and Crawdads, with a chance of . In the , the Depth Meter may also be crafted.

On the , depth is measured in feet, with one tile equaling two feet.
On the , depth is measured in meters, with two tiles equaling one meter.
On the , depth is not measured, and the only benefits the accessory grants are from its modifiers.

Depth is measured relative to the boundary between the Surface and Underground layers, which has a depth of 0 and is displayed as "Level". The display shows the vertical distance to this line and the name of the current layer.

Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Notes
Depth is measured at the level of the player's feet.
The Depth Meter will not work if equipped in a vanity slot.
Cave Bats spawned by a Bat Statue have a  chance of dropping a Depth Meter.

Tips
The Depth Meter is useful for finding biomes that are generated at specific elevation ranges, such as Floating Islands, the Underground Jungle, and the Underworld. It also helps with locating the first three tiers of Hardmode ores.
The Depth Meter can be used to avoid spawning Dungeon Guardians by staying above 0 depth.
The Depth Meter can only be crafted with Copper, Silver, and Gold, so it might be inevitable to create another world. However, if the world count is full, you can jump through worlds, although you can instead get gold by using the Extractinator.

History

---

# 文档：Destroyer Emblem

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：destroyer emblem
- 来源：wiki/Destroyer_Emblem

The Destroyer Emblem is a Hardmode, post-Golem accessory that gives the player 10% increased damage and 8% increased critical strike chance.

Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Crafting tree
Notes
The Destroyer Emblem, Avenger Emblem, Celestial Emblem, and Warrior/Ranger/Sorcerer/Summoner Emblem have a stacking damage bonus if they are equipped simultaneously.
For summoners, it is best to keep the Avenger Emblem instead of crafting the Destroyer Emblem, since minions cannot deal critical hits.
This accessory actually gives less critical strike chance boost than the Eye of the Golem, one of its components (8% as opposed to 10%). It also gives less damage boost than the Avenger Emblem, its other component (10% as opposed to 12%).

Tips
The critical hit chance of the Destroyer Emblem can be combined with the Eye of the Golem to get a total of 18% increased critical hit chance. This can be further increased to 26% if both accessories have the Lucky modifier.
The average damage increase is 18.8%, as the critical chance increase stacks multiplicatively with the damage increase. This makes it better than the base class emblems by about 3%. This makes it the accessory with the highest classless damage bonuses.

History

---

Notes
All developer's wings have a flight time of 2.5 seconds.
Some of the coloration on Arkhalis's set depends on the color of the player's clothing.
On the , developer items are unobtainable legitimately, and can only be obtained via glitches or exploits.
Since the update to the  1.3 equivalent, any existing Sparkly Wings in a player's inventory are replaced by Cenx's Wings upon loading the game.
There are 18 sets, so each set has around a 0.3% chance of being obtained from a given treasure bag.

Trivia
Developer items, along with 0x33's Aviators, are the only Expert-exclusive items that do not have the Rainbow rarity.
Some items (unlisted) were used by the developers during game testing, and were removed from normal gameplay, making them unobtainable: Red Potion and Zapinator (the Zapinator was later released on , a different version of the Zapinator was re-released in 1.4.0.1, and the Red Potion is available in 1.4.0.1 worlds with the seeds "05162020" or "for the worthy").
The developer items were unobtainable before the 1.3.0.1 update, and, if somehow worn, most of the items would inflict a variety of debuffs on the wearer, even if worn in the vanity slot, thus rendering them useless. One of the exceptions to this rule were Red's Wings, which would give infinite flight when worn.

History

---

# 文档：Discount Card

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：discount card
- 来源：wiki/Discount_Card

The Discount Card is a rare Hardmode accessory that, when equipped, causes item prices in NPC inventories to be discounted by . The discount also applies to reforge costs. It has a  chance to be dropped by pirates (except Parrots) during Pirate Invasions. It also has a  chance to be dropped by the .

The Discount Card does not affect the following:
Sell prices (when selling items to NPCs).
Items repurchased after selling them (e.g. selling a Titan Glove for  will still cost  to take back).
Items sold by the  for Defender Medals, like his sentry summon weapons.
Costs for healing at the Nurse.

Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Notes
This item is quite rare. It takes an average of 16–17 complete Pirate Invasions before it drops.
The discount does not stack with the Greedy Ring's discount.
As of 1.4.4, it cannot be equipped or removed while in an NPC shop.
The effect of the Discount Card multiplicatively stacks with the effects of , effectively allowing the player to buy items at up to 40% off.
In  worlds, the Discount Card can be purchased from the Princess for  after a Pirate Invasion has been defeated.

Tips
Farming Lucky Coin/Discount Card/Gold Ring is most efficient from Flying Dutchman, rather than from common pirates. After getting event progress to 50%, you can ensure 10 Flying Dutchmen per event by getting high enough above ground (using small platform or flying mount), but not too high, so that spawned enemies will be in "Active Rectangle" and will despawn after 12.5 seconds, and then spawn again. Until Flying Dutchman eventually (usually it takes a short time) will spawn. The only other enemy capable of reaching you in this case is the Parrot, which will not count toward event progress anyways and can be killed safely.

History

---

# 文档：Diving Gear

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：diving gear
- 来源：wiki/Diving_Gear

The Diving Gear is an accessory which allows the user to swim and breathe underwater for an extended amount of time. It combines the functionality of the Flipper and Diving Helmet. Apart from combining the two effects into a single accessory, it does not have other unique properties.

Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Notes
Tips
The Diving Gear is one of the few items that combine a piece of armor (Diving Helmet) with an accessory (Flipper). This allows equipping a complete armor set, which usually grants a set bonus, without having to go without the single piece's functionality.
Note that while the Diving Helmet, one of the ingredients, grants +2 defense, the Diving Gear does not grant any. Contrary to the Diving Helmet, however, the Diving Gear may gain modifiers, being an accessory, potentially granting a +4 defense bonus if obtaining the Warding modifier.

History

# 文档：DPS Meter

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：dps meter
- 来源：wiki/DPS_Meter

The DPS Meter is an informational accessory purchased from the Traveling Merchant for . It displays the  (damage per second) of the weapon that last dealt damage to any entity.

Crafting
Used in
Notes
The DPS Meter takes into account every factor which actual damage is affected by as well, such as critical strike chance, enemy defense, or boosts from armor, accessories, buffs, etc.
Strangely, the DPS Meter is unable to account for critical strikes dealt by melee weapons that swing overhead, though critical hits dealt by any projectiles that they fire are recorded properly.
The DPS Meter displays the damage per real-time second instead of the damage per in-game second (60 ticks). This is apparent when the frame rate is less than 60 and when Frame Skip is enabled.

History

---

# 文档：Dunerider Boots

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：dunerider boots
- 来源：wiki/Dunerider_Boots

Dunerider Boots are an accessory that functions similarly to Hermes Boots, except that the wearer gains an additional speed boost when running on Sand Blocks, Sandstone Blocks, or Hardened Sand Blocks. They can be found in Sandstone Chests in the Underground Desert, and in Oasis Crates or Mirage Crates.

Crafting
Used in
Notes
The Dunerider Boots have a speed of 30 mph on regular tiles and a speed of 53 mph on any Sand, Sandstone, or Hardened Sand Blocks.
This effect is applied even on the Corrupted, Crimson, and Hallowed variants of those blocks.
The Dunerider Boots' effect also works with the use of Gravitation Potion or Gravity Globe. Since sand blocks are subject to gravity, they would have to be supported by Platforms in order to be walked on the underside.
When crafted together with Rocket Boots to make the Spectre Boots, the sand-boosting ability will be lost. It may be better to save them for their bonus on sand.
Using Dunerider Boots alongside Sand, Sandstone, or Hardened Sand runways could be considered a pre-Hardmode alternative to Asphalt.

Trivia
The Dunerider Boots' tooltip is a reference to the science fiction media franchise . The deadly  in Dune are attracted to rhythmic vibrations in the sand, which indicate the movement of prey. To escape the notice of the sandworms, a traveler in the desert must learn to "walk without rhythm" in a manner that simulates the natural sounds of the desert.

History

---

# 文档：Echo Chamber

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：echo chamber
- 来源：wiki/Echo_Chamber

The Echo Chamber is a furniture item. It can be activated and deactivated similarly to a monolith. When active, it grants the ability to see Echo Blocks, Echo Platforms, Echo Walls, and tiles painted with Echo Coating. It can also be equipped as a vanity accessory, which enables its effect globally for the player, similar to the Radio Thing.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Like other monoliths, the effective range of the Echo Chamber while active is 90 tiles in each direction.
It can also be activated via wiring.

Tips
When placed behind enough blocks to be completely shadowed, the floating cube on top of the Echo Chamber loses its glow, becoming effectively invisible. Shadow Paint does not cause the same result. Illuminant Coating still works, making it visible regardless of light level.

Trivia
An  is a hollow space used to produce reverberations. By extension,  can also refer to an environment in which beliefs (true or false) are magnified and reinforced by communicating solely with people who share said belief, while alternative ideas are not expressed or considered.

See also
Spectre Goggles

History

---

# 文档：Extendo Grip

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：extendo grip
- 来源：wiki/Extendo_Grip

The Extendo Grip is an accessory that extends block mining and placement reach by 3 tiles horizontally and 2 tiles vertically. It is purchased from the Traveling Merchant for .

Crafting
Used in
Notes
The effects of the Extendo Grip stack with the Toolbelt's +1 tile reach and Toolbox's +1 tile reach. However, the Toolbelt only extends block placement reach, not mining reach, while the Extendo Grip and Toolbox affect both. Therefore, equipping both items will still mean you will be able to place blocks a single tile farther away than you can mine them.
When combined with a Toolbox and Laser Drill, you can achieve +15 range.
Having this item equipped also allows players to speak to NPCs from a farther distance than normal, as well as activate levers and such. The Architect Gizmo Pack, which retains the effect of the Extendo Grip, can do the same. This can allow for hidden switches for wiring purposes and safely boxed-in NPCs to still be interacted with.
This item can be particularly useful with the Shroomite Digging Claws, as they can counteract the claws' smaller range.

Tips
This item, when equipped, can be useful to mine Hellstone at a distance, without the danger of lava.

History

---

# 文档：Eye of the Golem

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：eye of the golem
- 来源：wiki/Eye_of_the_Golem

The Eye of the Golem is a Hardmode, post-Golem accessory that grants +10% critical strike chance. It has a   /   chance of being dropped by the Golem.

Crafting
Used in
Tips
This accessory is helpful when fighting the Dungeon Guardian, as critical strikes deal more damage.
The accessory is also helpful when making AFK boss farms.
The critical hit chance of the Eye of the Golem can be combined with the Destroyer Emblem to get a total of 18% increased critical hit chance. This can be further increased to 26% if both accessories have the Lucky modifier.
By using other damage increasing accessories along with this one, like emblems and quivers, the player can massively increase their damage output.

History

While the Spectre Boots can be further crafted into the Lightning Boots, the Fairy Boots cannot. It is therefore advised to save the Spectre Boots and avoid crafting Fairy Boots.

# 文档：Fart in a Balloon

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：fart in a balloon
- 来源：wiki/Fart_in_a_Balloon

The Fart in a Balloon is an accessory that combines the effects of the Fart in a Jar and the Shiny Red Balloon. It boosts jump height by 33% and jump speed by 30%, increasing the player's first jump's height to ~10.5 blocks (~75% increase), and provides a double jump that goes to 18.5 blocks, for a total of 29 blocks. Unlike most other double-jump balloons, the Fart in a Balloon's full jump is high enough for the player to take minor fall damage, unless the player has fall damage immunity accessories like the Lucky Horseshoe. The jump also makes a gassy fart sound.

Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Notes
The Fart in a Balloon can stack with separate double-jump items, such as the Tsunami in a Bottle and the Cloud in a Bottle. Stacking multiple items that give the same type of double-jump (in this case, Fart) will not increase the effects of the jump.
The Fart in a Balloon is not one of the items required to make the Bundle of Balloons, despite one of the balloons in the sprite being green; instead, the Blizzard in a Balloon is required.

Tips
The double jump can be used to negate fall damage if timed correctly.
The double jump can be stopped midway by releasing the  button.
The Fart jump provides a speed boost when used, which can be used to quickly turn around while retaining some speed.

Gallery
History

---

# 文档：Fart in a Jar

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：fart in a jar
- 来源：wiki/Fart_in_a_Jar

The Fart in a Jar is an accessory that allows the player to perform a double jump, accompanied by a farting sound effect. The fart jump will continue to propel you upwards if the  button is held, reaching up to 10.5 blocks on its own, or 16.5 blocks when put together with the first jump. Due to this behavior, the jump behaves more similarly to the Rocket Boots than the Cloud in a Bottle. Unlike the Rocket Boots, however, the jump cannot be used in multiple bursts, and cancelling the jump will forfeit the rest of the height.

Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Notes
With the Shiny Red Balloon equipped, total jump height increases to 29 blocks (10½ blocks + 18½ blocks). Jumping to that height can result in some minor fall damage if landing at the same height that was jumped from.
This item can stack with the Cloud in a Bottle, Tsunami in a Bottle, Blizzard in a Bottle, and Sandstorm in a Bottle for up to five extra jumps.
The Fart jump is the second-best double-jump in the game, but still falls plenty short of the Sandstorm in a Bottle's 15 blocks (21 blocks when the height of the first jump is added).

Tips
The double jump can be used to negate fall damage if timed correctly.

The double jump can be stopped midway by releasing the  button.
The Fart jump provides a speed boost when used, which can be used to quickly turn around while retaining some speed.

Trivia
On the , the Whoopie Cushion sound effect can be heard while jumping with this accessory equipped.
The Fart in a Jar is the only double-jump base accessory that has "in a Jar" at the end instead of "in a Bottle", and is the only double-jump base accessory that must be crafted.
During the  in 1665, doctors suggested that patients store their farts in jars to combat bubonic plague. Doctors believed inhaling the fumes would ward off the  that came with the disease.

Gallery
History
References

# 文档：Feral Claws

- 来源：wiki/Feral_Claws

Feral Claws are an accessory that increases melee attack speed by 12%, and stacks with all other attack speed boosts. Feral Claws can be found in Jungle Crates, , and Ivy Chests located in Jungle Shrines and at the base of a Living Mahogany Tree in the Underground Jungle.

Crafting
Used in
Notes
As of 1.4.4, the autoswing feature has been rendered obsolete with the introduction of the autofire setting, allowing any weapon to be continuously swung regardless of whether or not the feral claws are equipped.
Even though this item is a melee accessory, it will also affect whips.
Trivia
Before 1.2, Feral Claws were the only pre-Hardmode offensive accessory.

# 文档：Fire Gauntlet

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：fire gauntlet
- 来源：wiki/Fire_Gauntlet

The Fire Gauntlet is a Hardmode accessory that boosts melee weapons in the following ways:
They cause the Hellfire  / On Fire!  debuff (does not apply in PvP)
+  duration
Duration !! Chance
||
||
||
Knockback: +100%
Damage: +12%  / +10%  / +9%
Attack speed: +12%  / +10%
They gain autoswing (also applies to whips)

Both the weapon sprite and projectile emit orange light particles that gives off light

Crafting
Recipes
Crafting tree
Tips
Crafting the Fire Gauntlet from the Mechanical Glove does not reduce any of its stat boosts, making it strictly an upgraded version of the latter.
One may probably find it more useful to keep the Mechanical Glove, since, unlike many other combined accessories, the Fire Gauntlet loses some functionality of its constituent parts. The Mechanical Glove gives 2%  / 3%  more damage and speed in its boost than the Fire Gauntlet, and the On Fire! damage from the Fire Gauntlet is negligible.
The Fire Gauntlet can be worn alongside the Mechanical Glove with the effects of both stacking, greatly boosting damage and melee speed. This is useful for the few hardmode weapons significantly affected by melee speed such as the Psycho Knife or Fetid Baghnakhs.

# 文档：Fish Finder

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：fish finder
- 来源：wiki/Fish_Finder

The Fish Finder is an informational accessory that provides the information displayed by the Fisherman's Pocket Guide, which displays the player's current fishing power, Weather Radio, which displays the current weather, and Sextant, which displays the moon's current phase, all of which are acquired randomly by completing quests for the Angler NPC.

Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Notes
Tips
On  and , turning off autosave can be used to force getting a particular item without having to play through multiple quests.
When completing a fishing quest, set up the character with the quest fish in the inventory, and the Angler near the spawn point. Now, turn off autosave in the "Settings" menu, "Save & Exit" the world, then rejoin.
Turn in the quest, and if the quest reward does not contain the desired item, exit the game without saving. This can be accomplished by ending ("kill") the game program "from outside", rather than with its own "Save & Exit" option. In current PC versions, the window-close button will be grayed out, but the Task Manager or + can still be used to kill the program. Each console has its own way of allowing a user to force close the game from the outside.
Now begin the loop: Turn in the quest, and if the desired item is not rewarded, exit Terraria without saving and rejoin, then turn in the quest fish again. Repeat as needed until the desired item is got.
If the current quest has a guaranteed reward (e.g., the 5th quest), it is only possible to get multiple copies of that item, instead of a random reward. Just save the game after the quest is awarded, play through to the next quest and try again.

Trivia
The item closely resembles the Dragon Radar from the Japanese media franchise .

Reference
History

---

The Fisherman's Pocket Guide is an informational accessory that displays the current Fishing Power. This accessory can be received as a   /   chance reward for completing Fishing quests for the Angler NPC.

On the , the Angler will not give you a Fisherman's Pocket Guide as a reward if you already have one in your inventory or in any of your Portable Storages. However, if you have a Fisherman's Pocket Guide, Weather Radio, and Sextant (or at least one of the accessories crafted from them: the Fish Finder, PDA, or ) in your inventories, he will be able to give you duplicate Fisherman's Pocket Guides.

# 文档：Fishing Bobbers

- 类别：accessories

- 来源：wiki/Fishing_Bobbers

The Fishing Bobbers are an accessories that provide a flat value of 10 your Fishing power when equipped.

The Fishing Bobber is acquired as a  chance reward for completing a Fishing quest for the Angler NPC. Using 5 Fallen Stars at a Tinkerer's Workshop, it can be crafted into a Glowing Fishing Bobber.

The Angler will not give the player a Fishing Bobber as a reward if they already have one in their inventory, in any of their portable storage items or loadouts. However, if the player has all of Angler's fishing and informational accessories (or their upgrades like the Angler Tackle Bag and Fish Finder) in their inventories, this rule will be ignored.

# 文档：Flame Waker Boots

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：flame waker boots
- 来源：wiki/Flame_Waker_Boots

The Flame Waker Boots are a vanity accessory obtained by opening Obsidian Crates or Hellstone Crates, found by fishing in Lava. They cause the player’s feet to leave trails of fire while moving on blocks. They are entirely cosmetic and provide no functional benefit of their own.

Crafting
Used In
Notes
The flame effect also appears when riding a Minecart across a Minecart Track laid right on top of a flat surface.
Like Meteor armor, Molten armor or Rocket Boots, dyes will also change the flame effect's color.
If the player is walking on water (e.g. using the Water Walking Potion, the Water Walking Boots, or any of its upgrades) with the Flame Waker Boots equipped, the flame effect will not appear unless the water is 1 tile or less tall.

Tips
Trivia
"Get cold feet" is a phrase that describes people not showing up to an event out of a sense of fear or anxiety despite being expected to be there, such as people not attending their own wedding or not going to a party due to anxiety despite saying they would attend.
A "waker" is one who wakens or rouses someone or something; this refers to this item's ability to rouse up flames behind the player, akin to a ship's wake. The word "waker" also appears visually similar to "walker", which was possibly intended as a pun.
This item is the only vanity accessory that is also a crafting material.

History

---

# 文档：Flesh Knuckles

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：flesh knuckles
- 来源：wiki/Flesh_Knuckles

Flesh Knuckles are a Hardmode accessory. They increase the player's defense by   / 7  and aggro by . Flesh Knuckles have a  chance to be dropped by Crimson Mimics.

Crafting
Used in
Tips
Reforging the Flesh Knuckles to the Warding modifier will grant the player a total of 12  / 11  additional defense, a very significant boost for boss fights.
If playing in cooperative multiplayer, the Flesh Knuckles synergize well with Turtle armor, Beetle armor, and Solar Flare armor, as all of these armor sets have the same effect as the Flesh Knuckles – drawing enemy aggro more frequently, likely sparing teammates with less defense from taking as much damage. The Paladin Shield, , and Frozen Turtle Shell can also be equipped for something of a "tank" build.
It can be used in multiplayer when most of the players are at low health (during boss fight or events) and only one has a good amount of health to avoid further damage to the others.
It and its upgrades can be used to make AFK farms more efficient.

Trivia
The Flesh Knuckles are the Crimson's counterpart to the Corruption's Putrid Scent, since they decrease damage received and increase the odds of being targeted, whereas the Putrid Scent increases damage dealt and decreases odds of being targeted.
This is also supported by the fact that both are dropped by their respective Mimics.
The Flesh Knuckles and  provide the second highest defense of any accessory, with  providing the highest defense.
The Flesh Knuckles provides the highest defense of any accessory.
Flesh Knuckles are based on real-life , implements worn on one's hands to both increase the damage caused by a punch and reduce the damage from the shock on the user's hands. Despite the name, they are available in several materials, including non-metals such as .
The Flesh Knuckles' palm grip, in addition to being made of bone, appears to be spiked, which would make them realistically impractical to wear due to it stabbing into the wearer's hand.

History

---

# 文档：Flipper

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：flipper
- 来源：wiki/Flipper

The Flipper is an accessory that allows a player to swim in liquid. With the Flipper equipped in an accessory slot, the player can swim upwards in Water, Lava, or Honey, by pressing  repeatedly &mdash; essentially allowing unlimited multi-jumps while within liquid. The Flipper is found in Water Chests, which are found submerged in water. They can also be found in  and .

Crafting
Used in
History

# 文档：Flower Boots

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：flower boots
- 来源：wiki/Flower_Boots

Flower Boots are a rare accessory which cause flowers to grow when the player walks on regular, Jungle, and Hallowed grass. These flowers are functionally the same as the tall weeds that grow naturally on grass, and drop Seeds and Hay normally.

On the , wearing Flower Boots drastically decreases the chance of critters spawning from destroyed tall grass and plants.

Flower Boots can occasionally be found in Ivy Chests,  Jungle Crates and .

Crafting
Used in
Notes
When walking on regular grass or Hallowed grass blocks, there is a 50% chance for a short flower and a 50% chance for a tall flower to generate. See the overview to the right for the flowers which can be generated. When walking on Jungle grass blocks, all flowers generated are tall ones.
No flowers are generated if the player is currently using a  or if the block is covered by any liquid or hammered in any way.
When wearing Flower Boots, the chance of a critter spawning from destroyed plants is reduced to  of the original chance. For instance, a Grasshopper has a  chance to spawn from destroyed tall grass and plants. With the Flower Boots equipped, it only has a  chance to spawn. This applies to all tall grass and flowers, even if they were not generated by a player with Flower Boots.
Flowers are generated every tick, if the conditions are fulfilled. This means that flowers will generate even when standing still: Any flora destroyed will instantly be replaced with a new flower where the player is standing.
On , flowers will only generate when the player is moving.
Neither Plantera's Bulbs nor Life Fruits will be grown by the boots when walking on Jungle grass.
When using reversed gravity, flowers will grow on the grass block that the head touches, if there is a 3-tiles tall gap for the player to fit in. The block which the player is actually standing on will not sprout upside-down flowers, nor will it sprout vines.

Tips
When combined with the Sickle, they can be used to quickly generate large amounts of Hay.
It may be unwise to have Flower Boots equipped permanently, as they essentially eliminate the chance of obtaining critters for bait from destroyed plants.

Trivia
Upgrading the Flower Boots to  will decrease the rarity from  to .
True to their name, the Flower Boots will always cause flowers to grow, never ordinary tall grass. This discrepancy is purely aesthetic – flowers have the exact same properties as tall grass.

History
Footnotes

---

# 文档：Flurry Boots

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：flurry boots
- 来源：wiki/Flurry_Boots

Flurry Boots are an accessory that can be found in Frozen Chests in the underground Snow Biome, as well as  and . When equipped, they allow the player to gain a speed boost after running for a certain distance continuously. Your speed will continue increasing until maximum speed is attained. At full sprint, they will increase your horizontal movement speed by about 40%. (See Sprinting accessories for more information on the sprint effect.)

The Flurry Boots, Hermes Boots, Dunerider Boots and the Sailfish Boots have identical functionality, with their only differences being aesthetic (and being faster on Sand Blocks, in the case of the Dunerider Boots). In the case of the Flurry Boots, the user will leave a short dust trail under themselves as they run, which disappears immediately upon stopping, unlike the Hermes Boots.

All four listed boots are interchangeable when crafting Spectre Boots.

Crafting
Used in
Notes
This item increases the player's base speed from 15 mph to 30 mph.
It can be equipped with the Hermes Boots or Sailfish Boots, but these effects do not stack.

History

---

# 文档：Flying Carpet

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：flying carpet
- 来源：wiki/Flying_Carpet

The Flying Carpet is an accessory that allows the player to float for about four seconds, similar to the Hoverboard. It can only be found inside Chests located within Pyramids in the Desert.

The Flying Carpet is activated by holding  in mid-air, after all double-jumping accessories are used. Once it is activated, the player is put on an imaginary horizontal rail. Releasing the key deactivates the carpet, but it may be reactivated at a lower height if there is still flight time left.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
In the , the Flying Carpet activates automatically after jumping.
The Flying Carpet will not propel the player upwards on its own.
The carpet is only visible when being used.
The Flying Carpet moves at a speed of  and is affected by movement speed bonuses.

Tips
With practice, it is possible to closely estimate total flight time to maximize usage efficiency.
With skillful use of a hook, the carpet will allow for one of the best early-game mobility options.
While rapidly pressing the  key allows the Flying Carpet to stay afloat twice as long, this is rather impractical.
Paired with a long-ranged weapon, it can be very useful when fighting most enemies.
The Flying Carpet is a useful early-game tool for catching flying critters, due to its high speed and ability to stay at the right height until the critter glides to normal flying height.
Using the Flying Carpet allows the player to very quickly ascend single-block slopes and staircases.
It can be used together with Shoe Spikes or Climbing Claws to imitate the Tiger Climbing Gear's ability to remain at a fixed height on a wall, albeit rather briefly.

History

# 文档：Frostspark Boots

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：frostspark boots
- 来源：wiki/Frostspark_Boots

The Frostspark Boots are an accessory crafted by combining Lightning Boots and Ice Skates together at a Tinkerer's Workshop. It provides the benefits of both. They also have an 8% increased movement speed.

Frostspark Boots display a different particle effect than the Spectre Boots or Lightning Boots: they produce snow clouds similar to those made by the Blizzard in a Bottle.

Crafting
Recipes
Crafting tree
Used in
Notes
This item increases the player's base speed from 15 mph to 34 mph.
If the player runs into a wall with a 1- to 2-block opening in the floor next to the wall, the player will sometimes fall through.
This item will stack with wings, granting a small flight time bonus to them.

Tips
It may be preferable to use the first Ice Skates that the player obtains to create the Arctic Diving Gear, as this provides a few extra benefits over the Frostspark Boots – namely, the ability to enter water in Snow biomes in  without receiving the Chilled debuff, and the ability to emit a blue glow while underwater, rather than the pink glow of the Jellyfish Necklace. However, in Hardmode the Arctic Diving Gear is made less useful by the Neptune's Shell – crafting the Frostspark Boots will grant the effects of the Ice Skates and the other boots in a single slot, while the Arctic Diving Gear requires two slots for the same effect.

Trivia
They were incorrectly called "Sparkfrost Boots" in the Terraria 1.2.1.2 changelog. Their name still appears as such in some places within the game's source code.
This accessory is one of the few pre-Hardmode accessories to have a base rarity of .
The color of the running particle uses a different dye slot than that of the flying particle. This is easily seen when using Intense Flame Dye.
The snow particles from the Frostspark Boots fall as fast as the player is falling.

History

---

# 文档：Frozen Shield

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：frozen shield
- 来源：wiki/Frozen_Shield

The Frozen Shield is a post-Plantera Hardmode shield accessory combining the functionalities of Paladin's Shield and the Frozen Turtle Shell, redirecting some damage done to teammates to the player, increasing defense by 6, and providing the player with a damage-absorbing shield when they are at low health.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
The low-health shield does not stack with Frozen Turtle Shell, and the team damage redirection effect does not stack with the Paladin's Shield or the Hero Shield.
When the player's health is between 25% and 50%, they both give the Paladin's Shield buff to nearby teammates and get the Ice Barrier buff at the same time.

Tips
Although technically available later in the game than the Ankh Shield,  the Frozen Shield is a great alternative because of its defensive abilities and much simpler crafting tree, especially if debuff resistance is not necessary.
The Frozen Shield can be considered more effective than the Ankh Shield during single-player boss fights, given that most boss encounters do not cause debuffs that can be prevented by the Ankh Shield, and the Paladin's Shield effect will not cause the player to take increased damage.
With the Warding modifier, the Frozen Shield has 10 defense and a 25% damage reduction when the player is below 50% health, making it an extraordinarily strong defense accessory in single-player.

History

---

# 文档：Frozen Turtle Shell

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：frozen turtle shell
- 来源：wiki/Frozen_Turtle_Shell

The Frozen Turtle Shell is a Hardmode accessory that has a   /   chance of being dropped by the Ice Tortoise, an enemy found in the Ice biome. It grants the Ice Barrier buff when equipped, with the effects varying between versions:
Gives 25% damage reduction as long as their health remains at or below 50%.
Gives the player 30 defense when below 25% health.

While not normally visible, it displays as a small blue circle spinning around the character that emits light when active.

Crafting
Used in
Tips
This accessory works well with the Shrimpy Truffle, which gives the player a damage and speed boost when at or below 50% life.
The Frozen Turtle Shell is useful for fighting bosses and events, which usually expose the player to constant damage, thus activating the accessory more often than during regular gameplay.
Turning on autopause and swapping to this accessory when below 50% life may be advisable.

Trivia
Despite it being a Turtle Shell, it is dropped by the Ice Tortoise.
The buff can be canceled, although it will immediately activate again.

History

# 文档：Gentleman's Beard

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：gentlemans beard
- 来源：wiki/Gentleman's_Beard

The Gentleman's set is a set of vanity items consisting of the Gentleman's Beard, the Gentleman's Long Beard, the Gentleman's Magnificent Beard, the Gentleman's Vest, and the Gentleman's Trousers. The Vest and Trousers can be crafted, while the Gentleman's Beard can be bought from the Stylist for .

When equipped in an accessory slot, the Gentleman's Beard will grow longer over time. After 12 minutes of being equipped, the beard has a  chance every second to grow to the next stage. This growth is not affected by speeding up time, and will not occur when the beard is equipped in a vanity slot. The beard also changes colors based on the player's hair color.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Trivia
This set is a reference to  in the video game .
The tooltip of all variants of the Beard is a reference to 's examination quote for the  in Don't Starve.

See also
Firestarter's set

# 文档：Goblin Tech

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：goblin tech
- 来源：wiki/Goblin_Tech

The Goblin Tech is an informational accessory, and is a combination of the Stopwatch, DPS Meter, and Metal Detector, displaying the most valuable nearby item, your current , and your speed. The Stopwatch and DPS Meter are sold by the Traveling Merchant, while the Metal Detector is dropped by the Nymph.

Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Notes
Trivia
The texture of Goblin Tech looks like the texture of its components put together.
Despite being called the "Goblin Tech", none of the components are obtained from a Goblin.
However, the crafting station, the Tinkerer's Workshop, is obtained from the Goblin Tinkerer.
The Goblin Tech is one of the hardest items to get for the PDA, surpassed only by the Fish Finder, because of the fact that the Metal Detector only drops from Nymphs.

# 文档：Gold Ring

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：gold ring
- 来源：wiki/Gold_Ring

The Gold Ring is a Hardmode accessory that rarely drops from enemies exclusive to the Pirate Invasion, with the exception of Parrots. It has a  drop chance from the Pirate Captain and a  drop chance from the Flying Dutchman. Every other enemy has a  chance to drop it.

Equipping the Gold Ring increases the player's coin pickup radius to 25 tiles, instead of the normal 3-tile radius. This item works similarly to the Celestial Magnet,  and Heartreach.

Crafting
Used in
Notes
The Gold Ring can be useful for picking up dropped coins from flying enemies, collecting coins from an enemy farm, and more.
It is the least rare of the ingredients for the Greedy Ring, so it is mostly likely to be obtained farming for the Lucky Coin or the Discount Card.

Tips
Farming Lucky Coin/Discount Card/Coin Ring is most efficient from Flying Dutchman, rather than from common pirates. After getting event progress to 50%, the player can ensure 10 Flying Dutchmen per event by getting high enough above ground (using a small platform or a flying mount), but not too high, so that spawned enemies will be in the "Active Rectangle" and will despawn after 12.5 seconds, and then spawn again. Eventually, the Flying Dutchman will spawn (it usually takes a short time). After killing it, repeat the process until the event ends. Parrots could reach the player when using this method, but they can be killed safely as they do not count towards event progress.
This item and its upgrades can be useful for mining large areas, as they help prevent the buildup of excess coins.

Trivia
The Gold Ring is visually very similar to the Diamond Ring. While the Gold Ring's gems have a slightly blue hue and the gem in the middle has small golden spots on the side, the Diamond Ring consists of completely white gems.

History

---

# 文档：Gold Watch

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：gold watch
- 来源：wiki/Gold_Watch

The Gold Watch is a high-tier watch. The Platinum Watch is an alternative to it. When equipped in an accessory slot or simply held in the inventory, a clock will appear in the upper-right corner of the playing screen, revealing the in-game time accurate to the last minute. A minute of Terraria game time is equivalent to a second of real-world time. It is required for crafting the GPS and 1 Second Timer.

Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Notes
It will not show the time when the inventory is open or when it is equipped in a Vanity slot.
It must be equipped to work in the . It will not display the time if it is in the inventory.

History

---

# 文档：Golf Balls

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：golf balls
- 来源：wiki/Golf_Balls

Golf Balls are accessories that are used in golf and can be purchased from the Golfer for  each. Golf Balls come in a variety of colors.

They can be used to place golf balls in the world. They can be used either by right-clicking a Golf Tee, starting a swing with a Golf Club in front of a Golf Tee or simply right-clicking the air while it is selected to drop/throw the ball. If a player doesn't have a golf ball equipped or in their inventory, right-clicking on a golf tee will create a plain white ball.

Golf Balls that have been placed are projectiles that can be hit with a Golf Club and despawn when entering a Golf Cup.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
Golf Balls do not need to be placed in the accessory slot; simply carrying it around in the inventory will allow the player to place golf balls.
Because placed golf balls are considered projectiles, they can trigger teal pressure pads.
Golf Balls can be placed in the world an unlimited number of times and do not need to be picked up, so there is often little reason to buy more than one.
The player's camera follows the golf ball when it is struck and if the player does not move after hitting it. This can be used to locate Floating Islands and other hidden structures.
You can hit multiple golf balls with one swing, and thus potentially make multiple holes-in-one even into the same hole.
Doing this will make the camera follow one of the golf balls and the rest will not be followed.

Notes
Because the Golf Ball is considered an accessory, it can be reforged at the Goblin Tinkerer.
You can score outside world borders using TEdit to place a Golf Cup outside the world.
If you hit a Golf Ball up past the upper world boundary, it will stay up there and it will not come down. The arrow indicating where your Golf Ball is will also point up there.

History

---

# 文档：GPS

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：gps
- 来源：wiki/GPS

The GPS is an informational accessory that enables an on-screen display of the player's current depth, lateral position, and time.

Depth is displayed as follows:
On the  of the game, depth is measured in feet, with one tile equivalent to two feet.
On the , depth is measured in meters, with two tiles equivalent to one meter.

Crafting
Recipes
Crafting tree
Used in
Notes
Tips
If you have access to these crafting materials and you are not planning on selling it, make the GPS so that it doesn't take up as much space in your inventory/accessory slots.
Selling the GPS is more profitable than selling its separate parts ( for the Gold Watch or  for the Platinum Watch,  for the Depth Meter, and  for the Compass).
However, since it will probably be necessary for the player to craft the watch themselves, they should consider the cost of its components instead. In the worst case, if the world has platinum and the player does not have any spare bars, they will need  Platinum Ore to craft the watch, which sell for  on their own, making a measly  of profit, which can even be negated if the two other accessories have particularly good modifiers.

Trivia
GPS stands for "", which in real life is used to track the user's location.
The GPS was the first combined informational accessory, existing far before any of the other accessories used to craft the .

History

---

# 文档：Gravity Globe

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：gravity globe
- 来源：wiki/Gravity_Globe

The Gravity Globe is an Expert Mode Accessory found in the Moon Lord's Treasure Bag. When worn, it allows the holder to flip gravity, so that the player falls up instead of down, and toggle gravity back to normal afterward. Once equipped, pressing the  key toggles the gravity direction. This only affects the player with the item equipped.

Notes
Gravity cannot be changed in mid-air. The player must be standing on the ground or ceiling to reverse gravity.
If the character hits the upper limit of the map while gravity is reversed, gravity will revert to normal. This will not reset the flight timer.
Players falling upwards will not be stopped by platforms, and will instead fall through them.  Hammering a platform three times will allow the player to "land" on it upside down, and the flight time will be reset.
The Gravity Globe doesn't change gravity while grappled, not even while gravity is flipped.
Mounts cannot be summoned while in reversed gravity.
The Wisp in a Bottle, as well as its controls, remains upside down if the player is in reversed gravity.
Fishing is possible on reverse gravity; the fishing bobber will fall "upward" into the water. However, golf cannot be done on reverse gravity; the player will not even be able to swing the club.
If the Gravity Globe is unequipped while in inverted gravity, gravity will return to normal.

Tips
It is useful in the Dungeon for quick ascents up the zigzag corridors.
The Gravity Globe can be a useful accessory when fighting the Dungeon Guardian.
It is basically a permanent version of the Gravitation Potion, so you can feel free to empty your inventory of said potions into one of your chests when you obtain this

See also
Gravitation Potion, a potion which affects the player in a similar manner.

History

---

# 文档：Greedy Ring

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：greedy ring
- 来源：wiki/Greedy_Ring

The Greedy Ring is a Hardmode accessory that combines the Discount Card's 20% discount off at any NPC shop, the Lucky Coin's 20% chance of spawning 1-10 extra coins whenever an enemy is hit, and the Gold Ring's increased coin pickup range.

Crafting
Recipes
Crafting tree
Notes
All items required for crafting it are rare drops from Pirates and the Flying Dutchman, but not Parrots.
The Greedy Ring does not stack with any of the items in its crafting tree.
The Greedy Ring does not affect the prices of items that require defender medals.
As of 1.4.4, it cannot be equipped or removed while in an NPC shop.

Tips
Equipping this will make coin farms more efficient, and can be very useful for gathering coin ammunition for the Coin Gun.
Despite the money-making and money-saving potential of the Greedy Ring and its components, none of them are particularly valuable when sold. This, combined with their rarity, makes them much more valuable as multiplayer trade items.
Equipping this item before reforging your items can make a world of difference, especially because the Goblin Tinkerer can sometimes repeat prefixes, good or bad.

Trivia
The sprite strongly resembles a golden ring with three rubies, being made of valuable materials, hence the "greedy" part of the name.
The Greedy Ring is one of the most difficult items to obtain, given that the components have some of the lowest drop chances in the game.

History

---

# 文档：Green Horseshoe Balloon

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：green horseshoe balloon
- 来源：wiki/Green_Horseshoe_Balloon

The Green Horseshoe Balloon is an accessory that grants immunity to fall damage, increases jump height by 33%, jump speed by 30%, and grants a double jump. The height of the first jump is increased from 6 blocks to 10.5 blocks (~75% increase), while the double jump's height is increased from 10.5 blocks to 18.5 blocks, reaching a total of 29 blocks with both jumps. It also has the fart sound of the previous fart double jumps.

Jump height is oddly reduced when underwater, to 18 blocks (7 blocks + ~11 blocks from Fart jump).

Crafting
Recipes
Crafting tree
Notes
None of the Horseshoe Balloons can be crafted with an Obsidian Horseshoe. The original Lucky Horseshoe must be used.

Multiple double-jump items be worn at once for up to five extra jumps. However, equipping multiple balloons will not stack the jump height bonus, nor will stacking additional Horseshoes have any extra effect.
The Fart in a Jar and Fart in a Balloon will not grant any additional bonuses when equipped alongside the Green Horseshoe Balloon.

Tips
The double jump can be stopped midway by releasing the  button.
The Fart jump provides a speed boost when used, which can be used to quickly turn around while retaining some speed.
Due to the unique significant speed boost, and being the second highest jump in the game just slightly behind the Yellow Horseshoe Balloon, it can be considered the best double jump accessory depending on the preference of the player.
The Green Horseshoe Balloon is available in any world given that you grind for a woopee cushion drop, as opposed to the Yellow Horseshoe Balloon being limited to worlds containing pyramids or the celebration seed.

Gallery
History

---

# 文档：Guide to Plant Fiber Cordage

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：guide to plant fiber cordage
- 来源：wiki/Guide_to_Plant_Fiber_Cordage

The Guide to Plant Fiber Cordage is an accessory that, when equipped, allows the player to collect Vine Rope when destroying vines. It can be found inside surface Chests or in Wooden Crates obtained from fishing. On  can be purchased from Skeleton Merchant for  during First Quarter.

Notes
It works on regular and Jungle vine, but it does not work on Hallowed or Crimson vine (there are no Corruption vines).
In single-player, this affects any vines that are destroyed by whatever means, including breaking the blocks they hang from.  Minions can also break vines as they shoot at passing enemies, and these too will drop as vine rope.
In Multiplayer, vines will only drop if the player closest to the vine has the Guide to Plant Fiber Cordage equipped, regardless of who destroyed them.

Tips
The Vine Rope produced by this item is identical to ordinary Rope in every way but appearance (which is more visible), and vines are naturally occurring and abundant. Finding this item early can greatly decrease the difficulty of exploring deep caves before acquiring mobility aids like Grappling Hooks.
It is especially useful in Large worlds, where a single hellevator can use well over a 999-stack of ropes just for the straight distance.  With this item, a fresh stack of vine ropes can be gathered in a few minutes of bouncing around surface or Jungle caves.

Trivia
The symbol on the book appears to be a Vine Rope, the item this book allows the player to collect.
The book seems to be closely related to the Guide To Critter Companionship, which has a similar name and sprite. However, unlike the Guide To Critter Companionship, this item is an accessory, and does not work if it is simply inside the player's inventory.

History

---

# 文档：Guide Voodoo Doll

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：guide voodoo doll
- 来源：wiki/Guide_Voodoo_Doll

The Guide Voodoo Doll is a summoning item for the Wall of Flesh. It is a guaranteed drop from Voodoo Demons, a fairly rare enemy in The Underworld.

When the doll is equipped as an accessory, it becomes possible for the player and other NPCs to kill the Guide with direct attacks from weapons (which normally would not damage him). If the item is dropped and destroyed by lava, then the Guide will instantly die, if he exists in the world. If the doll is destroyed in The Underworld (and the Guide exists), it will summon the Wall of Flesh.

As of 1.4.4 Guide Voodoo Dolls can be stacked up to 9,999. Throwing a stack of more than one into the lava will summon the Wall of Flesh, and kill the Guide, as well as killing a number of other randomly chosen NPCs in the world (including town pets and town slimes), equal to the number of dolls that were thrown. There are no other effects to doing this, however.

Notes
Only one Wall of Flesh can be summoned at once, so throwing a second doll into lava while a Wall of Flesh is currently spawned will waste the doll.
If a dropped doll lands on a player who is standing in lava, the doll will sometimes be collected while also summoning the Wall of Flesh, making it reusable.
The Wall of Flesh's sweep direction is determined by where the doll is dropped. If to the left of the world center (the player's initial world spawn point at the start of the game), the boss sweeps from left to right, and vice versa.
Throwing the doll into the closest lava sea to the left or right end will cause the Wall of Flesh to despawn right after it spawns.
On the , the Guide Voodoo Doll is not required to summon the Wall of Flesh. It can also be summoned by directly killing the Guide via lava in the Underworld.

Achievement
Trivia
In popular culture, a  is a figurine resembling a person, which can be harmed in order to magically inflict harm on the person being mimicked.
Before the Wall of Flesh is defeated, several NPCs sometimes make reference to the doll's function:
Arms Dealer: ""
Nurse: ""
Guide: ""
Its old sprite appears on the icons of these following buffs and debuffs:

The Guide Voodoo Doll is one of only four pre-Hardmode boss summoning items that cannot be crafted. The others are the Clothier Voodoo Doll, , and the .

Throwing a stack of dolls will kill multiple NPCs, such as it killing the guide and merchant if you throw 2 inside.

History

---

# 文档：Hand Of Creation

- 类别：accessories
- 标识：hand of creation
- 来源：wiki/Hand_Of_Creation

The Hand Of Creation is a post-Goblin Army accessory for building, crafted at the Tinkerer's Workshop using the Architect Gizmo Pack, Ancient Chisel, Treasure Magnet, and Step Stool.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Despite this Accessory supposedly being the ultimate Building Accessory, there are still multiple items which aren't included in the recipe. Those are the Presserator, the Toolbelt, the Toolbox and the Spectre Goggles. This is likely because the Toolbox is exclusive to the Christmas Event and the Spectre Goggles are bought from the Cyborg, which would make the Hand of Creation a post-Plantera item and thus unobtainable until much later into progression.
The effects of this Accessory do not stack with the Ancient Chisel or the Step Stool.

The Hellfire Treads are functionally identical to the Spectre Boots, except for the purely cosmetic trail of flames. Although the Hellfire Treads are a combination of two items, only one has actual effects. If equipped in a vanity slot, the trail of flames is still visible.

The Hercules Beetle is a Hardmode, post-Plantera accessory that increases the damage of summon weapons by 15% and increases their knockback by 2. It is sold by the Witch Doctor for  if he is spoken to in the Jungle after Plantera has been defeated.

Crafting
Used in
Notes
Despite the tooltip implying that the accessory only affects minions, it also affects sentries and .

Trivia
The  is a real-life insect. It is the largest species of rhinoceros beetle. They are also one of the overall largest beetles in the world.
As the item grants 15% increased summon damage and increased summon knockback, it can be considered an upgrade to the Summoner Emblem.
