## ARMOR
共 96 篇文档

# 文档：Armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：armor
- 来源：wiki/Armor

Armor is a set of equipable defense items that reduce damage taken from enemies and most other damage sources. Armor pieces are equipped by placing them in the armor slots of the player's inventory. Armor can also be placed in vanity slots to change the player's appearance without affecting stats. Armor pieces are either crafted, purchased from NPCs, or dropped from enemies.

Each piece of armor has a defense statistic that reduces inflicted damage by half /  /  its value, rounded up. For example, a full set of Copper armor provides a total of  defense, reducing damage by  /  /  points. Silver armor grants a total of  defense, reducing damage by  /  /  points, after having been rounded up. An attack cannot normally be reduced to zero; all attacks will always deal at least 1 damage (or 2 damage for a critical hit), regardless of the quality of a player's armor. The only times an attack can do 0 damage is if the player has invincibility for whatever reason or there is another player on the same team with the Paladin's Shield equipped and the attack does 1 damage normally.

In addition to the defense provided by individual armor pieces, most armor types provide a Set Bonus when all three pieces of the same armor type are worn simultaneously. For the basic pre-Hardmode ore armors (Copper through Platinum), this is an additional defense bonus. More advanced or unique armors can provide other specialized set bonuses (e.g.,  the Shroomite armor will provide invisibility when not moving).}}

Achievements
Notes
The chestplate on each armor set changes with the gender of the character, with the female breastplate often having an exposed cleavage, stomach or both. This is a purely visual change and does not increase or decrease defense.
The ancient armor pieces are shown in the inventory to be facing to the left instead of the right.
The default Hardmode ore armors (Cobalt, Mythril, and Adamantite) are often mistaken as direct downgrades to the others (Palladium, Orichalcum, and Titanium). Though it is true they all have lower armor value (except for Adamantite) and don't have fancy effects, they often give better, more class-specific bonuses (e.g. Cobalt Melee Set is still the second-best melee speed armor only bested by the Hallowed Melee Set). The default Hardmode ore armors also do not require as many bars to create, making swift progression easier.
For most Hardmode armors with helmet variants, the helmets sell for more than other pieces of their respective sets, despite needing fewer bars to make. This is due to the helmet carrying class-specific bonuses or other special abilities. For example, the melee helmet of such sets will hold melee modifiers and a disproportionate share of Defense.
If armor pieces are placed into accessory slots using third-party tools, they will not give their defense and stat buffs.

Mixed armor sets
While set bonuses are a strong incentive to use a matched set, there are many cases where the powers of individual pieces can be combined usefully, especially late in the game.  Particularly versatile pieces include the  Valhalla Knight's Breastplate for its regeneration and the Forbidden Treads for the extra 2 minions (though the Forbidden Treads now provide only 1 extra minion in version 1.4, making them less ideal).  Some examples include:
The Turtle chest piece can be combined with the Hallowed or Chlorophyte Mask and Hallowed Greaves for massively increased melee damage, but at the cost of losing the Thorns effect, a bit of defense, and most of the "enemy magnet" effect.  With the Hallowed Mask, this nets a defense of 62 (against 65 for the full Turtle set), +18% melee damage,  +18% melee critical chance, and +10% melee speed.
With the Chlorophyte Mask instead, the defense goes up a point to 63 and the melee damage bonus rises to +28%, but the melee critical chance bonus drops to +14% and the melee speed bonus is lost.  Either way, the wearer gets another +7% for all damage and +8% movement speed.
Doing this with the Valhalla Knight's Breastplate instead, gets another 3 points of defense, the regeneration, and a +30% bonus to summon damage, at the cost of some of the melee bonuses (leaving +10% or +20% damage, +10% or +6% critical chance).
The combination of the Chlorophyte Helmet and Shroomite Breastplate (or in the endgame, Vortex Breastplate) provides more ammo reduction than any set bonus. (See Sandgun for further discussion.)
A regenerative Melee/Summoner set consisting of the Chlorophyte Mask, Valhalla Knight's Breastplate, and Valhalla Knight's Greaves provides +16% melee damage, +26% melee critical strike chance, +30%  movement speed, +50% minion damage, and +8 health/second regeneration.
For rangers, if the set bonus for Shroomite armor is not needed, the chest piece can be swapped with the Valhalla Knight's Breastplate for the regeneration. Those who use this approach may also wish to swap out the Shroomite Leggings for Forbidden Treads; the extra two minions (in versions earlier than 1.4) can provide a larger total DPS increase despite losing some movement speed and ranged critical bonus.
For magic users, Hallowed Headgear, Valhalla Knight's Breastplate, and Dark Artist's Leggings grants +100 mana capacity, +12% magic damage, +37% magic critical strike chance, +50% minion damage, and +8 health/second regeneration.
Summoners can summon their minions while wearing the Shinobi Infiltrator's Helmet, Valhalla Knight's Breastplate, and Red Riding Leggings for maximum damage bonuses, swap to the Spooky Helmet to summon one more, swap to the full Spooky Armor set to summon two more, before re-equipping the Forbidden Treads and Valhalla Knight's Breastplate (in that order). This grants high survivability while retaining decent minion damage/capacity bonuses, and is the strongest possible pre-Moon Lord summoning loadout that uses the Breastplate.

References

---

# 文档：Adamantite armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：adamantite armor
- 来源：wiki/Adamantite_armor

Adamantite armor is a Hardmode armor set. Like most early-Hardmode armors, has three different helmet options, allowing the player to focus on magic, melee or ranged combat; each option changing the armor's set bonus. Crafting a one-helmet set requires 54 Adamantite Bars, made from   /   Adamantite Ore. Crafting all five pieces requires 78 Adamantite Bars, made from   /   Adamantite Ore. The Adamantite armor's alternative is Titanium armor.

A full set of Adamantite armor gives off a faint pulsing aura around the character. This effect is purely visual.

Set
Helmet variants
Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Tips
If the player changes their mind, the helmet piece can be shimmered and crafted into an alternative.
When compared to Titanium armor, Adamantite armor grants higher offensive stats overall:
The magic set for Adamantite provides less damage boosts than Titanium, but does offer more critical strike chance and mana boosts when the set bonus is considered. It also provides 2 more defense.
The melee set for Adamantite provides more damage bonuses, (melee speed, and movement speed with set bonus) and critical strike bonuses when compared to Titanium. It also provides 1 more defense.
The ranger set for Adamantite provides less damage boosts over Titanium, but has higher critical strike chance, and has a 25% chance not to consume ammo (set bonus). It also provides 2 more defense.
While Adamantite armor might be chosen because it provides higher offensive stats than Titanium armor, it does not provide the highest offensive stats available. If the goal is simply to maximize damage output, there are better options:
Mythril armor is significantly stronger for melee users, though it has lower defense.
Ranged users (and melee users who do not wish to use Mythril armor) can maximize their offensive bonuses by equipping a Frost Breastplate and Mythril Greaves while keeping the Adamantite Mask or Helmet in the head slot.
Adamantite armor is a decent choice for magic users, who might appreciate the full set's defense and mana cost reduction. Those who are more confident in their dodging skills can maximize their offense with a set consisting of Adamantite Headgear, Mystic Robe, and .
In Expert Mode,  is effectively stronger than Adamantite armor because it frees up an accessory slot that would normally be occupied by the Shield of Cthulhu, allowing an extra offensive accessory (such as an emblem or any other class-specific accessory) to be worn instead.
Melee players may not find it worth it to upgrade to Hallowed armor, as Adamantite armor has the same amount of defense (50) and higher raw damage increase. However, it could be worth crafting the Hallowed armor for the Holy Protection buff, if the player is going for a "tank style" of play.
The set bonus with the helmet 20% increased melee and movement speed can prove to be useful for some early to mid-hardmode movement speed builds, pairing it with the Swiftness potion gives an extra 25%, an anklet of the wind for an extra 10% an aglet for an extra 5%, and some lightning boots for an extra 8%, putting the “Quick” modifier will give an extra 12% resulting in 80% increased movement speed.

Trivia
Both the headgear and helmet armor sets have more defense than the equivalent sets of Titanium armor, which makes it the only original armor to have more defense than its alternative.
While the Adamantite Mask appears to provide the greatest face protection and looks similar to many other melee-specialized masks, it has lower defense than the Adamantite Helmet and provides ranged bonuses.
The full Adamantite set equipped with the helmet looks similar to Crimson armor.

History

---

# 文档：Amber Robe

- 类别：armor
- 标识：amber robe
- 来源：wiki/Amber_Robe

The Amber Robe is an Armor item and part of the Wizard set, being wearable in the shirt slot. It is the second strongest Gem Robe, only being weaker than the Diamond Robe.

Wearing the Wizard Hat alongside the Amber Robe provides a set bonus of +10% magic critical strike chance while wearing a Magic Hat alongside it provides a set bonus of +60 Mana. Any leg-slot equipment can be worn with the set bonuses remaining intact.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
This item is the armor analog to the Amber Staff.

History

---

# 文档：Amethyst Robe

- 类别：armor
- 标识：amethyst robe
- 来源：wiki/Amethyst_Robe

The Amethyst Robe is an Armor item and part of the Wizard set, being wearable in the shirt slot. It is the weakest Gem Robe.

Wearing the Wizard Hat alongside the Amethyst Robe provides a set bonus of +10% magic critical strike chance, while wearing a Magic Hat alongside it provides a set bonus of +60 Mana. Any leg-slot equipment can be worn with these set bonuses remaining intact.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
This item is the armor analog to the Amethyst Staff.

Trivia
The Amethyst Robe is one of the three items which provide bonuses when equipped in an armor slot but have defense value of zero. The others are Wood Greaves (provides 1 point of defense if full set is equipped) and Djinn's Curse (provides immunity to fall damage).

History

---

# 文档：Ancient Cobalt armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：ancient cobalt armor
- 来源：wiki/Ancient_Cobalt_armor

Ancient Cobalt armor is an armor set obtained from Hornets and Man Eaters in the Underground Jungle. Each piece is dropped individually with a  chance.

The Ancient Cobalt armor design is based on a version of Cobalt armor that appeared before the 1.0.3 update, when it was replaced by Jungle armor. The old design was later re-introduced, in 1.2, as Ancient Cobalt armor. It currently shares identical stats with Jungle armor, but differs in appearance. Furthermore, Jungle armor and Ancient Cobalt armor pieces can be mixed and matched while still providing their set bonus.

With a full set equipped, the player leaves an after-image effect when in motion, similar to Shadow armor, Necro armor, and the current version of Cobalt armor.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Despite its name, Ancient Cobalt armor is not functionally related to the current Cobalt armor set, nor with Cobalt Ore.

History

---

# 文档：Ancient Gold Helmet

- 类别：armor
- 标识：ancient gold helmet
- 来源：wiki/Ancient_Gold_Helmet

Gold armor is the fourth armor set in the game.

It takes a total of  75 /  90 Gold Bars to craft a full suit, or  300 /  360 Gold Ores.

It consists of a Gold Helmet, Gold Chainmail and Gold Greaves.

The Ancient Gold Helmet is a rare drop from Skeletons in the Underground, and is functionally identical to the Gold Helmet. The two helmets are interchangeable, with no bonus loss.

A full set grants a basic 13 defense plus a set bonus of 3 defense, which equals a total of 16 defense.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
The Ancient Gold Helmet has the same sprite that the Gold Helmet had before 1.0.3, thus the term "Ancient".
The Ancient Gold Helmet is available even if a world generated with Platinum Ore. However, obtaining a Gold armor set to complete its set bonus is much harder in this case.

Tips
Due to its high ore requirement and relatively low defense, it is advisable to skip Gold armor. The next tier of armor is usually available before the player can get enough Gold to craft the armor set.

Trivia
Prior to 1.3, if the Frost Breastplate and Leggings were paired with a Gold Helmet, and all pieces were dyed with Blue Flame and Black Dye, it would cause an afterimage and pulsing aura, akin to Hallowed armor. This was the armor Red used to wear before his specific set was added.

History

---

# 文档：Ancient Hallowed armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：ancient hallowed armor
- 来源：wiki/Ancient_Hallowed_armor

Ancient Hallowed armor is a Hardmode armor set. It has ranged, melee, summoner, and magic set versions, depending on the equipped headpiece. Crafting a full set with one headpiece requires 54 Hallowed Bars (dropped by the mechanical bosses), or 90 Hallowed Bars for the full set including all four headpieces. Wearing a full set gives off a faint pulsing aura around the player and displays an after-image effect while in motion. To acquire the necessary Hallowed Bars for a full set with one helmet, a player will need to kill a mechanical boss 2-4 times, and 3-4 times for all four helmets.

It currently shares identical stats with Hallowed armor, but differs in appearance. Furthermore, Ancient Hallowed and Hallowed armor pieces can be mixed and matched while still providing their set bonus.

Set
Helmet variants
Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Tips
Since the armor is crafted at an altar, crafting this set will allow entirely skipping a Hardmode anvil. However, creating the anvil eventually is still recommended due to the vast majority of useful Hardmode items requiring it.

Trivia
The sprite of this armor set is the old sprite of Hallowed armor (pre 1.4).
Unlike other "Ancient" items, the Ancient Hallowed Hood was not a sprite that existed prior in the game as there was no Hallowed summoner headgear prior to 1.4.0.1.
The Ancient Hallowed Hood is a completely new item with the style of the old Hallowed armor.
This armor set is the only Ancient armor that is crafted instead of being dropped by enemies.
It is also the only Ancient armor set that corresponds to a Hardmode set of armor.
The Ancient Hallowed armor pieces are the only Hardmode items crafted at an Altar.

History

---

# 文档：Ancient Iron Helmet

- 类别：armor
- 标识：ancient iron helmet
- 来源：wiki/Ancient_Iron_Helmet

Iron armor is the third-lowest tier of metal armor in the game, being Lead armor's weaker counterpart. It takes a total of  60 /  75 Iron Bars to craft a full suit, this translates to  180 /  225 Iron Ore per player to craft all the necessary bars for this armor.

The set consists of an Iron Helmet, Iron Chainmail and Iron Greaves.

The Ancient Iron Helmet is a rare drop from Skeletons in the Underground, and is functionally identical to the Iron Helmet. The two helmets are interchangeable, with no bonus loss.

A full set grants a basic 7 defense plus a set bonus of 2 defense, which equals a total of 9 defense.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Trivia
The Ancient Iron Helmet resembles the Bucket while equipped.
The Ancient Iron Helmet has the same sprite that the Iron Armor Helmet had prior to the 1.0.3 update, thus the term "Ancient".

History

---

# 文档：Ancient Shadow armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：ancient shadow armor
- 来源：wiki/Ancient_Shadow_armor

Ancient Shadow armor is an armor set that reproduces the pre-1.0.3 Shadow armor design, and is otherwise identical in all ways to the current Shadow armor set. Each piece is interchangeable with the equivalent Shadow armor piece, while still granting the same set bonus.

When the full set is equipped, the player will emit purple bubbles, and create a semi-transparent after-image effect behind themselves when moving. This effect appears to be clones of the player. These clones do not appear to have bodies inside of the armor, as there is no face prevalent.

Each piece has a  chance of being dropped by an Eater of Souls. Each piece can also be obtained by transmutating its non-ancient counterpart in Shimmer.


Set
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
If Ancient Shadow armor is equipped in social slots, the visual after-image effect will still appear, even though you will not get the set bonus.
If you mix and match the Ancient Shadow armor and the Shadow armor, you will still get the set bonus. However, you will not get the visual after-image effect that comes with either set.

Tips
If you have a decent amount of gear with a new player, you can farm Eater of Souls and skip to Shadow armor very early on.
The Corrupt Statue allows this armor to be obtained even in Crimson worlds.
Despite its very low drop chance, it's actually quite easy to farm. Simply make two sloped sides leading onto lava. The Eater of Souls' AI will simply fly at the slopes and be guided onto the lava, allowing the player to farm mostly AFK. It's wise to build it in the air to make sure Devourers don't disturb the farming process.
It's a surprisingly good armor choice for summoners on the  – it is obtainable prior to defeating a boss and can be effortlessly farmed, and provides multiple benefits for summoners: it has high defense combined with increased melee speed (which is very useful for Whips) and movement speed, making it a great armor set prior to defeating a boss.

History

---

# 文档：Angler armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：angler armor
- 来源：wiki/Angler_armor

Angler armor is an armor set consisting of an Angler Hat, Angler Vest, and Angler Pants. Each piece of the set provides a 5% increase to Fishing Power and can be received as a reward for completing Fishing quests given by the Angler NPC. The hat is given at 10 quests completed, the vest piece at 15, and the pants at 20. Although its effects on fishing are useful throughout the game, it only rewards a total of 4 defense.

Wearing the full set decreases enemy spawn rate.

Set
Tips
As the low defense of this armor can put Hardmode players at a disadvantage, it is recommended to take measures when fishing (e.g. a box of blocks with a floor of platforms, Peace Candles, Traps, Minions) in order to prevent enemies from getting too close and/or quickly killing the player.
Fishing near NPCs also help as enemy spawn rates drastically decrease when near a town.

See also
Angler Earring
Fishing Pole

History

---

# 文档：Apprentice armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：apprentice armor
- 来源：wiki/Apprentice_armor

The Apprentice armor is a Hardmode magic and summoning armor set purchased from the Tavernkeep for  in total, which consists of the Apprentice's Hat, Apprentice's Robe and Apprentice's Trousers.

The total bonus to max number of sentries is +2, allowing 3 to be summoned at once. The Dark Artist armor can be considered a direct upgrade of the Apprentice armor.

A full set of Apprentice armor will provide the following benefits:

+32 defense
+20% /  +10% magic damage
+30% minion damage
+2 sentry slots
+20% magic critical strike chance
-10% mana usage
+20% movement speed

The set bonus of the Apprentice armor gives a 40% increase to Flameburst sentries' projectile velocity, a 50% increase to target range, and a 90% increase to field of view.

Set
Notes
The Apprentice's Trousers provide an unlisted 20% increase to magic critical strike chance.

Tips
The Apprentice armor set may seem weaker than other post-Mechanical boss magic armor (such as Hallowed armor or Chlorophyte armor), with weaker boosts to magic damage and magic critical strike chance. However, its boosts to the Flameburst sentry summons make it ideal for setting up boss arenas. Three boosted Flameburst towers with the increased damage from the Apprentice armor can provide significant damage against Skeletron Prime, the Destroyer, or Golem.
Alternatively, this set allows the summoning of three Queen Spiders from the Queen Spider Staff with strongly boosted damage.

See also
Apprentice's Scarf
Squire armor
Huntress armor
Monk armor

History

---

# 文档：Ash Wood armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：ash wood armor
- 来源：wiki/Ash_Wood_armor

Ash Wood armor is an armor set that consists of the Ash Wood Helmet, the Ash Wood Breastplate and the Ash Wood Greaves. It is the most powerful Pre-Hardmode wooden set, however at the point it is obtainable the player likely already has better armor, though it may still be considered due to its set bonus

A full set of Ash Wood armor provides the following benefits:
+7 defense
Reduces lava contact damage by 50% and the duration of the inflicted On Fire! debuff by 3.5 seconds.

Crafting a full set requires a total of 75 Ash Wood.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
In Don't dig up and Get Fixed Boi seeds, the Ash Wood armor receives the following changes:
Each set piece only has 1 defense (total of 3)
The armor's set bonus reduces lava damage by 64% (reduced from 200 to 72), instead of only 50%.

# 文档：Bee armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：bee armor
- 来源：wiki/Bee_armor

Bee armor is an armor set which offers minion boosts. It is one of 3 minion-boosting armors available prior to Hardmode.

A full set of Bee armor provides the following benefits:
+13 defense
+23% minion damage
+2 minion slots

Crafting a full set requires a total of 30 Bee Wax. Bee Wax is always dropped by Queen Bee in stacks of 16-29; crafting the entire set therefore requires defeating Queen Bee twice.

# 文档：Beetle armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：beetle armor
- 来源：wiki/Beetle_armor

Beetle Armor is a Hardmode, post-Golem armor set that provides melee and defensive bonuses. Though it is crafted from Turtle armor, it trades away the Thorns effect in exchange for stronger offensive or defensive bonuses (depending on which chest piece is worn). It is the final melee armor set available on the . On the , it is the penultimate melee only armor set followed by Solar Flare armor.

Beetle armor has two variants for its chest piece; Beetle Scale Mail and Beetle Shell. The Beetle Scale Mail enhances damage output and is slightly more agile, whereas the Beetle Shell provides more defense.

Beetle armor provides the following bonuses when fully equipped:

With
+8% melee critical hit chance
+14% melee damage
+12% melee speed
+12% movement speed
Significantly higher chance that enemies will target the wearer
Beetle Might offensive buff: increases melee speed and damage by increments of 10% when dealing melee damage to enemies. The boost can be raised up to 30%, and the rate at which it increases depends on total damage dealt per second. Dealing less than roughly 180 damage per second will not grant Beetle Might at all. The buff depletes at an accelerating rate as long as the wearer does not deal damage, meaning that the buff will reset very quickly outside of combat.

With
+5% melee critical hit chance
+11% melee damage
+6% melee speed
+6% movement speed
Significantly higher chance that enemies will target the wearer
Beetle Endurance defensive buff: When the player avoids taking damage, this buff reduces damage taken on the next hit by 15%, with further boosts to 30% and 45% if the player continues to avoid damage for a longer duration. The buff gets first applied after 3 seconds from taking damage, and will then increase after another 3 seconds. When the player takes damage thereafter, each hit lowers the boost applied to the next hit by one step. This buff features a visual effect showing one to three "beetles" flying around the player, signifying the 15%, 30%, or 45% damage reduction boost currently in effect.

Crafting the full set with one chest piece requires 18 Beetle Husks and a full set of Turtle armor (54 Chlorophyte Bars (or 324 pieces of Chlorophyte Ore), and 3 Turtle Shells). Crafting all four pieces from scratch requires 26 Beetle Husks, 78 Chlorophyte Bars (or 468 pieces of Chlorophyte Ore), and 4 Turtle Shells.

Set
Chest variants
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
The Beetle Shell armor has the worst damage boosts out of all hardmode melee armors.
When upgrading to Beetle Armor from Turtle Armor, the stats and defense increase greatly, but the thorns effect is lost. Keep this in mind when preparing for activities requiring a thorns effect.
While the Beetle Shell is equipped without any items occupying the social slot, Wings will not appear unless the player is in mid-air, similar to the Turtle Armor, Leaf Blower, and Heat Ray.
The Endurance Potion stacks with the Beetle Endurance buff.

Beetle armor at its 45% state, Worm Scarf (17%) and Endurance Potion (10%) will reduce damage by nearly 60%.

Frozen Turtle Shell (25%) will further increase it to almost 74%.

Tips
If the player changes their mind, the chest piece can be shimmered and crafted into the alternative.
It is possible to reach a defense of over 100 without buffs by wearing the full set with the Shell, a Frozen Shield or a Hero's Shield, and all accessories reforged to Warding. However, bear in mind that even the highest possible defense cannot efficiently defend against enemies alone.

Trivia
With the full Beetle Endurance buff and a Lifeforce Potion (600 HP) and/or Endurance Potion, it is possible to survive a hit from the Dungeon Guardian.
Beetle Armor is the only armor set to have multiple chest piece variants as opposed to helmet variants.
provides more defense than either of the chest variants. However, the Beetle Shell provides 45% max damage reduction as opposed to the Solar Flare shield's maximum 30% reduction, and regenerates shields 2 seconds faster.
Excluding the Gi, the Beetle Shell and Beetle Scale mail is the only chest plate to provide a Movement speed buff.

History

---

# 文档：Boots of Ostara

- 类别：armor
- 标识：boots of ostara
- 来源：wiki/Boots_of_Ostara

The Boots of Ostara are a drop from the boss Lepus. They provide 3 defense to the player and create grass on dirt when worn. The Boots of Ostara will also enable the wearer to perform "bunny hops" where each successive jump in a series will increase in height until a maximum height of 16 blocks is reached. The Boots also give a green particle effect when jumping.

Tips
It is a good replacement to the Shiny Red Balloon, as it is fairly easy to get.
It is very useful when going underground early in the game.
Its sell price is pretty high if you decide to farm Lepus.

Trivia
The "bunny hops" are similar to Mario's jump patterns in more recent Super Mario Bros games.
, or Ēostre, is the pre-Christian name for Easter and the Germanic pagan . Unlike Easter it carries no Christian religious meaning, instead being used as a name for the Spring  and, allegorically, the rebirth of nature.
If worn with the Silver Helmet and Silver Chainmail, they still give the usual 3 defense set bonus. When placed on a Mannequin, it will become Silver Greaves.

History

---

# 文档：Boreal Wood armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：boreal wood armor
- 来源：wiki/Boreal_Wood_armor

Boreal Wood armor is an armor set consisting of the Boreal Wood Helmet, Boreal Wood Breastplate, and Boreal Wood Greaves.

It is made solely out of Boreal Wood obtained from chopping Trees native to the Snow Biome. Like the Wood armor set, it is fairly weak in terms of defense, making it only useful very early on in the game.

Wearing the full set grants a basic 3 defense plus a set bonus of 1 defense, providing a total of 4 defense.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Stats-wise, Palm Wood armor and Rich Mahogany armor are exact copies of this armor.

Trivia
The helmet of the armor set has horns similar to the Viking Helmet.

# 文档：Buckets

- 类别：armor
- 标识：buckets
- 来源：wiki/Buckets

Buckets are tools used to carry water, lava, or honey, allowing them to be removed or placed. With an Empty Bucket, pressing the  button on any sufficient body of liquid collects it, which replaces the Empty Bucket with the respective Water Bucket, Lava Bucket, or Honey Bucket. Pressing the  button again will deposit the liquid at the location of the cursor, wherein the filled bucket is again replaced with an Empty Bucket. Each bucket holds one tile of liquid.

The , , Bottomless Honey Bucket and Bottomless Shimmer Bucket act as infinite water, lava, honey and shimmer placing buckets respectively. They also add/remove liquids at a slightly faster speed than their normal bucket counterparts (especially when moving the cursor around while filling/draining). Similarly, the  and  and Honey Absorbant Sponge are essentially "Bottomless" Empty Buckets that accept only water, lava, or honey, respectively, but which cannot place the liquids. The Ultra Absorbant Sponge will absorb any liquid.

Buckets
Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Notes
When using Empty Buckets on a liquid, the game checks a 3×3-tile square around that tile for more liquid of the same type. If it finds at least half a tile of liquid in that area, each Empty Bucket collects up to a full tile of that liquid. Regardless of whether it collected less than a full tile, an Empty Bucket is always filled completely and is able to pour out a full tile.
An Empty Bucket can also be placed in the armor's helmet slot, where it provides +1 defense.
Putting an Empty Bucket in both the vanity slot and the armor slot will stack the buckets on top of each other:
Only two other items can stack this way,  and the .
Because the Empty Bucket has no hue, dyes mostly have an unnoticeable effect on its appearance.

Tips
Buckets may be utilised when duplicating liquids. When they are placed in a location that causes the liquid to begin flowing, this separates the liquid tile into multiple portions. This combines with the fact that each bucket collected is always full and will place a whole tile of liquid, even when a half tile portion is removed from the world. In other words, a half bucketful portion can be collected which will still place a whole tile and restart the cycle, while the remaining portion accumulates somewhere else. Overall because of this "rounding up" effect more liquid is now present than before. In practice this can be accomplished by either repeatedly using a full bucket on a single tile to place and remove the liquid, or instead splitting the fluid into two separate half tiles and collecting one of them.
This is ideally done with one bucket in the inventory, which will switch states immediately as it is used. With a stack of buckets present, the player will have to reselect the appropriate stack of items after each "use" action. Therefore it is best to store extra buckets during the actual duplication process and retrieve them afterwards when all of them can be filled at once.
The Empty Bucket "helmet" is of little practical use. While it is much cheaper than a proper Iron Helmet, it is also no more effective an a Wood Helmet, which is far more accessible than the Empty Bucket.
While falling, a Water or Honey Bucket can be used as an emergency method to prevent potentially fatal fall damage, although this may require some practice to perform effectively. Water Walking Boots or any of their upgrades prove useful. With protection against lava, e.g. via an Obsidian Skin Potion or Lava Charm (or any of its upgrades), a Lava Bucket can also be used.

History

# 文档：Chlorophyte armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：chlorophyte armor
- 来源：wiki/Chlorophyte_armor

and attacking a Skeleton.]]

Chlorophyte armor is a Hardmode armor set available after defeating all three mechanical bosses. It can be considered a partial upgrade of Hallowed armor, granting more defense and being geared more towards high ranged and magic damage output.

Equipping the full set causes a Leaf Crystal to permanently appear over the player's head, automatically attacking enemies via a ranged projectile that costs no mana or ammunition, and emitting a small green light. The projectile is capped at 100 damage.

The Leaf Crystal is capable of firing two separate simultaneous volleys of projectiles. Each volley fires every  0.67 /  0.83 seconds. It is possible for both volleys to target the same enemy.
One volley targets any enemies in range on its own. It requires a clear line-of-sight, but can still pierce blocks if it misses or if the player hides behind cover right before the volley is shot.
A second volley activates when an enemy takes damage from other sources first (e.g. from a player attack, or from lava, a minion, a trap, etc). This volley will target enemies through blocks, and the projectile will travel through blocks to reach the target.

A full set of Chlorophyte armor requires 54 Chlorophyte Bars, or  270 /  324 Chlorophyte Ore. Crafting the full set with every helmet type requires 78 bars ( 390 /  468 ore).

Set
Helmet variants
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
The Leaf Crystal has the following unique properties:
When firing, it fires a medium/unlimited range beam that cant miss the target.
In PvP, only the second volley will target players (i.e., they need to take damage from another source first for the crystal to target them).
It emits a small amount of green light.
It does not benefit from damage-increasing accessories and cannot deal critical hits.
If a minion is attacking an enemy behind blocks, it may also shoot through the blocks to hit the same target.
It cannot target critters directly but can still kill them if it managed to fire at them.
It is affected by neither  nor the pet slot dye, thus it cannot be dyed, unlike the .
If a full set of Chlorophyte armor is equipped, it will display a dark pulsating effect. This only occurs if no vanity is covering it or it is equipped as vanity itself.
Chlorophyte armor can still be useful not just for farms but as an armor set up to pre- Moon Lord, Due to pretty good defense and offense bonuses.
Pairing Chlorophyte armor (mask) with the Chlorophyte claymore can deal some very strong melee damage
In version 1.4.1 if you grapple onto a ceiling and the crystal leaf is above the ceiling it can still deal damage, and it can be used for farming some enemies whilst inactive, you may want to use the Ballista rod (or it’s upgrades) to protect yourself against Wraiths.

Tips
If the player changes their mind, the helmet piece can be shimmered and crafted into an alternative.
The Leaf Crystal can be very useful for farming certain enemies while inactive, for example in search of rare drops and Biome Keys.
Despite Chlrophyte armor's upsides, it may be advantageous to avoid upgrading from whatever previous armor you were using, for a multitude of reasons:
Hallowed armor proves to be a superior option in case you prefer more defensive playstyles, as Chlorophyte armor's set bonus is more offensive in comparison. The Hallowed armor enacts the Holy Protection buff which grants the player immunity to the next attack from enemies whereas Chlorophyte's set bonus acts as a sentry tower to fight for them.
In case you haven't went for a Hallowed set, it might be advantageous to stick to Adamantite armor if you are a mage, and Frost armor if you are a ranger or melee. The former's mana cost reduction bonus might prove more useful than the leaf crystal's extra damage depending on your weapon of choice, meanwhile other stat bonuses of the sets are almost the same. As for the other two classes, Frost armor's frostburn can prove to be a more reliable source of extra damage than the crystal's shots, which can also be spread across multiple enemies. The Frost set also offers higher increase to damage, and provides a bonus to melee attack speed.
Melee players have an option to go straight for Turtle armor, which serves as a defensive alternative to more offensive oriented Chlorophyte set.
On versions before 1.2.0.3, for melee players Chlorophyte armor can substitute Turtle armor for the Plantera fight better because of the mask giving 28 defense instead of 20.
It has similar benefits to nebula armor, despite it gives more maximum mana.

Trivia
The Chlorophyte Mask used to have the highest total melee  bonuses of any head slot item, but as of , the Solar Flare Helmet gives a better DPS.
The old Dragon armor () and Chlorophyte armor sets offer the highest universal critical strike chance bonus of any armor set in the game.
Despite its set bonus being very similar to summoning weapons, there is no summoner helmet.

History

---

# 文档：Cobalt armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：cobalt armor
- 来源：wiki/Cobalt_armor

Cobalt armor is a Hardmode armor set crafted from Cobalt Bars and is the alternative to Palladium armor. Like many Hardmode ore-based armors, Cobalt armor has three different headpiece options, allowing players to focus on magical, melee, or ranged combat respectively, each changing the bonus applied for wearing a full set. A full set also grants a total of  +3% critical hit chance and +10% movement speed /  +3% damage, +5% critical hit chance and +10% movement speed, from its individual pieces.

Wearing a full set causes the player to display an after-image effect while in motion, similar to the Ninja armor, Shadow armor, and Necro armor.

Crafting a full set with a single headpiece requires 45 Cobalt Bars (135 Cobalt Ore). Crafting all five pieces requires a total of 65 Cobalt Bars (195 Cobalt Ore).

This armor is not the same as the Ancient Cobalt armor set dropped by Jungle enemies; the latter is effectively reskinned Jungle armor, using the former sprites of this set.

Set
Helmet variants
Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Notes
The Cobalt melee set offers the second-best melee speed bonus in the game, only bested by the Hallowed melee set.

Tips
If the player changes their mind, the helmet piece can be shimmered and crafted into an alternative.
With the exception of the Cobalt Helmet, each piece of this set seems to offer less defense than the preceding armor tier, the Molten armor. This may be due to the Helmet being an upgrade to the melee-based Molten Armor; the Mask being upgrade to the ranged-based Necro armor; and the Hat being an upgrade to the magic-based Jungle armor.
Despite this, none of the different sets are actually quite upgrades on their pre-Hardmode "counterparts". The helmet and mask versions of the set have respectively higher melee speed and ranged crit chance which may not be preferable to the Molten and Necro armors' higher raw damage, and the hat is somewhat worse than the Jungle armor with 40 less mana and 2% more mana consumption in exchange for only 10% increased movement speed, and also unlike the other versions does not have any more armor or crit chance than its predecessor.

See also
Ancient Cobalt armor

History

---

Copper armor is one of the earliest, most easily obtainable armor sets in the game.

It takes a total of  48 /  60 Copper Bars to craft a full suit, or  144 /  180 Copper Ores.

It consists of a Copper Helmet, Copper Chainmail and Copper Greaves.

Set
Crafting

The fact that the helmet of the copper armor has what resembles a hair bun may be a reference to the Terracotta Warriors in China.
However, on the promotional image for the town slime shirt on 2022 august state of the game it is revealed to more closely resemble the Palladium Helmet

# 文档：Crimson armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：crimson armor
- 来源：wiki/Crimson_armor

Crimson armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Crimson Helmet, Crimson Scalemail, and Crimson Greaves. Equipping the full set grants a greatly increased health regeneration rate, represented by faint red pulses around the player: Each pulse represents one health point regenerated. These become more frequent the longer the player avoids damage, and even more frequent while standing still. Each piece also provides a +3% weapon damage boost, the entire set providing a total of +9% damage.

Crimson armor is the Crimson equivalent of Shadow armor and leaves a similar trail of red bubbles during motion when the full set is equipped. However, unlike Shadow armor, it does not have an interchangeable Ancient version.

Crafting the full set requires 60 Crimtane Bars and 45 Tissue Samples.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Trivia
While Crimson armor is named directly after its biome (Crimson), its Corruption counterpart is named Shadow armor, rather than "Corruption armor."

History

---

# 文档：Crystal Assassin armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：crystal assassin armor
- 来源：wiki/Crystal_Assassin_armor

Crystal Assassin armor is a Hardmode armor set which consists of the Crystal Assassin Hood, Crystal Assassin Shirt, and the Crystal Assassin Pants. The armor set grants non-class-specific bonuses. It is obtained from Queen Slime; she always drops one piece /  upon defeat.

A full set of Crystal Assassin armor provides the following benefits:
+36 defense
+15% damage
+15% critical strike chance
+20% movement speed
-10% mana cost
+10% ammo conservation
+10% melee speed
Ability to dash

Set
Tips
Due to its respectable defense and stat bonuses, this is a viable alternative to Hardmode ore sets, especially for those who do not wish to grind for resources.
It works well with the Nimbus Rod, Clinger Staff, and Queen Spider Staff since it boosts their damage output without sacrificing bonuses to melee or ranged weapons.
In Classic Mode, Crystal Assassin armor's dash is the only dash effect that is accessible before defeating Plantera.
In Expert Mode, the dash may be used as a replacement for the Shield of Cthulhu or Master Ninja Gear, freeing up an accessory slot. If this slot is used for an offensive accessory like an emblem, Crystal Assassin armor effectively provides higher offensive bonuses than Adamantite or Titanium armor, which cannot achieve the same level of offense without sacrificing the dash. However, the lack of maximum mana bonus might make this a less attractive choice for mages.
The dash provided by Crystal Assassin armor is often better than the Shield of Cthulhu's dash, since it covers a greater distance and will not be interrupted by contact with an enemy if a Cobalt Shield or its upgrades are equipped. There are many situations where accidentally bumping into an enemy can be extremely undesirable, notably when fighting Plantera or Skeletron Prime. However, it should be noted that this also makes dashes potentially riskier in other situations, as dashing into an enemy will no longer provide the player with invincibility frames.

Trivia
The curved horns featured on the helmet resemble the horns of some types of , commonly known as samurai helmets.
This is the last set of armor that is dropped directly by a certain boss or enemies. This trait is shared with the Ninja armor, Gladiator Armor, Ancient Cobalt armor, and Ancient Shadow armor.
It is also the only Hardmode armor with this trait. Frost armor was once made available through the Ice Golem, before Frost Cores were introduced.

See also
Ninja armor, a pre-Hardmode counterpart dropped by King Slime

History

---

# 文档：Dark Artist armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：dark artist armor
- 来源：wiki/Dark_Artist_armor

The Dark Artist armor is a Hardmode, magic-oriented armor set purchased from the Tavernkeep for  in total, after Golem has been defeated. The set consists of the Dark Artist's Hat, Dark Artist's Robe and Dark Artist's Leggings. Wearing the full set increases the attack range of Flameburst sentry summons by 50%, allowing them to attack up to 84 blocks away. It also increases projectile velocity by 40% and increases the field of view significantly.

A full set of Dark Artist armor will provide the following stats:

+42 defense
+25% magic damage
+60% minion damage
+25% magic critical strike chance
-15% mana cost
+20% movement speed bonus
+3 extra sentry slots

The Dark Artist armor set is an upgrade of the Apprentice armor set.

Set
Notes
The total bonus to max number of sentries is +3, allowing 4 to be summoned at once.
The Dark Artist's Leggings offer the highest increase to magic DPS for the leg slot.
The hat and robes give off a small amount of light.
Its male and female variants look identical.
If the player has the Brain of Confusion equipped along with Dark Artist set, it will have a different dodge effect, showing blue flames rather than brains.

Tips
This set has the same defense as Spectre armor with the mask option, increases magic damage by a larger percentage, and has almost double the increase in magic critical hit percentage. It does not share the Spectre set's set bonuses; however, the extra slots and bonuses to the Flameburst sentry summons allow it to place 4 powerful sentries at once. This is especially useful in arenas for boss fights, with the set bonus allowing the Flameburst sentries to attack from far away.
Also, the increase in minion damage makes both the Xeno Staff and Stardust Dragon Staff much more powerful. This can be capitalized on by using the Papyrus Scarab and Necromantic Scroll or Pygmy Necklace and Bewitching Table or Summoning Potion to more than quadruple the damage.
The Dark Artist set's emphasis on sentries and minions make it better suited to a cautious play style. Players that have difficulty attacking while kiting bosses with Spectre armor may find this easier.
The Dark Artist's Leggings give a 25% magical critical hit boost, making them excellent for hybrid sets.
The Dark Artist's Hat represents a direct upgrade to the Forbidden Mask for the head slot in hybrid sets, affording 1 more defense and two additional sentry slots.

See also
Valhalla Knight armor
Red Riding armor
Shinobi Infiltrator armor
History

---

Wearing the Wizard Hat with the Diamond Robe provides a set bonus of +10% magic critical strike chance, while wearing a Magic Hat provides a set bonus of +60 Mana. Any leg-slot equipment can be worn with these set bonuses intact.

# 文档：Dragon armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：dragon armor
- 来源：wiki/Dragon_armor

Dragon armor is a Hardmode melee armor set consisting of the Dragon Mask, the Dragon Breastplate and the Dragon Greaves. Each separate piece of Dragon armor has a  chance of being dropped by Ocram, or can otherwise be crafted.

A full set of Dragon armor provides the following benefits:
+60 defense
+20% melee damage
+25% critical strike chance
+38% melee speed
+35% movement speed

Additionally, if the full set is visible the player will have an afterimage, similar to Necro armor.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Cobalt and Mythril Ore is required to craft the set. If the player's world has Palladium/Orichalcum Ore instead of Cobalt/Mythril, they will either have to create a new world or fish for crates (creating a new world may be quicker than fishing for crates).
Despite the stats saying the critical hit rate are 15% for the mask and 10% for the breastplate, they are in fact 10% and 5% respectively. This means a full set only provides a total of 15% increase in critical hit rate rather than the listed 25%.

Tips
Dragon armor is one of the best melee armor sets in the game, however it can be outclassed by Beetle or Turtle armors in certain aspects, so this can be regarded as a situational set.
Though it is a very effective melee set, the Turtle armor provides an extra 5 defense points, is easier to obtain, and has the thorns set bonus, which returns all of the damage taken by melee attacks back to the attacker.
If the player is willing to forgo the set bonus, the Beetle Scale Mail offers 8% increased melee damage and critical strike chance, and 6% increased movement and melee speed (with a positive change in defense), though is harder to obtain.
The 35% increased speed can be used to make some late-game movement speed builds like this: Dragon armor, Swiftness potion, Lightning boots, Panic necklace (use the lightning boots to take little fall damage to keep buff active) Aglet, Anklet of the wind, Tabi for dashing, and put the “Quick” modifier on all accessories and you get 153% increased movement speed.

Trivia
Dragon armor can be considered an upgrade of the Shadow armor.
The Dragon Armor sprite was updated in 1.3, despite not being used in the game.

See also
, the ranged exclusive armor.
, the magic exclusive armor.

History

---

# 文档：Ebonwood armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：ebonwood armor
- 来源：wiki/Ebonwood_armor

Ebonwood armor is an armor set consisting of a Ebonwood Helmet, Ebonwood Breastplate, and Ebonwood Greaves.

It is crafted solely from Ebonwood harvested from Corrupt Trees. It acts as the Corruption counterpart to the Crimson's Shadewood armor, which shares same stats. Along with its counterpart, it is the most powerful Pre-Hardmode wooden set, though it still pales in comparison to most other armor sets.

Wearing a full set grants a set bonus of 1 additional defense, providing a total of 5 defense.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
It may be a good idea to craft the Cactus armor instead, since it has the same stats and only requires entering the Desert; characters that could make use of Ebonwood armor are likely not ready to enter the Corruption.
While Ebonwood is initially obtainable in Corruption worlds only, one can transfer an artificial Corruption biome or the armor itself into Crimson worlds. It is to be noted, however, that this strategy is impractical and inefficient, and it is easier to simply use Cactus armor.

Trivia
The helmet appears to have spikes, which might be a reference to the Eater of Worlds' spikes, or devil horns.

History

---

# 文档：Emerald Robe

- 类别：armor
- 标识：emerald robe
- 来源：wiki/Emerald_Robe

The Emerald Robe is an Armor item and part of the Wizard set, being wearable in the shirt slot. It is the third strongest Gem Robe.

Wearing the Wizard Hat alongside the Emerald Robe provides a set bonus of +10% magic critical strike chance while wearing a Magic Hat alongside it provides a set bonus of +60 Mana. Any leg-slot equipment can be worn with these set bonuses intact.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
This item is the armor analog to the Emerald Staff.

# 文档：Empty Bucket

- 类别：armor
- 标识：empty bucket
- 来源：wiki/Empty_Bucket

Buckets are tools used to carry water, lava, or honey, allowing them to be removed or placed. With an Empty Bucket, pressing the  button on any sufficient body of liquid collects it, which replaces the Empty Bucket with the respective Water Bucket, Lava Bucket, or Honey Bucket. Pressing the  button again will deposit the liquid at the location of the cursor, wherein the filled bucket is again replaced with an Empty Bucket. Each bucket holds one tile of liquid.

The , , Bottomless Honey Bucket and Bottomless Shimmer Bucket act as infinite water, lava, honey and shimmer placing buckets respectively. They also add/remove liquids at a slightly faster speed than their normal bucket counterparts (especially when moving the cursor around while filling/draining). Similarly, the  and  and Honey Absorbant Sponge are essentially "Bottomless" Empty Buckets that accept only water, lava, or honey, respectively, but which cannot place the liquids. The Ultra Absorbant Sponge will absorb any liquid.

Buckets
Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Notes
When using Empty Buckets on a liquid, the game checks a 3×3-tile square around that tile for more liquid of the same type. If it finds at least half a tile of liquid in that area, each Empty Bucket collects up to a full tile of that liquid. Regardless of whether it collected less than a full tile, an Empty Bucket is always filled completely and is able to pour out a full tile.
An Empty Bucket can also be placed in the armor's helmet slot, where it provides +1 defense.
Putting an Empty Bucket in both the vanity slot and the armor slot will stack the buckets on top of each other:
Only two other items can stack this way,  and the .
Because the Empty Bucket has no hue, dyes mostly have an unnoticeable effect on its appearance.

Tips
Buckets may be utilised when duplicating liquids. When they are placed in a location that causes the liquid to begin flowing, this separates the liquid tile into multiple portions. This combines with the fact that each bucket collected is always full and will place a whole tile of liquid, even when a half tile portion is removed from the world. In other words, a half bucketful portion can be collected which will still place a whole tile and restart the cycle, while the remaining portion accumulates somewhere else. Overall because of this "rounding up" effect more liquid is now present than before. In practice this can be accomplished by either repeatedly using a full bucket on a single tile to place and remove the liquid, or instead splitting the fluid into two separate half tiles and collecting one of them.
This is ideally done with one bucket in the inventory, which will switch states immediately as it is used. With a stack of buckets present, the player will have to reselect the appropriate stack of items after each "use" action. Therefore it is best to store extra buckets during the actual duplication process and retrieve them afterwards when all of them can be filled at once.
The Empty Bucket "helmet" is of little practical use. While it is much cheaper than a proper Iron Helmet, it is also no more effective an a Wood Helmet, which is far more accessible than the Empty Bucket.
While falling, a Water or Honey Bucket can be used as an emergency method to prevent potentially fatal fall damage, although this may require some practice to perform effectively. Water Walking Boots or any of their upgrades prove useful. With protection against lava, e.g. via an Obsidian Skin Potion or Lava Charm (or any of its upgrades), a Lava Bucket can also be used.

History

# 文档：Forbidden armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：forbidden armor
- 来源：wiki/Forbidden_armor

Forbidden armor is a Hardmode armor set consisting of the Forbidden Mask, Forbidden Robes, and the Forbidden Treads. Crafting the full set requires 3 Forbidden Fragments and 46 Titanium Bars or Adamantite Bars.

A full set of Forbidden armor will provide the following stats:

+26 defense
+25% /  +15% magic and summon damage
+80 maximum mana
+2 minion slots

While wearing the full set, a golden, spiky, rune-like object, known as the Forbidden Sign, floats behind the player in a very similar fashion to the Stardust Guardian from the Stardust armor set. Double tapping  or  (depending on the "Activate Set Bonuses" setting) will send a beam of dust particles that summon a sand tornado at the nearest block surface beneath the cursor position at the cost of 20 mana. The beam itself does not deal any damage, but the tornado rapidly hits any enemy that comes in direct contact with it for 20 base damage. The Forbidden Sign also casts a faint yellow light around the player, similar to the Mining Helmet.

The dust particles cannot pass through solid objects; if there is a solid object between the character and the cursor, the beam will hit the solid object, stop there, and the sand tornado will not be summoned. Only one sand tornado can be summoned at a time, and when a new tornado is summoned, the old one disappears after roughly 1 second. A space of at least 3×12 tiles is required for the tornado to be summoned. If no enemies come in contact with the tornado, it disappears after 15 seconds.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
While the damage of the sand tornado summoned by the Forbidden Sign is affected by both increases to magic and minion damage, the tornado's critical strike chance is based entirely on the player's magic critical strike chance.
Items that grant a bonus to all damage types (like the Celestial Shell or Avenger Emblem) will effectively contribute their value twice.
Since the full Forbidden armor set is equipped, which grants a combined bonus of  30% /  50% damage, the tornado's base damage is always already increased to  26 /  30.
The total bonuses of the Forbidden armor are composed of the following on the :
15% magic and minion damage each from the Forbidden Mask.
+40 maximum mana, 10% minion damage, and +1 minion capacity from the Forbidden Robes.
+40 maximum mana, 10% magic damage, and +1 minion capacity from the Forbidden Treads.
The total bonuses are composed of the following on the :
15% magic and minion damage each from the Forbidden Mask.
+80 maximum mana from the Forbidden Robes.
+2 minion capacity from the Forbidden Treads.
The sand tornado can be summoned even when afflicted with the Stoned debuff.
Dyeing the Forbidden Robes will change the color of the Forbidden Sign, even if a vanity chest piece is in use.

Tips
In terms of stats, this set is less powerful than Mythril armor with the mage headpiece, the Mythril Hood. Although, with efficient use of the sand tornado and the extra minion slots, one could get higher  than with Adamantite armor.
The set bonus can be very useful for fighting The Destroyer and other worm enemies, as the sand tornado can very rapidly hit multiple segments.
If only summoning bonuses are needed, it may be a better idea to simply go for Spider armor instead. Spider armor can be obtained directly after Hardmode begins and offers superior summoning bonuses.
Though it is weaker than Spider armor ( and Obsidian armor) for pure summoners, its mixed nature is useful for summoners playing on harder difficulties, especially in , as magic weapons allow for much safer strategies when fighting mechanical bosses.

Trivia
The Forbidden armor is the Desert counterpart to the Snow biome's Frost armor, as they are both obtained similarly. However, Frost armor buffs melee and ranged weapons instead of magic and summon weapons.
The set resembles the Egyptian guide and judge-god of the Dead , who is known for being depicted as a man with a canine head associated with the souls of the dead, the afterlife, guidance, embalming, necropoles, and protection.
Unlike other minions and sentries, the Forbidden Sign is not a projectile. It uses the  texture in the game files.
Equipping the Safeman's Sunny Day in the vanity helmet slot will cause the Sun to sit within the ring of the Forbidden Sign, moving together.

History
References

---

# 文档：Fossil armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：fossil armor
- 来源：wiki/Fossil_armor

Fossil armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Fossil Helmet, Fossil Plate, and Fossil Greaves.

On the , the Helmet and Greaves increase ranged critical strike chance by 4% per each, with the Plate increasing ranged damage by 5%, and wearing the full set grants a 20% chance not to consume ammo.

On the , Fossil armor instead greatly increases the velocity, damage, and critical strike chance of throwing weapons, and wearing the full set grants a 50% chance to not consume thrown items (not including ).

The Fossil armor set is made using  60 /  75 Sturdy Fossils.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Tips
The ammo conservation bonus makes Fossil armor great for using the Minishark as well as other early-game guns such as the Flintlock Pistol or The Undertaker, and also bows.
The chance not to consume thrown items makes Fossil armor great for using Bone Javelins, Bone Throwing Knives, and other throwing weapons.

Trivia
In the early-access release of Terraria 1.3, the armor's set bonus was 75% chance to not consume thrown items. It was reduced to 50% before the public release.
This is the only pre-Hardmode armor set in which the helmet sells for less than the rest of the pieces.
There seems to be an amber on the front of the helmet and in the middle of the chest plate, although no amber is used in the crafting recipe. This is likely because amber is considered the gemstone associated with the Desert biome.

History

# 文档：Frost armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：frost armor
- 来源：wiki/Frost_armor

The Frost armor is a Hardmode armor set that grants the ability to inflict Frostbite  /  Frostburn  on enemies with melee or ranged attacks when the whole set is worn, as well as causing all ranged and melee attacks to give off light. Frostbite is a variant of the Frostburn debuff that deals 25  instead of 8.

On the , the Ice Golem will drop a Frost Core, required to craft one piece of Frost armor. On the , Ice Golems will drop a random piece of Frost armor.

Visually, wearing the set causes a "snow trail" to be left behind the player as they walk, similar to running with Hermes Boots. Also, the sound when player takes damage is replaced with the  heard when breaking through Thin Ice.

The Desert's  is the counterpart to this armor set, buffing magic and summon abilities instead of melee and ranged.

It takes 46 Adamantite or Titanium Bars (Hallowed in the ) as well as 3 Frost Cores to craft a full set of Frost armor.

A full set of Frost armor will provide the following benefits:

Ranged and melee attacks cause  Frostbite /  Frostburn
+43 defense
+26% melee and ranged damage
+16% melee and ranged damage
+11% melee and ranged critical strike chance
+8% movement speed
+10% melee speed

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Tips
The Frost armor set can easily be farmed by creating a large one-sided trench with a roof over the top of it containing a one block wide hole. The Ice Golem will not be able to shoot the player because of its laser spawning off-center when being above it. Using Water Candles increases the spawn rate.
The Dungeon Guardian is not immune to the debuff, thus making Frost armor useful in obtaining the Bone Key.
The Frostbite / Frostburn debuff is particularly devastating when used against worm enemies because it affects each segment, thereby multiplying the damage dealt by the number of affected segments. Additionally, the light and particles from the debuffs allow the player to track them through tiles.
Unfortunately, The Destroyer is immune to the debuff, thus limiting the effectiveness of this strategy.
The debuff is also useful for most bosses as only three of them (The Destroyer, Duke Fishron and Mourning Wood) are immune.
When used with Rotten Eggs, Frost armor is a highly efficient way to kill friendly NPCs.
The Frostburn effect adds light to all melee and ranged attacks, including tools such as pickaxes and drills, and to ordinary ammunition, such as that used by the Endless Quiver and Endless Musket Pouch.
This makes the armor set very useful for exploring caves.
Selling Frost armor results in less profit than selling its crafting ingredients.
For melee users, pairing this armor with Magma Stone/Fire Gauntlet and a flask will inflict three different debuffs on a single hit, possibly more if the weapon itself inflicts another debuff. This proves particularly useful when used with the Shadowflame weapons.
For ranged users, this armor has slightly more defense than Hallowed armor with the Hallowed Helmet, however the damage bonuses are only equal to those from Mythril armor.
As a ranged user with Adamantite armor, it may better to change the Adamantite Breastplate for the Frost Breastplate, as this will result in 11% more critical strike chance and 4 more defense at the cost of 6% damage and its set bonus, which only reduces ammo consumption by 25%.

Trivia
Prior to 1.3, if the Frost Breastplate and Leggings were paired with a Gold Helmet, and all pieces were dyed with Blue Flame and Black Dye, it would cause an afterimage and pulsing aura, akin to Hallowed armor. This was the armor Red used to wear before his specific set was added.
The Frost Breastplate and Leggings look strikingly similar to the Molten Breastplate and Greaves.
The sound effect when a player is struck while wearing Frost armor takes priority over other items that also alter the sound effect of a player being struck, such as the Moon Charm at night.
Unlike other armors, the Frost Leggings increase melee attack speed instead of just melee speed; but in terms of gameplay, they both mean the same.

See also
, its Desert counterpart

History

---

# 文档：Gi

- 类别：armor
- 标识：gi
- 来源：wiki/Gi

The Gi is an armor item that can be equipped in the shirt slot. It provides 4 defense along with a 5% damage and critical strike chance boost to weapons. It also provides a 10% melee and movement speed increase. It is purchased from the Traveling Merchant for . The Gi will visually cover up what is equipped in the pants slot.

Tips
The Gi gives a wide variety of damage bonuses, making it more potent than many other pre-Hardmode armors. If the player values damage over defense, and does not want a set bonus, then they can use the Gi with a helmet and leggings from another set.
This, combined with the fact that it is obtainable very early, makes it a very powerful piece of armor.

Trivia
A Gi, or Keikogi (稽古着 or 稽古衣), is a traditional uniform favored by martial artists.

History

---

# 文档：Gladiator armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：gladiator armor
- 来源：wiki/Gladiator_armor

Gladiator armor is an armor set consisting of the Gladiator Helmet, Gladiator Breastplate, and Gladiator Leggings. Each piece is dropped by Hoplites, which spawn in Marble Caves, with a   /   chance to drop per piece.

It grants a total of   /  7 defense. On the , wearing the full set provides immunity to knockback.

Set
Tips
The decently high drop chance, useful set bonus, and defense on par with Gold armor could make this set a good alternative to Gold / Platinum armor in Pre-Hardmode. Hoplites deal high damage, but can also be stunlocked relatively easily, and in addition to the armor they can drop Javelins.
The Gladiator armor can be particularly useful for melee players due to its high defense and complete immunity to knockback.
For players who are particularly fond of the complete knockback immunity who commonly opt to equip a Cobalt Shield or Obsidian Shield, this armor set provides decent defense and can let them swap out their shield for another accessory while keeping the knockback immunity. However, it still cannot replace the burn-resistant property of the Obsidian Shield.
Due to the danger of Hoplites and the hard-to-find biome, this armor set is unlikely to be better than other armor available to the player by the time they have obtained it. However, it can still be valuable as vanity armor.

Trivia
Despite being named after the people who fought in the  in , the Gladiator armor visually resembles the armor of s (a type of soldier in ancient Greece) whom the enemies that drop it are named after as well.

History

---

# 文档：Gold armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：gold armor
- 来源：wiki/Gold_armor

Gold armor is the fourth armor set in the game.

It takes a total of  75 /  90 Gold Bars to craft a full suit, or  300 /  360 Gold Ores.

It consists of a Gold Helmet, Gold Chainmail and Gold Greaves.

The Ancient Gold Helmet is a rare drop from Skeletons in the Underground, and is functionally identical to the Gold Helmet. The two helmets are interchangeable, with no bonus loss.

A full set grants a basic 13 defense plus a set bonus of 3 defense, which equals a total of 16 defense.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
The Ancient Gold Helmet has the same sprite that the Gold Helmet had before 1.0.3, thus the term "Ancient".
The Ancient Gold Helmet is available even if a world generated with Platinum Ore. However, obtaining a Gold armor set to complete its set bonus is much harder in this case.

Tips
Due to its high ore requirement and relatively low defense, it is advisable to skip Gold armor. The next tier of armor is usually available before the player can get enough Gold to craft the armor set.

Trivia
Prior to 1.3, if the Frost Breastplate and Leggings were paired with a Gold Helmet, and all pieces were dyed with Blue Flame and Black Dye, it would cause an afterimage and pulsing aura, akin to Hallowed armor. This was the armor Red used to wear before his specific set was added.

History

---

# 文档：Hallowed armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：hallowed armor
- 来源：wiki/Hallowed_armor

due to the Holy Protection set bonus.]]

Hallowed armor is a Hardmode armor set. It has melee, ranged, magic, and summoner  set versions, depending on the equipped headpiece. Crafting a full set with one headpiece requires 54 Hallowed Bars (dropped by the mechanical bosses), or 90  / 78  Hallowed Bars for the full set including all four  / three  headpieces. Wearing a full set gives off a faint pulsing aura around the player and displays an after-image effect while in motion. To acquire the necessary Hallowed Bars for a full set with one helmet, the player will need to kill at least 2 mechanical bosses, and all four helmets will require 3–5 kills on the , or 2–4 kills on the  for all three helmets.

Additionally, wearing a full set while striking an enemy will grant the Holy Protection buff, which allows the player to dodge the next attack dealt by an enemy. It has a cooldown of 30 seconds, starting from when the last dodge was used. This set bonus is identical to the  buff from Titanium armor. Wearing a full set with the Hallowed Hood additionally increases minion capacity by 2 as a set bonus, making the total minion capacity of the hood-set 3, for a total minion count of 4.

It currently shares identical stats with , but differs in appearance. Furthermore, Ancient Hallowed and Hallowed armor pieces can be mixed and matched while still providing their set bonus.

Set
Helmet variants
Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Tips
If the player changes their mind, the helmet piece can be shimmered and crafted into an alternative.
Crafting any of the helmets and selling them is more profitable than selling the bars required to make them.
With the exception of certain exclusive armors, the Hallowed armor holds several records in Terraria:
The melee set provides the highest movement speed and melee speed bonuses, and the second-highest critical strike chance bonuses (after Chlorophyte armor). When using Turtle armor, the Hallowed Mask can easily replace the Turtle Helmet, trading away the situational Thorns effect for better stats across the board.
The magic set provides the highest maximum mana (alongside Titanium armor) and mana usage reduction bonuses, and the second-highest critical strike chance bonuses (after Orichalcum armor). The Hallowed Headgear provides the highest overall magic  increase of any head slot item.
The ranged set provides the highest ammo conservation (alongside Adamantite armor) and second-highest ranged damage bonus (after Titanium armor).

Trivia
on Discord).]]
The Hallowed Hood provides the lowest defense out of all Hardmode armor pieces, despite it being available after defeating a mechanical boss.
Additionally, the Hallowed summoner set provides lower minion damage than Spider armor, an early Hardmode summoner armor set, with the former providing only +17% minion damage, compared to the latter's +28% minion damage.
The Hallowed Mask's tooltip contains the only reference to melee haste in the game. This stat should be called melee speed, and newer versions correct this.
Sometimes the heads of the duplicates created by Holy Protection will not be visible.
Pre 1.4.0.1 Hallowed Plate Mail (female version) is surprisingly revealing. This is a possible reference to "Chainmail Bikini" trope commonly recurring in rpg games. Similar traits can be seen on Jungle armor and Necro armor.

History

---

# 文档：Huntress armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：huntress armor
- 来源：wiki/Huntress_armor

The Huntress armor is a Hardmode armor set purchased from the Tavernkeep for  in total after one of the Mechanical bosses has been defeated. It consists of the Huntress's Wig, Huntress's Jerkin and Huntress's Pants.

A full set of Huntress armor will provide the following stats:

+36 defense
+20% ranged damage
+30% minion damage
+10% ranged critical strike chance
+20% movement speed
+10% ammo conservation
+2 extra sentry slots.

Additionally, Explosive Traps' attacks will recharge faster (from every 1.83 seconds to every 1.23 seconds) and have a 1/2 chance to give 4-9 seconds of the Oiled debuff, making enemies take increased damage from On Fire!, Cursed Inferno, Frostburn or Shadowflame.

The Red Riding armor is a direct upgrade to this armor set.

Set
Tips
The Huntress armor is fairly strong when it is obtained, with stats similar to the ranged versions of Titanium armor or Adamantite armor. Its enhanced minion damage and extra sentry slots give it an extra source of damage.
However, the Explosive Trap sentry summons have a limited range, making them less effective against highly mobile bosses. They are, however, quite good against the The Destroyer and Golem, the least mobile of Hardmode bosses.

Trivia
The Huntress's Wig, Elf Hat, Goblorc Ears, and Vulkelf Ears are the only items that change the shape of the player's ears.
The Huntress armor has a striking resemblance to Link's outfit from the Legend of Zelda series

See also
Huntress's Buckler
Squire armor
Apprentice armor
Monk armor

# 文档：Iron armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：iron armor
- 来源：wiki/Iron_armor

Iron armor is the third-lowest tier of metal armor in the game, being Lead armor's weaker counterpart. It takes a total of  60 /  75 Iron Bars to craft a full suit, this translates to  180 /  225 Iron Ore per player to craft all the necessary bars for this armor.

The set consists of an Iron Helmet, Iron Chainmail and Iron Greaves.

The Ancient Iron Helmet is a rare drop from Skeletons in the Underground, and is functionally identical to the Iron Helmet. The two helmets are interchangeable, with no bonus loss.

A full set grants a basic 7 defense plus a set bonus of 2 defense, which equals a total of 9 defense.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Trivia
The Ancient Iron Helmet resembles the Bucket while equipped.
The Ancient Iron Helmet has the same sprite that the Iron Armor Helmet had prior to the 1.0.3 update, thus the term "Ancient".

# 文档：Jungle armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：jungle armor
- 来源：wiki/Jungle_armor

Jungle armor is a pre-Hardmode armor set consisting of the Jungle Shirt, the Jungle Hat and Jungle Pants. It is crafted from items found in the Underground Jungle.

A full set of Jungle armor grants the wearer 17  / 15  defense and boosts magic weapons, reducing mana usage by 16% and creating a grass particle effect when moving. Each individual piece increases maximum mana by 20–40 and magic critical strike chance or damage by  6% /  4%, for a total of 80  / 60  higher maximum mana and 12% magic critical strike chance and 6% magic damage  / 9% magic critical strike chance  when the whole set is equipped.

Jungle armor pieces are interchangeable with pieces from the Ancient Cobalt armor set, while still providing the same 16% reduced mana usage set bonus, but not the grass particle effect.

The total material cost of making a full set is:
10 () / 12 () Stingers
2 Vines
32 Jungle Spores

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Trivia
On the , the Dragon armor was formerly a resprite of the Jungle armor.
Despite being the cheapest to craft, the hat sells for the most Coins.

History

Lead armor is the 4th weakest metal armor, being the stronger alternative to Iron armor.

It takes a total of  60 /  75 Lead Bars to craft a full suit, or  180 /  225 Lead Ores.

It consists of a Lead Helmet, Lead Chainmail and Lead Greaves.

A full set grants a basic 8 defense plus a set bonus of 3 defense, which equals a total of 11 defense, 2 more than its iron counterpart.

# 文档：Meteor armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：meteor armor
- 来源：wiki/Meteor_armor

The Meteor armor is an armor set that consists of the Meteor Helmet, Meteor Suit, and Meteor Leggings. Equipping the full set reduces the mana cost of the Space Gun to 0. In the , the Gray Zapinator, Orange Zapinator and Laser Rifle are also affected by this change. Each piece of the armor worn increases magic damage by  9% /  7%. If the full set is visible, the player leaves a trail of flames as they move.

Crafting a full set requires a total of  45 /  60 Meteorite Bars (135 /  180 Meteorite ore).

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Tips
In versions prior to 1.4, it could be beneficial for players to aim to destroy a Shadow Orb (or Crimson Heart) as quickly as possible, as the Meteor armor and the Space Gun make for a strong combination in early pre-Hardmode. In 1.4, Meteorite won't spawn before player kills Eater of Worlds/Brain of Cthulhu, but Meteor armor with Space Gun still remain a very useful combination after that.
Meteorite Bars can be found in Gold Chests in the lower 250 tiles of the Cavern layer above the Underworld, allowing Meteor Armor to be obtained prior to defeating the Eater of Worlds / Brain of Cthulhu. They can also be obtained from Shadow Chests in the Underworld.
Oddly enough, the set bonus also affects the Gray Zapinator and Orange Zapinator, which makes this armor set useful into Hardmode. However, the lack of defense will be a great drawback.

Trivia
The fire particle effect can be altered by dyes. Using different dye in each slot of the armor causes the colors to blend.
Despite the Meteorite Helmet requiring the fewest bars to craft, it sells for the most, which is the case for many armor sets.

History

---

# 文档：Mining armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：mining armor
- 来源：wiki/Mining_armor

Mining armor is an armor set consisting of the Mining Helmet or , Mining Shirt, and Mining Pants. The helmets alone provide light when worn in the armor slot, but not when worn in the social slot. Each piece grants 1defense (aside from the Ultrabright Helmet, which grants 4), for a total of 3 (or 6 with the Ultrabright Helmet) when the full set is worn. A full set also provides a set bonus of 30% increased mining speed.

The Mining Shirt and Mining Pants are dropped from Undead Miners with a   /   chance.
The Mining Helmet can be purchased from the Merchant for   /  .
The Mining Helmet is also a drop from Undead Miners with a  chance in the .

Set
Crafting
Used in
Tips
Mining armor is most valuable for digging tunnels, especially with Smart Cursor enabled.
Using the Picksaw/Shroomite Digging Claw/ along with the mining armor is the best for digging hellshafts/hellevators, mainly because of their speed.
Using a Mining Potion increases mining speed by 25%; the mining bonuses stack with the Mining armor, for a total of 55% increased mining speed.
Equipping an Ancient Chisel increases mining speed by 25% (found in Oasis Crates, Mirage Crates, and Sandstone Chests). Equipping together with the Mining armor increases mining speed by a total of 55%, and if also combined with a Mining Potion, the mining speed increases by a total of 80%.
Using a Shroomite Digging Claw with the Light modifier, Mining armor, and the Well Fed buff a player can achieve the fastest mining speed in the game.

History

---

# 文档：Molten armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：molten armor
- 来源：wiki/Molten_armor

Molten armor is an armor set consisting of the Molten Helmet, Molten Breastplate, and Molten Greaves. It has the highest defense of any pre-Hardmode armor set, but can also be one of the most difficult to obtain.

A full set of Molten armor provides the following benefits:
+25 defense
+17% increased melee damage
+7% melee speed
+7% melee critical strike chance
Immunity to the On Fire! debuff
If the full set is visible, it will leave a fire trail similar to that of the Meteor armor.

It takes a total of 45 Hellstone Bars to craft a full set of Molten armor, or 135 Hellstone and 45 Obsidian.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
History
