# 文档：Monk armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：monk armor
- 来源：wiki/Monk_armor

The Monk armor is a Hardmode armor set purchased from the Tavernkeep for  in total, which consists of the Monk's Bushy Brow Bald Cap, Monk's Shirt and Monk's Pants. Wearing the full set will cause Lightning Aura sentry summons to deal damage 20% faster and allow them to deal critical hits at a fixed 16.7% rate.

The Shinobi Infiltrator armor can be considered a direct upgrade of the Monk armor.

A full set of Monk armor provides the following benefits:

+46 defense
+20% melee damage
+30% minion damage
+15%  / +10%  melee critical strike chance
+2 sentry slots
+20% melee speed
+20% movement speed

Set
Notes
The total bonus to max number of sentries is +2, allowing 3 to be summoned at once without other upgrades.
The attack speed and critical strike portions of this armor only apply to melee attacks.
The Monk armor and Squire armor both provide melee benefits. While the Squire armor has a higher melee crit increase, the Monk armor's increased melee speed means that (especially against enemies with low defense) it does more damage overall. This is especially true when accounting for Whips which are affected by melee speed bonuses, but not by melee damage bonuses. The Squire armor has more defense and a higher regeneration rate to compensate.
The Monk armor is a powerful armor against the mechanical bosses (once the player have defeated one to get it) and Plantera. Its bonuses are better than Hallowed armor, and having multiple Spider Queen or Lightning Aura sentries makes the fight much easier.  However, the Holy Protection provided by the Hallowed armor may be preferable.

See also
Monk's Belt
Squire armor
Huntress armor
Apprentice armor
Shinobi Infiltrator armor

# 文档：Moon Lord Legs

- 类别：armor
- 标识：moon lord legs
- 来源：wiki/Moon_Lord_Legs

The Moon Lord Legs are an armor item for the pants slot. They have a  chance to be found in Chests in drunk worlds.

The Moon Lord Legs replace the player's legs with Moon Lord-themed tentacles which produce a unique walking animation, a purely visual effect. In addition to providing  defense, the item has the following effects:

Notes
Any Chest type can spawn this item and it will generate alongside any loot already generated in that Chest.

Tips
This item is an excellent starting armor option which provides equivalent defense to a full set of Wood armor, increases base jump reach from  to  tiles, and has 5 times the speed boost of an Aglet.
The Moon Lord Legs can safely substitute the leg parts of some early armor sets, providing a mobility boost as well as a defense bonus in some cases:
Armors crafted from any type of wood only have +1 defense as a set bonus, with the greaves having either 1 defense as well or, in the case of regular Wood Greaves, no defense at all. As a result, replacing them with Moon Lord Legs gives a net defense bonus of +1 or +2 respectively.
Replacing the leg parts of Copper armor or Tin armor (both have 2 defense set bonus, while the corresponding greaves only have 1 defense) gives no net bonus to defense, but still may be useful because of improved mobility.
Doing the same with Iron armor will result in 1 less defense, because the Iron Greaves and the set bonus give a total of 4 defense, but this can be compensated with a Shackle, potentially allowing oneself to save enough Iron Bars for both a Pickaxe and a Broadsword.
For the Lead armor, the total loss is 2 defense, and for the higher-tier metal armors it only gets bigger. However, the Moon Lord Legs are made less useful this far into the game by such mobility accessories as Cloud in a Bottle and Hermes Boots, as well as Hooks.
The Moon Lord Legs provide an excellent synergy for early game mages using the Wizard Set, as those sets' bonuses are not inhibited by equipping leg slot armor. Some experimentation may be required to find an ideal combination, but the right Wizard Set variant coupled with the Moon Lord Legs could potentially be preferable over Meteor or even Jungle armor.
The Moon Lord Legs can also pair well with Rain armor due to it also lacking leg armor.
If one wishes to keep the original set’s visuals while wearing the Moon Lord Legs, they can equip the corresponding armor’s leg piece in a social slot.

Trivia
The Moon Lord's legs are a recurring in-joke in the Terraria community. This item was introduced by Redigit as a joke answer to a petition to add the Moon Lord's legs to the game as a boss.
The tooltip "" is a reference to the fact that Arkhayla jokingly claimed the Moon Lord to be "Cthulhu's youngest brother Steve".

History

---

# 文档：Mystic Robe

- 类别：armor
- 标识：mystic robe
- 来源：wiki/Mystic_Robe

The Mystic Robe (Gypsy Robe on ) is an armor item that can be worn in the shirt slot. It provides minimal defense (2) but grants a 6% damage and critical strike boost to magic weapons, along with a 10% reduction in mana use. It is purchased from the Traveling Merchant for   /  .

Wearing the Wizard Hat alongside the robe provides a set bonus of +10% magic critical strike chance, while wearing a Magic Hat alongside it provides a set bonus of +60 Mana. Any leg-slot equipment can be worn with these set bonuses intact.

# 文档：Mythril armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：mythril armor
- 来源：wiki/Mythril_armor

Mythril armor is an armor which requires Mythril Bars and must be crafted at a Mythril Anvil. Like all Hardmode ore armors, Mythril armor has three different headgear/helmet options, allowing players to focus on magical, melee or ranged combat, and each changing the set bonus. The alternative to Mythril Armor is Orichalcum Armor. Crafting a one-helmet set requires 45 Mythril Bars, or 180 Mythril Ore. Crafting all five pieces requires 65 Mythril Bars, or 260 Mythril Ore.

Set
Helmet variants
Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Tips
If the player changes their mind, the helmet piece can be shimmered and crafted into an alternative.

Trivia
is a fictional metal in J.R.R. Tolkien's Middle Earth writings, where it is a rare and high-quality material used for weapons and armor, and a shirt of mithril chainmail is a significant artifact in these writings. It has since become a widely used material in fantasy works, often under a minor alternate spelling such as mythril.

# 文档：Nebula armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：nebula armor
- 来源：wiki/Nebula_armor

Nebula armor is a post-Moon Lord armor set that provides magic weapon boosts. It is currently the final magic set available on the . Crafting this set requires a total of 36 Luminite Bars (144 Luminite) and 45 Nebula Fragments.

A full set of the Nebula armor will provide the following benefits:
+60 maximum mana
+26% magic damage
+16% magic critical strike chance
−15% mana usage
+10% movement speed

Additionally, equipping the full set grants a chance that special booster items will appear when striking enemies with  magic weapons /  any projectile (which includes minions). Each booster grants a buff that lasts 8 seconds and causes a spark effect to float around the player. When a booster item is picked up while an identical buff is already in effect, it stacks with the previous buff, for up to 3 stacked boosts. Every 8 seconds, one stacked booster expires and the buff decreases by one level. Booster items float in the air and have a greater pickup range than most items.

In multiplayer, any player can pick up these booster items and benefit from their buffs, even if they are not wearing Nebula armor. Additionally, picking up a booster item will grant nearby players (within a 43.75-tile radius) one instance of the buff. If the player acquiring the booster is on a team, only other players within that team will obtain the buff; otherwise, unaffiliated players will spread the buff to all other nearby players upon obtaining a booster.

width=150 | Item !! width=220 | Grants Buff !! Buff Description
||  ||  for 8 seconds
||  ||  ( 3 /  5 per second) for 8 seconds
||  ||  (about 10 per second) for 8 seconds

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
The Mana Booster, unlike natural or other sources of mana regeneration, will restore mana while using weapons that use mana.
There is a probability of  to yield a booster each time a player hurts an enemy, with a cooldown of 0.5 seconds.
The following enemies cannot yield boosters: Bubble Shield, Tesla Turret, Martian Drone, and Ancient Doom.
In addition to increasing its respective stack, picking up a booster will also refresh its duration.
All armors made from Luminite have no sell value.
As of , Luminite armor pieces have all been given a proper sell value.
Like all armors made from Luminite, equipping Nebula armor makes the player give off light, in this case purple. The more pieces equipped, the brighter the light is.

Tips
Due to its increased defense, positive damage bonuses, and extremely high-potential health regeneration, this armor set outclasses both Spectre armor variants offensively while providing survivability close to that of the Spectre Hood.
Additionally, the Moon Lord's Moon Bite debuff will not stop the Life Booster from working as it increases passive health regeneration, unlike the Spectre armor with Hood, which gives instant health restoration.
The Celestial Magnet does not increase the pickup range of the boosters, unlike the , since the game treats the boosters as items, not as Stars.

Trivia
The sprites of the boosters before they are picked up strongly resemble the Nebula Fragments that are needed to make the armor.
In real life, a  is a diffuse interstellar cloud of ionized gases. They are often hotspots of star formation.

See also
Solar Flare armor
Vortex armor
Stardust armor

History
References

# 文档：Necro armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：necro armor
- 来源：wiki/Necro_armor

Necro armor is a post-Skeletron armor set consisting of the Necro Helmet, Necro Breastplate, and Necro Greaves. Crafting a full set requires a total of 135 Cobwebs and 150 Bones.

The Ancient Necro Helmet is a rare drop from Angry Bones and Dark Casters in the Dungeon, with a  chance of dropping. On the , it's functionally identical to the Necro Helmet and the two helmets are interchangeable with no bonus loss.

A full set grants:
Defense: 19  / 16
Ranged damage:
Necro Helmet: 15%
Ancient Necro Helmet: 15%  / 14%
Ranged critical strike chance: 10%
Ammo consumption: 20% chance not to consume ammo

It's currently one of only two ranged-specific pre-Hardmode armor sets on , the other being Fossil armor. This set provides the highest offensive bonuses for the class prior to Hardmode. On , it's the only ranged armor set available in pre-Hardmode.

If the full set is worn, the player leaves an afterimage while running just like full Shadow armor. In addition, the sound the player makes when taking certain types of damage will be the same as for a Skeleton .

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
In a , this armor set is obtainable before defeating Skeletron, as Dungeon enemies can spawn prior to defeating the boss.
The Ancient Necro Helmet has the same sprite as the Necro Helmet had before 1.0.3, thus the phrase "Ancient" in it.
The Ancient Necro Helmet grants 1% less bonus damage than the Necro Helmet.

Tips
The set bonus stacks with other ammo conservation bonuses. When used with the Megashark, it produces a 60% chance to not consume ammo. Furthermore, using the Ammo Box from the Traveling Merchant will result in a 68% chance not to consume ammo. If an Ammo Reservation Potion is then used, the player's chance not to consume ammo amounts to 74.4%, meaning only approximately every fourth shot consumes a bullet.
Given both Cobwebs and Bones in surplus, making Necro Helmets is the most profitable way of monetizing either of them.
In the Drunken World seed, this set is obtainable prior to defeating any bosses, as Angry Bones will spawn above where the Dungeon Guardians will, and cobwebs are always obtainable in the underground.

Trivia
The term  comes from the Greek word νεκρός (nekrós, "dead").
It is the only armor set in the game of a  rarity.

History

---

# 文档：Night Vision Helmet

- 类别：armor
- 标识：night vision helmet
- 来源：wiki/Night_Vision_Helmet

The Night Vision Helmet is an armor piece for the helmet slot. For the player wearing it, it increases the apparent light level provided by all light sources on the screen, similarly to the Night Owl buff. It has a  chance to be dropped by Granite Elementals and Granite Golems. It can also be purchased from the Cyborg for   /  , after the Martian Madness event has been defeated at least once.

Crafting
Used in
Notes
The Night Vision Helmet's effect on lighting will not trigger if it is placed in the social helmet slot. It must be worn in place of a helmet armor piece.
The effect has the same strength as that of the Night Owl buff, granted by the Night Owl Potion. The Night Vision Helmet does not stack with Night Owl.
The Ultrabright Helmet can be used in place for the Mining Helmet when using the Mining armor set.

# 文档：Ninja armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：ninja armor
- 来源：wiki/Ninja_armor

Ninja armor or Ninja set is an armor/vanity set made up of the Ninja Hood, Ninja Shirt and Ninja Pants. King Slime drops each piece separately (1 per kill with a 1:3 chance each), or two pieces from the Treasure Bag it drops in Expert Mode. In the , pieces of the Ninja set can also be acquired from Lepus, but with only a 3% chance.

In the , it provides very little defense, but increases velocity and damage of throwing weapons.
In the , it provides very little defense, but increases crit chance and movement speed.
In the , it provides no defense, but does have throwing weapon bonuses listed in its stats. However, it does not provide the bonuses listed, and throwing damage is not a separate damage type in these versions.
In the , it is merely a vanity item and will provide no bonuses or defense.
In all versions though, having the full set equipped grants an after-image movement effect, similar to Shadow, Necro, and Cobalt armors.

Set
Trivia
It is possible to replace any part of the set with one of the Shadow armor's – the after-image movement effect will remain. (It is unknown if this is also the case on the , or with other armor sets.)

See also
Crystal Assassin armor, a Hardmode counterpart dropped by the Queen Slime.

History

---

# 文档：Obsidian armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：obsidian armor
- 来源：wiki/Obsidian_armor

Obsidian armor is an armor set that consists of the Obsidian Outlaw Hat, Obsidian Longcoat, and the Obsidian Pants.

A full set of Obsidian armor provides the following benefits:

+15 defense
+31% minion damage
+1 minion slot
+15% whip speed
+30% whip range

Obsidian armor does not grant a set bonus on the .

Crafting a full set requires a total of 60 Obsidian, 30 Silk, and  20 Shadow Scales or Tissue Samples.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
On the , Obsidian armor is statistically identical to Gold armor, without taking into account Gold armor's set bonus. Both can also be acquired before the player obtains a Deathbringer or Nightmare Pickaxe, but acquiring the Obsidian will require  or an alternate form of mining. On the , it requires fighting the Brain of Cthulhu or the Eater of Worlds to obtain Tissue Samples or Shadow Scales. Defeating them is not strictly necessary, however, as the ingredients are dropped during the fight. It can be crafted after killing an average of 9 Creepers or 27 Eater of Worlds segments.
Despite being made from Obsidian, this armor set grants no special protection against the On Fire! or Burning debuffs, whereas other Obsidian-related items do, like the Obsidian Skin Potion and Obsidian Skull.
The Whip Speed from its set bonus stacks multiplicatively with melee speed bonuses.
Despite being a pre-Hardmode armor set, this armor's offensive bonuses are comparable to those of Post Golem sets making it a viable choice all the way up to the late game despite its low defense.

Trivia
This set's unique set bonus to whip damage is possibly a reference to cowboys often being depicted with whips, as this armor is themed around a wild west outlaw.
Despite being a pre-Hardmode armor set, it gives the third most summon damage boost out of every summon set, bypassing Bee armor, Spider armor, Hallowed armor, and even Tiki armor, only beaten by Spooky armor and Stardust armor.
The internal names of the Obsidian Outlaw Hat and Longcoat are “ObsidianHelm” and “ObsidianShirt”, which may signify that the original designs looked more like traditional armour pieces. These could also just be placeholder names.
Despite being available in late pre-Hardmode, it only has a Rarity of .

History

---

# 文档：Orichalcum armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：orichalcum armor
- 来源：wiki/Orichalcum_armor

Orichalcum armor is a Hardmode armor set and is the counterpart to Mythril armor. Like all Hardmode ore armors, Orichalcum armor has three different headwear options, allowing players to focus on magical, melee, or ranged combat, and each with a corresponding set bonus. Crafting a one-helmet set requires 54 Orichalcum Bars or 216 Orichalcum Ore. Crafting all five pieces requires 78 Orichalcum Bars or 312 Orichalcum Ore.

Whenever the player strikes an enemy while wearing a full set, a flower petal is summoned at the edge of the screen and shoots to the other end of the screen. The flower petal will always be aimed at the enemy that triggered it, but it will damage any other enemy that it goes through. The flower petal has 36 base damage and can pierce, causing damage to multiple foes in a line with a single attack. Petals travel very quickly and pass through solid blocks and liquid with ease. There is a 20 frame cooldown before another flower petal can be summoned again, meaning that this set bonus adds roughly 100 DPS.

Set
Helmet variants
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
The flower petals will destroy most background objects, like Pots or the Larva inside a Bee Hive, which can lead to accidentally summoning the Queen Bee.
The flower petals can be triggered by minion attacks and the stars from the Star Cloak.

Tips
class=title colspan=3 | Orichalcum armor test
using dyes on accessories
||  ||
No dye || Pink Dye || Violet Dye
If the player changes their mind, the helmet piece can be shimmered and crafted into an alternative.
To color-match items to Orichalcum armor, use Violet Dye instead of Pink Dye.
Orichalcum armor's set bonus is powerful against worm-type enemies like Wyverns or The Destroyer, as the petals will hit all of the worm's segments if they enter at a lucky angle.
This armor is effective with high fire-rate weapons such as the Clockwork Assault Rifle, which can trigger the set bonus constantly.

Trivia
is a metal mentioned in ancient writings, but its nature and means of creation is lost to history, leaving it known only by name. It is frequently used in fantasy works as a material more valuable than real-world metals, but there is little consistency between its properties in popular culture.
The Orichalcum armor set with the headgear is a reference to Andromeda Shun from the anime Saint Seiya.

History

---

# 文档：Palladium armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：palladium armor
- 来源：wiki/Palladium_armor

Palladium armor is a Hardmode armor set crafted from Palladium Bars. It is the alternative to Cobalt armor. Like many Hardmode armor sets, Palladium armor has three different headpiece options, allowing players to focus on magical, melee, or ranged combat.

With the full set equipped, successful attacks trigger the Rapid Healing buff, which heals
2  / 3  HP/s (12  / 15  health over 5 seconds). Further attacks will refresh the duration. The buff is not canceled if the armor is removed. It can be canceled manually by right-clicking the buff icon.

Crafting a one-headpiece set requires 54 Palladium Bars, or 162 Palladium Ore. Crafting all five pieces requires 78 Palladium Bars, or 234 Palladium Ore.

Set
Helmet variants
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
The set bonus allows regeneration while afflicted with the Bleeding debuff.
The buff is triggered even when attacking Critters, like Bunnies and Penguins.
The buff is also triggered when the player's minions attack.
The buff is also triggered when you hit a dummy.

Tips
If the player changes their mind, the helmet piece can be shimmered and crafted into an alternative.
While not the largest or quickest heal, it can be useful for the beginning of Hardmode, especially when used with ranged weapons, to safely regenerate from a distance.
Despite having lower defense than Adamantite Armor, Palladium will usually be better at keeping you alive. Comparing the melee versions of the armors, Adamantite has 18 more defense. This means the player will take 9 /  /  more damage than they would if they had used Adamantite, so as long as you are skilled enough to avoid taking damage for at least every 4.5 /  /  seconds, Palladium armor might be a better option for survival.
The Squire armor, available from the Tavernkeep after defeating a mechanical boss, can be considered an upgrade to Palladium armor. The Squire's Great Helm provides a constant 2 HP/sec regeneration. Later on, the Valhalla Knight armor's breastplate provides 4 HP/sec regeneration.
If used with the  your life regen will increase considerably. Although the effects stack additive.
Trivia
Lovestruck's cosmetic effect is indentical to the heart particles emitted from Rapid Healing. It is purely visual and offers no healing, however.
The Palladium armor represents a fuedal European/medival theme

History

---

# 文档：Palm Wood armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：palm wood armor
- 来源：wiki/Palm_Wood_armor

Palm Wood armor is an Armor set that consists of the Palm Wood Helmet, Palm Wood Breastplate, and Palm Wood Greaves.

Making this armor set requires the player to chop down Palm Trees, which can grow naturally in the Ocean and  Oasis biomes or be manually planted using Acorns on Sand Blocks. Like the other Wood armor sets, it is fairly weak, and only useful very early on. It does provide a one defense bonus over the normal Wood armor, meaning if the player comes across the  Oasis early on, it may be a wise idea to chop down Palm Trees to make this armor set as a minor upgrade.

Wearing a full set grants a basic 3 defense plus a set bonus of 1 defense, which equals a total of 4 defense.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Boreal Wood armor and Rich Mahogany armor share identical stats. Because the player will encounter the Jungle or the Snow biome depending on which direction they travel, these armors are more readily available.

History

---

# 文档：Pearlwood armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：pearlwood armor
- 来源：wiki/Pearlwood_armor

Pearlwood armor is a Hardmode armor set consisting of the Pearlwood Helmet, the Pearlwood Breastplate, and the Pearlwood Greaves.

Wearing a full set grants a basic 7 defense plus a set bonus of 1 defense, which equals a total of 8 defense.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Tips
It is the most defensive basic wooden armor, though it is the weakest Hardmode armor set, and can only be acquired in a Hardmode world in most cases. This makes the armor set seem to serve little purpose outside of vanity or collectability as the mere presence of the Hallow in a world means that Hardmode has already been triggered, and a defense rating of 8 is woefully insufficient for the tough enemies and bosses awaiting players.
An exception would be in  worlds, where crafting this armor is viable due to natural spawning of Hallowed grass on Floating Islands.

Trivia
The Pearlwood Helmet appears to have a Unicorn Horn on it.
Pearlwood armor and Spooky armor are the only Hardmode Armor sets crafted at a Work Bench.

History

---

# 文档：Pink Snow armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：pink snow armor
- 来源：wiki/Pink_Snow_armor

The Pink Snow armor (on ) or Pink Eskimo armor (on ) is an armor set consisting of the three following pieces:
Pink Snow Hood, Pink Snow Coat, and Pink Snow Pants
Pink Eskimo Hood, Pink Eskimo Coat, and Pink Eskimo Pants

Wearing the full set provides immunity to being frozen or chilled. It grants a total of  defense.

It is crafted from the Snow armor by adding Pink Thread. It is completely identical to the original set, except in its color.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Tips
This is a rather redundant armor set. It is cheaper and easier for the player to just equip three Pink Dyes along with the normal Snow armor.

Trivia
This armor set may be a reference to Nana, one of the protagonists of the vertical platform video game  which was developed and published by  for the  in 1985. Nana wears an outfit almost identical to the Pink Snow armor.
Prior to , this armor set was (and on the  still is) called Pink Eskimo armor instead of Pink Snow armor.
s are the indigenous peoples who have traditionally inhabited the northern circumpolar region. In Canada and Greenland, the term Eskimo is predominately seen as pejorative and has been widely replaced by the term Inuit.
Previously, only the  of Terraria used the "Snow" set names.
History

---

# 文档：Platinum armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：platinum armor
- 来源：wiki/Platinum_armor

Platinum armor is a stronger alternative to Gold armor. It takes a total of  75 /  90 Platinum Bars to craft a full suit. This adds up to 360 Platinum Ore for the full set of Platinum Armor. It is also the strongest set that is made from ore which can be mined from the start (with a Copper Pickaxe)

It consists of a Platinum Helmet, Platinum Chainmail and Platinum Greaves.

A full set grants a basic 16 defense plus a set bonus of 4 defense, which equals a total of 20 defense, 4 more than gold, making it noticeably stronger. It is a viable alternative to Shadow armor or Crimson armor for entering Hardmode, if the player wants 1 extra defense.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Tips
It may be preferable to skip this armor set and go for Meteor, Jungle, or Hellstone armors instead because of how expensive the Platinum armor is to craft.

Trivia
While the Tin, Lead, and Tungsten armors closely resemble their ore counterparts, the Platinum Helmet is more similar in design to the Ancient Gold Helmet.

History

---

# 文档：Pumpkin armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：pumpkin armor
- 来源：wiki/Pumpkin_armor

The Pumpkin armor is an armor set crafted from Pumpkins at any Work Bench. 75 Pumpkins are required to craft the full set. It is much better than Wood or Cactus armor as it has higher defense than Cactus and increases all damage dealt by the player by 10%.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Due to the set bonus, the Pumpkin armor could be considered superior even to Gold or Platinum armor.
This armor set can give a large advantage to new players during Halloween, as Pumpkins can grow naturally on grass and can be harvested right at the beginning of the game.
If it is not Halloween, the player will either be required to have defeated a boss and gotten the Dryad to move in, or locate Shimmer and convert Cactus into Pumpkin.
During Halloween, this armor set may prove useful to those using Summon weapons. There are few other minion-damage boosting armor pieces that can be obtained before challenging a boss, and Pumpkin armor can be obtained with basically zero effort.

History

---

- 类别：armor

Rain armor or Rain set is an armor/Vanity items set that consists of the Rain Hat and the Rain Coat. It is armor on the . On the , the set only act as vanity items, and offer no Defense when equipped.

Each piece has a  chance to be dropped by Rain Coat Zombies.

Set
Trivia
Rain armor is currently one of two armor sets in the game that comes in two pieces, the other one being the Wizard set.
Despite the similar method of obtaining them, the Snow armor actually has a lower drop rate than the Rain armor and has three pieces, but can be obtained at anytime during the night.

# 文档：Red Riding armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：red riding armor
- 来源：wiki/Red_Riding_armor

The Red Riding armour is a Hardmode, ranger-oriented armour set purchased from the Tavernkeep (who will move in after Golem has been defeated). The set consists of the Red Riding Hood, Red Riding Dress and Red Riding Leggings. Wearing the full set will cause Explosive Traps to attack much faster (from every 1.83 seconds to every 0.67 seconds) and to have a 1/2 chance to inflict 4-9 seconds of the Oiled debuff, making enemies take increased damage from On Fire!, Cursed Inferno, Frostburn and Shadowflame.

A full set of Red Riding armor will provide the following stats:

+48 defense
+25% ranged damage
+60% minion damage
+20%  / +10%  ranged critical strike chance
+20% ammo conservation
+20% movement speed
+3 extra sentry slots.

The Red Riding armor set is a direct upgrade of the Huntress armor set.

Set
Notes
The total bonus to max number of sentries is +3, allowing 4 to be summoned at once.
The Red Riding Dress provides the highest ranged damage bonus in the game, and the Red Riding Leggings offer the highest minion damage bonus in the game. The Red Riding Leggings are tied with Mythril Greaves for the second-highest combined bonuses to ranged damage and critical strike chance, behind Vortex Leggings.
The Red Riding Leggings provide the game's highest minion damage increase for the leggings slot, 3% higher than the Stardust Leggings' bonus. However, the Stardust Leggings add two minions to your arsenal in addition to the damage bonuses, which would lead to higher DPS for a pure summoner. While the Stardust Leggings provide two additional minions, the Red Riding Leggings provide ranged bonuses in addition to the minion boost, which would likely be preferred for a mixed playstyle.

Tips
This set is a powerful hybrid between ranged and summoning styles. It offers higher offensive ranged bonuses than Chlorophyte armor and Hallowed armor, though not as high as those provided by Shroomite armor.
However, it also significantly boosts the damage output of both sentries and minions. When combined with the Necromantic Scroll and Papyrus Scarab, a player can have significant ranged damage boosts combined with, for instance, 3 boosted Xeno Staff summons. By the time you gain the Stardust Cell Staff or Stardust Dragon Staff, Red Riding armor can begin to significantly outperform Shroomite armor due to its minion damage.
As neither Shroomite armor nor Red Riding armor provide a very useful set bonus for a pure ranger, one can combine Red Riding armor with a head piece from Shroomite armor to gain the best of both worlds. The Red Riding Dress is slightly weaker for pure ranged than the Shroomite Breastplate, but its large bonus to minion damage outweighs this disadvantage when using minions.
The Explosive Trap sentry summons, ordinarily a poor choice due to being stationary and slow, become far more powerful when using the full set. They attack much more rapidly, dealing damage faster than any other sentries within the same tier. Placing four of them around you (or below you, if flying) during moon events can help you soar through the early stages.

Trivia
This armor set is a reference to the Huntress' Red Riding Hood costume in Dungeon Defenders II, which itself is a reference to the fairy tale .

See also
Valhalla Knight armor
Dark Artist armor
Shinobi Infiltrator armor

History

---

# 文档：Rich Mahogany armor

- 类别：armor

- 来源：wiki/Rich_Mahogany_armor

Rich Mahogany armor is an Armor set consisting of the Rich Mahogany Helmet, Rich Mahogany Breastplate, and Rich Mahogany Greaves.

It is slightly more defensive than normal Wood armor, although it is a bit more difficult to obtain, since it requires the player to go to the Jungle, which can be problematic for unprepared early characters.

Wearing a full set grants a basic 3 defense plus a set bonus of 1 defense, which equals a total of 4 defense.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Stats-wise, Palm Wood armor and Boreal Wood armor are exact copies of this armor.

Trivia
The Dye Trader NPC states that it is the only wood type worth dyeing, although this has no effect whatsoever as it is purely trivial.

# 文档：Ruby Robe

- 类别：armor
- 标识：ruby robe

The Ruby Robe is an Armor item and part of the Wizard set, being wearable in the shirt slot. It is the third strongest Gem Robe, weaker than the Amber Robe and the Diamond Robe.

Wearing the Wizard Hat alongside the Ruby Robe provides a set bonus of +10% magic critical strike chance, while wearing a Magic Hat alongside it provides a set bonus of +60 Mana. Any leg-slot equipment can be worn with these set bonuses remaining intact.

Recipes

This item is the armor analog to the Ruby Staff.

# 文档：Shadewood armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：shadewood armor
- 来源：wiki/Shadewood_armor

Shadewood armor is an armor set consisting of the Shadewood Helmet, Shadewood Breastplate, and Shadewood Greaves.

It is crafted solely from Shadewood harvested from Crimson Trees. It acts as the Crimson counterpart to the Corruption's Ebonwood armor, which shares the same stats. Along with its counterpart, it is the most powerful Pre-Hardmode wooden set, though it still pales in comparison to most other armor sets. It requires 75 Shadewood to craft a full set.

Wearing a full set grants a set bonus of 1 additional defense, providing a total of 5 defense.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
It may be a good idea to craft the Cactus armor instead, since it has similar stats and only requires entering the Desert; characters that could make use of Shadewood armor are likely not ready to enter the Crimson.
Shadewood is initially obtainable in Crimson worlds only.  An artificial Crimson biome can created using materials from another gameworld or by buying Crimson Seeds from the Dryad while in a , but such things are not part of normal game progression.

# 文档：Shadow armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：shadow armor
- 来源：wiki/Shadow_armor

Shadow armor is an armor set that consists of the Shadow Helmet, Shadow Scalemail, and Shadow Greaves. The individual pieces provide a total of  defense, equal to its Crimson counterpart, Crimson armor. Each of the 3 pieces provide a +5% boost to critical strike chance (for a total of +15%), while wearing all 3 pieces at once grants an additional set bonus of +15% movement speed and an acceleration speed bonus.

Wearing a full set causes the player to display an afterimage effect during motion (similar to Necro armor) and emit shadow particles. Also, the Rocket Boots will emit purple flames (this does not occur with Spectre Boots or any of its derivatives).

Crafting a full set requires a total of 60 Demonite Bars (  /   Demonite Ore) and 45 Shadow Scales. Defeating the Eater of Worlds twice can provide enough to craft a full set.

Shadow armor pieces are interchangeable with pieces from the Ancient Shadow armor set, while still providing the same 15% increased movement speed set bonus, but not the afterimage effect.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Trivia
Despite requiring the fewest resources to craft, the Shadow Helmet sells for the most.

# 文档：Shinobi Infiltrator armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：shinobi infiltrator armor
- 来源：wiki/Shinobi_Infiltrator_armor

The Shinobi Infiltrator armor is a Hardmode armor set purchased from the Tavernkeep for   /   in total, after Golem has been defeated. It consists of the Shinobi Infiltrator's Helmet, Shinobi Infiltrator's Torso, and Shinobi Infiltrator's Pants.

Wearing the full set will cause Lightning Aura sentry summons to deal damage 20% faster and allow them to deal critical hits at a fixed 25% rate, as well as increasing their size by 4 tiles (from 8 to 12 tiles total) in each direction.

A full set of Shinobi Infiltrator armor will provide the following stats:
+54 defense
+20% melee damage
+20% melee speed
+60% minion damage
+25%  / +20%  melee critical strike chance
+30%  / +20%  movement speed
+3 extra sentry slots.

The Shinobi Infiltrator armor set is a direct upgrade to the Monk armor set.

Set
Notes
Shinobi Infiltrator armor provides higher melee bonuses overall than Solar Flare armor, the post-Moon Lord armor set, with slightly less of a damage boost and slightly more in speed and critical hit chance. However, each individual piece is outclassed by another armor piece (the Chlorophyte Mask, Solar Flare Breastplate, and Valhalla Knight's Greaves respectively).
Additionally, the torso could be switched for the Monk's Shirt for an extra 20% damage at the loss of the set bonus and 4 defense.
The offensive version of Beetle armor has lower base bonuses as well, but the full Beetle's Might buff, when active gives larger bonuses to both melee damage and melee speed.
The torso can be swapped out for the Valhalla Knight's Breastplate to greatly increase one's defensive capabilities in trade for a lower melee speed.
However it's also worth noting that if one was to wear the full Shinobi Infiltrator's set, they can place the enhanced Lightning Aura sentries, and then swap to better armor pieces. It requires a little more micro-management, but allows one to keep the set bonus, while still benefiting from better armor.
The total bonus to max number of sentries is +3, allowing 4 to be summoned at once.
This set also increases whip speed, making it still a considerable set for summoners (especially when synergized with the Titan Glove or any of its upgrades).

Trivia
A  (忍者) or shinobi (忍び) was a covert agent or mercenary in feudal Japan. The functions of the ninja included espionage, sabotage, infiltration, and assassination, and open combat in certain situations.

See also
Dark Artist armor
Red Riding armor
Valhalla Knight armor

# 文档：Shroomite armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：shroomite armor
- 来源：wiki/Shroomite_armor

Shroomite Armor is a Hardmode, post-Plantera armor set that boosts the effectiveness of ranged weapons. It is available after defeating Plantera and is the second-strongest ranger armor available, only beaten by Vortex armor () or Titan armor ().

With a full set equipped, standing still causes a player to enter "stealth mode": Over the course of roughly 1.5 seconds, the player will gradually turn almost fully transparent, which also diminishes the chance that enemies will target the player. Additionally, during this time, weapon damage, critical strike chance, and knockback will each be boosted gradually up to a maximum of +60% damage, +10% critical chance, and +50% knockback at full stealth. These boosts increase both weapon and ammo damage. The buff begins fading quickly if the wearer starts moving.

Shroomite armor has three headpiece options, each providing an extra ×1.15 damage boost that affects a different ranged weapon type:
Shroomite Headgear for arrows
Shroomite Mask for bullets
Shroomite Helmet for rockets and all other ranged weapons that do not use arrows or bullets.

Note that this increase is applied after applying damage increases from the rest of the armor set, accessories and buffs. As a result, Shroomite headpieces provide a larger damage bonus than their tooltip would suggest.

Some ranged weapons do not receive the specialized boost from any of the three options (e.g. Star Cannon, Piranha Gun and Flamethrower), though they still receive the set stealth boost.

A full set of Shroomite armor will provide the following stats:
+51 defense.
×1.15 damage of Arrows, Bullets, or Rockets, depending on which headpiece is worn.
+13% ranged damage for all ranged weapons, including those not covered by the headpiece of choice.
+25% ranged critical strike chance.
+20% chance not to consume ammo.
+12% movement speed.
Ability to enter stealth mode.

Crafting Shroomite Armor requires Shroomite Bars (crafted from Chlorophyte Bars via the Autohammer, which is in turn purchased from the Truffle NPC). Crafting a full set with a single headpiece requires 54 Shroomite Bars (or 810 Glowing Mushrooms and  270 /  324 Chlorophyte Ores). A full set with all three headpieces requires 78 Shroomite Bars (1170 Glowing Mushrooms and  390  /  468  Chlorophyte Ores).

Set
Helmet Variations
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
]]
With a full set equipped, the player leaves an afterimage effect during motion, similar to the one displayed by Shadow armor and Hallowed armor, but with a blue tint.
The +13% ranged damage bonus from the Shroomite Breastplate is not listed in its tooltip.
For the purposes of the specialized headpiece boost:
The Stynger, Jack 'O Lantern Launcher, and Nail Gun count as rocket weapons.
The Candy Corn Rifle and Coin Gun count as bullet weapons.
The Stake Launcher counts as an arrow weapon.
None of the headpieces boost the damage of the ammo items themselves.
Using the Rod of Discord or the Hook of Dissonance will not cancel stealth mode.
Stealth (and any associated bonus) will not work while mounted.
Stealth is canceled if the player takes damage, even if they are resistant to knockback (making the bonus useless in combination with an invulnerability machine).

Tips
If the player changes their mind, the helmet piece can be shimmered and crafted into an alternative.
This armor set has good synergy with the Shiny Stone, as both require the player to stand still and not get attacked to maximize their effectiveness.
While at first, tanking strategies such as abusing invincibility frames from lava damage with a Cross Necklace appear to pair well with Shroomite armour, because they require taking damage the stealth mode buff is removed and will not regenerate without stopping firing.
This armor set also has good synergy with the Sniper Rifle, as stealth massively boosts the Sniper Rifle's main strengths – damage and critical strike chance – even further. With the right loadout, each rifle shot can deal over 1000 damage, killing most non-boss enemies in one hit.
The set does not truly make the player invisible, as the armor glows a deep blue. For PvP purposes, consider using Familiar clothes in conjunction with Shroomite armor, and with no visible accessories. With stealth active, this can render the character completely invisible to other players. Also see Invisibility Potion.
Wearing a Shroomite Mask with Silver Dye can make the player's head resemble a cracked skull.
Some Dye combinations make the character sprite appear very near invisible at night. Provided below are several links to screenshots from testing.
This armor set can be particularly effective when used with the Hook of Dissonance, as the teleportation of the hook is not considered movement for the purposes of the armor's stealth.
Because many rocket weapons have low base damage and derive their power from the high base damage of their ammo, the Shroomite Helmet is often inferior to the Hallowed Helmet or Adamantite Mask, which boost both weapon and ammunition damage.
If not using the stealth properties of this armor, swapping out the leggings for Mythril Greaves or Red Riding Leggings will increase your ranged DPS (and minion damage, if using the Leggings). Swapping out the chestplate for the Red Riding Dress will provide roughly the same ranged DPS and ammo conservation while adding another 25% minion damage.

Trivia
Shroomite Armor is the only armor that has different headpieces with the same amount of defense.
The Shroomite Mask resembles the Sonar Goggles from the Tom Clancy's Splinter Cell series.
Because the stealth mode buff is on an analog scale, you can observe the damage of your weapons ticking up over the period of 1.5 seconds for the buff to take full effect.
Similar behaviour is observed in the Psycho Knife, which also features a stealth mode.

History
References

# 文档：Snow armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：snow armor
- 来源：wiki/Snow_armor

The Snow armor (on ) or Eskimo armor (on ) is an armor set consisting of the three following pieces:
Snow Hood, Snow Coat, and Snow Pants
Eskimo Hood, Eskimo Coat, and Eskimo Pants
Each piece is very rarely dropped from Frozen Zombies  (Zombie Eskimos ), each having a 0.67% drop chance.

Wearing the full set provides immunity to being frozen or chilled. It grants a total of  defense.

Due to its low defense, it is generally favored more as a vanity set rather than a viable armor set.

Set
Crafting
Used in
Tips
The best way for hunting this set is killing Frozen Zombies during a pre-Hardmode Blood Moon, with both a Battle Potion and a Water Candle in effect.
This set is fairly easy to get, therefore making it a good armor set to start the game with.

Trivia
This armor set may be a reference to Popo, one of the protagonists of the vertical platform video game  which was developed and published by  for the  in 1985. Popo wears an outfit almost identical to the Snow armor.
Prior to , this armor set was (and on the  still is) called Eskimo armor instead of Snow armor.
s are the indigenous peoples who have traditionally inhabited the northern circumpolar region. In Canada and Greenland, the term Eskimo is predominately seen as pejorative and has been widely replaced by the term Inuit, even though the  are a distinctive group of aboriginal peoples in Canada.
Previously, only the  of Terraria used the "Snow" set names.

# 文档：Solar Flare armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：solar flare armor
- 来源：wiki/Solar_Flare_armor

Solar Flare armor is a post-Moon Lord set of armor that provides melee weapon boosts. It is currently the final melee set available on the . This armor set also provides the most base defense. Crafting it requires a total of 36 Luminite Bars (144 Luminite) and 45 Solar Fragments.

A full set of the Solar Flare armor will provide the following benefits:
+26% /  +17%  melee critical hit chance
+29% /  +22% melee damage
+15% melee speed
+15% movement speed
Increased life regeneration (+3 HP/sec)
+900 aggro

Equipping the full set additionally provides a special set bonus that builds the longer the player avoids damage, up to 3 "charges" (similar to Beetle armor). Each takes  3 /  5 seconds to build. The charges provide a fixed damage reduction of 20%/30% regardless of the number of charges. The number of charges built is indicated by a shield that appears after the first charge, with two and three spikes appearing on the shield on the second and third charges, respectively.

Taking damage causes one charge to deplete, and discharges fire explosions around the player that can damage enemies in the vicinity. A charge can also be discharged manually by using the shield's special "dash" attack, activated by double-tapping  or . Dashing only consumes a charge if it strikes an enemy, and upon doing so causes a similar explosion. Regardless of depleted charges, a player can only dash once per second.

The set bonus also grants 12% base damage reduction, which stacks multiplicatively with the damage reduction from the shield.

Each piece equipped (even if hidden by vanity items) causes the player to emit light, with a higher light level for each additional piece equipped. Additionally, equipping the full set displays both an after-image motion effect and a pulsating effect, similar to Hallowed armor.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
The fire explosions are affected by melee bonuses and can critically hit.
The dash does not give you invincibility frames if the dash does not kill the enemy or if you contact a projectile.
Taking fall damage also triggers the explosion discharge, depleting one shield charge.
Holding the Psycho Knife will increase the damage of the dash.
The dash causes the player to move at .
This dash will cause the player to move  faster than the Dungeon Guardian and  faster than the dash of Biome Mimics when the player is too far away.
The dash effect of the armor overrides the dash effect of the Tabi, Master Ninja Gear, and Shield of Cthulhu accessories.
The Endurance Potion, Frozen Turtle Shell, and Worm Scarf's damage reduction can stack with this armor.
The Solar Blaze buff tooltip still displays "Damage taken reduced by 30%" even though the actual effect is 20%.
The set bonus still says "consume the shield power to dash", although, as of 1.3.0.6, the dash no longer consumes it if not hitting an enemy.
All armors made from Luminite have no sell value.
The shield can be dyed using the chest dye slot.
Like all armors made from Luminite, equipping Solar Flare armor makes the player emit light, in this case, red. Light radius increases with the number of pieces equipped.

Tips
The Beetle armor with the Beetle Scale Mail and full Beetle Might has over twice the offensive benefits of the Solar Flare armor (+44% melee damage and +42% melee speed for Beetle compared to merely +22% melee damage and +15% melee speed for Solar Flare armor). In terms of defense, Solar Flare armor provides slightly more defense than Beetle armor with the Beetle Shell, but provides 15% less damage reduction and has an activation cooldown of 5 seconds rather than 3. Solar Flare armor always reduces damage taken by 30%, whereas Beetle armor has a maximum damage reduction of 45%.
Due to this, the Dungeon Guardian deals 30 more damage to players with Solar Flare armor, given they have the Endurance buff active. With the buff and Beetle armor with Shell, the Dungeon Guardian deals 495 damage.
Since the 12% damage reduction from the set bonus stacks additively with some other buffs, the total damage reduction of Solar Flare armor gets less inferior in comparison to Beetle armor as more of these buffs are applied. With Endurance, Ice Barrier, and the Worm Scarf, Beetle armor offers at most  damage reduction, and Solar Flare, , which is only  percentage points (or  actual damage reduction) less.

Trivia
The shield acts somewhat like the Shield of Cthulhu, but does more damage, dashes further and does not knock the player backward on hit.
Solar Flare armor essentially combines the effects of Turtle armor (damage reflection), Beetle armor (damage reduction), and the Shield of Cthulhu (dash).
On the , wearing Beetle armor with the Beetle Shell provides the most defense, at 73. The Solar Flare armor, which is not available on those versions, exceeds this by 5 defense points.
A  is a sudden increase of brightness on the . More powerful flares are then followed by a coronal mass ejection, which is a significant release of plasma from the Sun's surface.

See also
Vortex armor
Nebula armor
Stardust armor

History

# 文档：Spectral armor

- 类别：armor

Spectral armor is a Hardmode magic armor set consisting of the Spectral Headgear, the Spectral Armor and the Spectral Subligar. Each separate piece of Spectral armor has a  chance of being dropped by Ocram, or can otherwise be crafted.

A full set of Spectral armor provides the following benefits:
+40 defense
+150 maximum mana
+30% magic damage
+25% magic critical strike chance
-33% mana usage
+10% movement speed

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
The Spectral armor has a non- exclusive counterpart, being Spectre armor as they are both obtained post-Plantera. Spectral armor is available pre-Plantera as it is possible to summon Ocram pre-mechanical bosses, but is not recommended.

Trivia
Prior to Console 1.02, the Spectral Subligar was the only item in the game to increase the player's mana by an amount not divisible by 20.
Spectral armor is tied with the Diamond Robe + Magic Hat in maximum mana increase.
The Spectral armor's shirt is called Spectral Armor, which can lead to some confusion when discussing the armor with other players. Magic armor sets usually have a breastplate or robe for the shirt slot.
Wearing the Spectral Headgear, the Diamond Robe and the Spectral Subligar will increase the player's maximum mana by the highest possible amount of 230.
Spectral armor is the only Ocram dropped armor that is comparable to its non- exclusive counterparts (based on stats alone.)

# 文档：Spectre armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：spectre armor
- 来源：wiki/Spectre_armor

Spectre armor is a Hardmode, post-Plantera armor set that offers magic weapon boosts. The set consists of the Spectre Robe and Spectre Pants, with either the Spectre Hood or Spectre Mask  headpiece. Wearing a full set causes small white orbs to manifest whenever the player hits an enemy with a magic weapon. The effect of these orbs depends on the headpiece option chosen: The Spectre Mask causes the orbs to act as homing projectiles, while the Spectre Hood causes the orbs to travel to a player, healing them.

Crafting a full set with one headpiece requires 54 Spectre Bars (27 Ectoplasm and  270 /  324 Chlorophyte Ore). The full set with both headpieces requires 66 Spectre Bars (33 Ectoplasm and  330 /  396 Chlorophyte Ore).

Crafting a full set with one headpiece requires 54 Chlorophyte Bars (325 Chlorophyte Ore) and 54 Ectoplasm. The full set with both headpieces requires 66 Chlorophyte Bars (396 Chlorophyte Ore) and 66 Ectoplasm.

Spectre Mask set bonus: Homing orb projectiles spawn when dealing magic damage to an enemy, which attack nearby enemies, or the same enemy. The orbs deal  100% /  50% of the damage dealt by the magic weapon fired, before it is doubled by a critical hit (if a critical hit deals 200 damage, the orb will have  100 /  50 base damage).

Spectre Hood set bonus: Life-stealing orbs spawn when dealing magic damage to an enemy, which fly to the player and restore health by  20% /  8% of damage done (e.g. dealing 50 magic damage heals  10 /  4 health). In multiplayer, the player with the lowest health on the team is healed instead.  Equipping full set with the Spectre Hood /  the Spectre Hood incurs a 40% magic damage decrease.

Set
Helmet variants
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
If an attack does not do at least 3 damage while the Spectre Mask is equipped, a damaging orb will not appear. If an attack does not do at least  5 /  13 damage while the Spectre Hood is equipped, a healing orb will not appear.
Both set bonuses have a limit to their efficiency:
The Spectre Hood's healing is based on a hidden stat, "Life Steal", which recharges by 30 every second, up to a maximum of 80 / . This stat is drained by the amount healed every time damage is dealt, meaning that while in constant combat its healing is capped at 30 health regained per second, with an initial burst of 80 / .
The Spectre Mask's damage orbs are based on a hidden stat, "Ghost Damage". Ghost Damage recharges by  400 /  150 every second, up to a maximum of  1500 /  1000. This stat is drained by the damage dealt in every hit, meaning that while in constant combat its extra  is capped at  400 /  150, with an initial burst of  1500 /  1000 when entering it.
Since the orb projectiles are able to critically strike, critical strike bonuses will increase this extra  further.
Wearing the full set with either helmet makes the player translucent.
Also, if the player is riding a Minecart, it will also appear semitransparent.
Dyes are not as visible on a full set of Spectre armor as they are on other armor sets.
As the rest of the Spectre armor grants a total of 15% increased damage, the total effect of wearing the Spectre Hood is only a 25% magic damage reduction.

Tips
If the player changes their mind, the helmet piece can be shimmered and crafted into the alternative.
One of the most efficient ways to play with this armor is by owning both the Spectre Hood and the Spectre Mask and switching between them, depending on the situation. This way the player will be able to both keep themselves alive and deal continuous high damage.
With the introduction of Loadouts in 1.4.4, one could make a healing-centric build with Spectre armor in a secondary loadout slot which can be switched to and from instantly with F1-3.
The combined effects of Mana Sickness and the Spectre Hood's damage reduction will render many magic weapons too weak to be effective, so players should find an alternate means of restoring mana.
Magic Cuffs or Celestial Cuffs synergize well with the Spectre Hood as they capitalize on any damage taken; with a highly-damaging weapon like the Razorpine, it is possible to maintain both health and mana at comfortable levels.
Spectre armor with the Spectre Hood is widely considered useless against the , whose Moon bite debuff disables lifesteal effects, unless one moves far enough to avoid his tongue to latch on. However, such strategy is usually ineffective since Moon Lord can teleport to the player if they move too far from him. Despite this, the buff may still be very useful, as in classic worlds there is often a time frame while the core is not exposed in which the moon bite debuff is not applied, usually after the Phantasmal Deathray has been used. Homing weapons with fast projectiles, especially the Nightglow, are useful for healing during this brief period, as the projectiles can build up, and heal you the second Moon Bite wears off.

Trivia
The Spectre Hood is the only piece of armor that decreases the player's stats. On the , the decrease is only caused indirectly, via the set bonus.
is another word for ghost or spirit, alluding to the fact that the Ectoplasm, required for crafting the armor set, is dropped by Dungeon Spirits.

History

---

Spider armor is an early Hardmode armor crafted from 36 Spider Fangs.

A full set of Spider armor provides the following benefits:

+20 defense
+3 minion slots
+28% minion damage.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
The total bonuses are composed of the following:
5% minion damage each from Mask and Breastplate ( despite the tooltip saying differently), 6% from Greaves, and 12% from set bonus.

Utilizing the Spider Staff only to summon minions allows for 5 minions instead of 4 due to each spider counting as .75 of a minion.

# 文档：Spooky armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：spooky armor
- 来源：wiki/Spooky_armor

Spooky armor is a Hardmode post-Plantera armor set made from Spooky Wood. Wearing the full set increases the wearer's minion capacity by 4 (to a total of 5), along with granting +58% minion damage and creating a glowing effect around the character. It requires a total of 750 [Spooky Wood] to create the full set.

A full set of Spooky armor provides the following benefits:

+30  / 27  defense
+4  / 3  minion slots
+58% minion damage
+11% movement speed (tool-tip says 20%, but actual measurements are more like 11.2% on 1.4.4.9)

On  Spooky armor's +28% minion damage and +11% movement speed is generally considered superior to Tiki armor's +20% whip range and +5 defense.  Summoners who play an active role with a whip and will prefer Tiki over Spooky (such summoners would also choose Obsidian armor over Bee armor pre-hardmode).  Post-Moon Lord,  is superior to either.

On  Spooky armor has one less minion and 3 less defense.  Without additional minion capacity accessories/buffs, this makes Tiki armor's minion damage slightly higher (130% × 5 = 650% vs. Spooky's 158% x 4 = 632%).  Once an extra minion or sentry is summoned, Spooky armor increases minion damage by 790%, while Tiki armor only increases it by 780%. The more minions that can be summoned, the more damage minions do with Spooky armor.  Spooky armor always increases whip damage more than Tiki.

Spooky armor is both the strongest and most expensive armor that is crafted with a form of wood.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
The total bonuses are composed of the following:
11% minion damage from each piece and 25% from set bonus.
+2 minion capacity from the breastplate, and +1 minion capacity from the helmet and leggings each.
+1 minion capacity from each piece.
+20% increased movement speed from the leggings.

Tips
If combined with the Necromantic Scroll, Hercules Beetle, Summoner Emblem and Papyrus Scarab, the resulting minions will get a total of 113% increased damage.
With an exchange rate of  per unit of Spooky Wood, crafting and selling Spooky Helmets is the most efficient way to convert Spooky Wood into coins.
The Spooky Leggings increase walking movement speed from 15 to 18 mph. However, if the player already has boots equipped (e.g. Hermes Boots), the Leggings' movement speed will not have that noticeable an increase to the player's movement speed.

Trivia
The only difference between male and female sets of Spooky armor seems to be the orange/red markings on the upper breastplate.
Even in terms of defense, the Spooky armor is the most powerful wooden armor.
Spooky armor and Pearlwood armor are the only Hardmode armor crafted at a Work Bench.
The Moon Lord's screen effect causes the Spooky armor to lose its orange/red markings.

History

---

# 文档：Squire armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：squire armor
- 来源：wiki/Squire_armor

Squire armor is a Hardmode armor set purchased from the Tavernkeep after defeating any mechanical boss, for the price of  in total, which consists of the Squire's Great Helm, Squire's Plating, and Squire's Greaves.

A full set of Squire armor provides the following benefits:
+58  defense
+15% melee damage
+30% minion damage
+15%  / +20%  melee critical strike chance
+20% movement speed
+2 sentry slots
+2  / +4  HP/sec.

The set bonus increases the velocity of the Ballista sentry summons and increases their penetration from 4 to 7. It also gives the wearer the Ballista Panic! buff upon taking any damage, which doubles the fire rate of all Ballista sentries summoned by the player for 5 seconds. The total bonus to max number of sentries is +2, allowing 3 to be summoned at once.

The Valhalla Knight armor can be considered a direct upgrade of the Squire armor.

Set
Notes
The Great Helm can be used even in the end game for hybrid builds focused on regeneration and sentries.
This set is an alternative to Spider armor for summoners against mechanical bosses, the Hardmode Dungeon and Plantera. It trades minions for sentries but increases regeneration and provides powerful towers for arenas.

See also
Squire's Shield
Huntress armor
Apprentice armor

# 文档：Stardust armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：stardust armor
- 来源：wiki/Stardust_armor

s.]]

Stardust armor is a post-Moon Lord set of armor that provides summon weapon boosts. It is currently the final summoner set available on the . Crafting the full set requires a total of 36 Luminite Bars (144 Luminite) and 45 Stardust Fragments.

A full set of the Stardust armor will provide the following benefits:
+5 minion capacity
+66% minion damage
+30% whip range
+1 sentry capacity
A unique Stardust Guardian minion will follow the wearer. It does not count against the player's minion capacity, and its function differs across versions:
On the , the Stardust Guardian is passive until an enemy is within a 30-tile radius from the player. It will quickly charge even through blocks towards the enemy, which it then strikes rapidly with a flurry of punches that each deal ~30 summon damage. Once there are no more hostile entities within range, the Stardust Guardian will return to following the player passively.
On the , it is passive until activated by double-tapping the  key, which places it on the nearest block beneath the cursor position. Once placed, the Guardian becomes a sentry-style minion that can chase enemies for very short distances before returning to its original position. It periodically triggers explosions that deal 20 damage and cause enemies in the blast radius to target the Guardian instead of the player. It is deactivated by holding the  key, which causes it to return to following the player passively.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Due to the Stardust armor increasing all summon damage by 66%, including the damage of the Stardust Guardian, its base damage is about 43-47.
On the :
The Stardust Guardian attacks about once every 0.25 seconds and will attack a target endlessly until the target dies or is out of range.
No blocks will halt the Guardian's attack on an enemy, and it will move freely to the enemy to continue the attack if it is within range.
Moving too far from the Stardust Guardian while it is attacking will cause it to return to the player as the enemies are no longer within its range.
On the :
Moving too far from the Stardust Guardian in its sentry position will cause it to return to the player.
The armor set's tooltip vaguely states that it increases minion capacity instead of a precise number. It actually grants 5 extra minions, 1 from Helmet, 2 from Plate and Leggings each.
All armors made from Luminite have no sell value.
The Stardust Guardian can be dyed via the pet slot in the player's equipment menu.
Like all armors made from Luminite, equipping Stardust armor makes the player give off light, in this case white. Unlike other Luminite sets, equipping just one piece of the set gives off the full amount of light.
Light given out from the armor does not stack with Shine Potion, and the brightness is exactly the same as Shine Potion.

Tips
Using Stardust armor and the proper accessories and buffs, it is possible to have 11 minions, not counting the Stardust Guardian.
While it is a potent offensive set, Stardust armor has relatively low defense, and although its ability to attack any enemies that come within a far distance of the player compensates somewhat, the Stardust Guardian does not protect the user against projectiles which leaves them vulnerable. The Valhalla Knight's Breastplate can be substituted into the set to provide much greater survivability, sacrificing the Guardian, and two minion slots. It may be worth it to wear the full Valhalla Knight armor instead of the Stardust armor, as it has much more defense, movement speed, health regeneration, and 3 extra sentries at the cost of 5 minions, the Guardian, and 6% minion damage.
The Stardust Guardian can move through blocks to attack enemies regardless of whether or not it has targeted the player. This can be exploited by walling oneself in, allowing the Guardian to take care of enemies. This method, however, does not work well against enemies that can attack through walls, such as the Cursed Skull.
The Stardust Guardian is affected by whips, including summon tag damage and critical strike chance, and debuffs which take effect when the afflicted target is hit by a minion.

Trivia
Stardust armor has the lowest defense of the four Luminite armors.
The Stardust Guardian was originally created as a reference to Algalon the Observer from the massively multiplayer online role-playing game '. However, due to the community's enthusiasm in comparing the Stardust armor to Star Platinum from the Japanese manga/anime ', which is the third story arc of the  series, lead programmer Yoraiz0r has acknowledged the resemblance and said the armor can be a reference. The similarities have become more apparent with the rework of the Star Guardian's attack style in 1.4.0.1: A barrage attack, referencing "Stand Barrage", an attack used by humanoid Stands (A "spirit" that helps its owner fight), where the stand unleashes a flurry of punches.

See also
Solar Flare armor
Vortex armor
Nebula armor

History
References

# 文档：Tiki armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：tiki armor
- 来源：wiki/Tiki_armor

Tiki armor is a Hardmode post-Plantera armor set purchased from the Witch Doctor for  in total.

A full set of Tiki armor provides the following benefits:

+35 Defense
+4 Minion Slots
+30% Minion Damage
+20% Whip Range

As of 1.4.4.9, Tiki Armor offers +20% whip range and +5 defense compared with Spooky armor's +28% minion damage and +20% movement speed (on  Spooky has one less minion slot).  The extra damage with whip, minions, and sentries makes Spooky generally stronger, but in a fight where the extra distance and defense is more important, Tiki may still play a role.  Spooky's movement boost does not apply to mounts.  Post-Moon Lord  is superior to either.

With the right buffs and accessories, a total of 10 minions can be summoned (see tips section below).

Pieces
Notes
Removing a piece of the armor causes the minion cap to drop, and any minions that were summonable due to its boost will despawn immediately. This can solve issues when a minion failed to be replaced by a newly summoned one, or simply right clicking the buff under the hotbar to despawn.
Both this armor and the Spooky armor can be regarded as the second most powerful summoning armor, only being surpassed by . If there is no increase in minion numbers, Tiki armor increases the damage by 650% (with 5 minions), while Spooky Armor increases the damage by 632% (with 4 minions). If the player does not have at least 1 accessory and/or buff that increases their maximum number of minions, Tiki armor will actually cause minions to do more damage, with the upper hand of more defense. When the player can summon 1 extra minion, Tiki armor only increases it by 780%, while Spooky armor increases minion damage by a further 10%. The more minions that can be summoned, the comparatively more damage minions do with Spooky armor.
The total bonuses are composed of the following:
+10% minion damage from each piece.
+1 minion capacity from each piece and +1 minion capacity from full set bonus.

Tips
A full set of Pygmy minions is able to devastate most Hardmode enemies, but minion movement limitations may require some help from the player, e.g. when fighting against airborne enemies. The Optic Staff or Raven Staff might prove more useful in this situation.
The Tiki armor currently allows achieving the second highest minion boost when equipped along with the Pygmy Necklace, Necromantic Scroll, and Papyrus Scarab, and the Summoning and Bewitched buffs, which push the minion count to 10 (1 from base, 3 from accessories, 2 from buffs, and 4 from armor). The highest minion count is 11 and comes from using the Stardust armor (1 on helmet, 2 on chest, 2 on pants), along with the previously mentioned equipment and buffs. This is not counting the Stardust Guardian, or sentry-style minions, which can increase this number by up to 5 for 16 minions total.
Tiki armor is the most powerful summoner armor set pre-Moon Lord, even in terms of defense, that a player can obtain without a Mythril or Orichalcum anvil.
It can be particularly useful while fishing (or AFK farming) due to the increased amount of minions available for easily dispatching enemies.
It is often a good idea to use Spooky armor to first summon the minions (for the increased damage), before switching to Tiki armor to gain the bonus minion.

History

Tin armor is the Tin counterpart of Copper armor, and is also one of the most simply craftable armor sets in the game.

It takes a total of  48 /  60 Tin Bars to craft a full suit, or  144 /  180 Tin Ore.

A full set grants a basic 5 defense plus a set bonus of 2 defense, which equals a total of 7 defense. That means a full set of tin armor grants 1 more defense than Copper armor's 6 defense.

The set consists of the Tin Helmet, Tin Chainmail and Tin Greaves.

Crafting

# 文档：Titan armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：titan armor
- 来源：wiki/Titan_armor

Titan armor is a Hardmode ranged armor set consisting of the Titan Helmet, the Titan Mail and the Titan Leggings. Each separate piece of Titan armor has a  chance of being dropped by Ocram, or can otherwise be crafted.

A full set of Titan armor provides the following benefits:
+45 defense
+30% ranged damage
+20% ranged critical strike chance
+10% movement speed
+48% ammo conservation

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Only the armor's reduced ammo-consumption bonuses are only additive. All others' are multiplicative. This means that, when pairing a full set of Titan armor (5% + 5% + 10% + 28%) with the Ammo Box buff (20%) and a Megashark (50%), there is a 79.2% (  ) chance to not consume ammo.
Titan armor pairs well with the Vulcan Repeater and Megashark due to its reduced ammo-consumption set bonus which grants an extra 28% chance not to consume ammo.
Excluding Vortex armor, the Titan armor set is superior to any  ranged armors in terms of saving ammunition, but does not add an effect like Shroomite armor's invisibility. In addition, Titan armor has lower defense.
Wearing Titan Leggings will cause the player to emit a pulsating aura, even if the rest of the set is not worn.
Despite Titan armor being so costly to craft, it has nowhere near the highest defense available.

Trivia
In Greek Mythology, Titans are the second generation of divine beings, and the ancestors of the Olympian gods.

See also
, the melee exclusive armor.
, the magic exclusive armor.

# 文档：Titanium armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：titanium armor
- 来源：wiki/Titanium_armor

The Titanium armor set is an armor set crafted from Titanium Bars, and is the alternative to Adamantite armor. Like many Hardmode armors, it has three different headgear/helmet options, allowing the player to focus on magical, melee, or ranged combat. Crafting a one-helmet set requires 59 Titanium Bars, made from  236 /  295 Titanium Ore. Crafting all five pieces requires 85 Titanium Bars or  340 /  425 Titanium Ore.

When the full set is equipped, striking an enemy with a melee, ranged, or magic weapon, or having an enemy be hit by a minion or sentry, will grant the Titanium Barrier buff, in which titanium shards spawn that will rotate around the player and damage enemies who come in contact with them. There can be a total of 7 shards spinning around the player, each dealing over 50 damage with high knockback, incapable of achieving critical strikes, and lasting 10 seconds, refreshing when the player deals damage.

When the full set is equipped, striking an enemy with a melee, ranged, or magic weapon, or having an enemy be hit by a minion or sentry, will grant the Shadow Dodge buff, which allows the player to dodge the next attack dealt by an enemy. It has a cooldown of 30  / 20  seconds.

Set
Helmet variants
Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Notes
Most dyes do not affect the color of this armor much, with a few exceptions like Intense Rainbow Dye. This may be in reference to how costly titanium is to dye in real life.
If you teleport while the Titanium Barrier buff is active, the shards will move to the position of the player quickly instead of teleporting.
On the , Dragon armor, Spectral armor, and Titan armor can be crafted with Titanium armor pieces. However the , effectively being equivalent to Console 1.02, offers no such substitution.
A Lightning Aura sentry can be used to continuously refresh the Titanium Barrier set bonus, though Target Dummies cannot be exploited for this purpose. Traps will not trigger the set bonus at all.

Tips
If the player changes their mind, the helmet piece can be shimmered and crafted into an alternative.

History

# 文档：Turtle armor

- 类别：armor
- 标识：turtle armor
- 来源：wiki/Turtle_armor

Turtle armor is a Hardmode armor set available after defeating all three mechanical bosses. The set consists of the Turtle Helmet, Turtle Scale Mail, and Turtle Leggings. A full set grants a vastly amplified Thorns effect, which reflects  the full /  twice the full damage from melee attacks onto the attacking enemy.

The full set of armor also provides the following:

14% increased melee damage,
12% increased melee critical strike chance,
+65 defense
+750 aggro, and
15% damage reduction.

With no vanity items covering the turtle armor, a turtle shell appears on the player's back (much like the Leaf Blower, Flamethrower, Elf Melter, and Heat Ray backpacks), if the full set is equipped.

Crafting this set requires a total of 54 Chlorophyte Bars ( 270 /  324 Chlorophyte Ore) and 3 Turtle Shells.

Set
Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Notes
Though it provides considerable defense, Turtle armor's bonuses to melee damage and critical strike chance are barely higher than those of Cobalt armor and Molten armor.
If one intends to craft both chest pieces of the Beetle armor, a total of 78 Chlorophyte Bars (390 Chlorophyte Ore) and 4 Turtle Shells are required to craft the whole Beetle armor set.
The set's Thorns effect cannot be improved by the Thorns Potion buff nor the  buff. It deals twice as much damage as a Thorns Potion but is otherwise identical.
The reflected damage is decreased by banners (25% in Classic or lower, 50% in Expert or above), rerolled inversely by luck (having negative luck will give a chance to increase incoming damage, and thorns as a result) and capped at 1000 damage.
Each armor piece of this set increases aggro by 250 points.

Tips
The passive Thorns effect usually allows players to ignore weak enemies that rely on contact damage, especially when combined with a shield for knockback immunity.
However, the Thorns effect deals negligible damage compared to most Hardmode weapons, causing it to be relatively worthless for boss fights. The effect also does not activate when players are hit by lasers or other projectiles that bosses tend to use.
Turtle armor works well as a support set for multiplayer since it draws aggro from enemies and bosses. It pairs well with the Paladin's Shield and both of its upgraded versions, increasing the survivability of the player's teammates as well as their own.
Unlike the Helmet and Leggings, Turtle Scale Mail has relatively decent offensive stats. It can be combined with a Chlorophyte Mask or Hallowed Mask and Squire's Greaves for a much stronger offensive melee set, though it lacks the 15% damage reduction and Thorns effect of full Turtle armor.
As of current versions, it is no longer possible to exploit this armor's Thorns effect to quickly defeat the Dungeon Guardian, as the Guardian's attack has been reduced to 1000 while its health remains at 9999 and the Thorns effect now deals 1 damage like all other attacks. Upgrading to Beetle armor, however, will allow the player to survive one hit while using a Lifeforce Potion and Endurance Potion. You also aren't able to use this exploit against the Empress of Light, despite her average defense and heavy damage.
On the , the Turtle armor can be seen as the Hardmode counterpart of the Cactus armor.

History

---

# 文档：Ultrabright Helmet

- 类别：armor
- 标识：ultrabright helmet
- 来源：wiki/Ultrabright_Helmet

The Ultrabright Helmet is an armor piece for the helmet slot that combines the functionalities of the Mining Helmet and the Night Vision Helmet.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
The Ultrabright Helmet can replace the Mining Helmet in the Mining armor while still triggering its set bonus.
It sells for more than the items used to craft it, if both were drops.
The Shine Potion stacks with the Ultrabright Helmet, but the Night Owl Potion does not stack.
The Ultrabright Helmet is one of the very few items (the only item?) that provides visibility in complete darkness, even when equipped in the Vanity slot, even without using any Dye.

Trivia
The Ultrabright Helmet is the only item in the game that uses the term "Ultrabright" that cannot be crafted from Ultrabright Torches.
