# 文档：Potions

- 类别：potions
- 标识：potions
- 来源：wiki/Potions

Potions are consumable items that either recover the user's health and/or mana either instantaneously or over time, or grant temporary buffs or debuffs to the user. Potions are commonly found in natural chests and Pots, can be dropped by enemies, purchased from NPCs, or crafted. Most potions can be crafted at a Placed Bottle or Alchemy Table, but flasks must be crafted at an Imbuing Station.

Potion buffs can be canceled at any time by right-clicking the buff icon (), by selecting the buff icon and canceling it in the equipment menu (), by selecting the buff with  + / then canceling the buff with  (), by double-tapping the buff icon (), or by canceling the buff from the buffs screen (). All potion buffs (including food buffs) are lost when dying, or when leaving and rejoining the world (as of 1.4.4, flasks stay).

In Journey Mode, all Potions require 20 research to unlock duplication, but 30 research is needed for recovery potions.



Recovery potions
These potions restore health, mana, or even both at the same time. They are limited in use by the Potion Sickness and Mana Sickness debuffs, respectively. The Potion Sickness debuff duration can be reduced with a Charm of Myths or a Philosopher's Stone.

Food and drink potions
Consuming foods will grant a Well Fed, Plenty Satisfied, or Exquisitely Stuffed buff, while drinks will grant the Tipsy effect.

Buff potions
These grant the character a temporary, usually short-lived buff when consumed. They are crafted at a Placed Bottle or an Alchemy Table.  Pressing the buff key ( by default) will consume one of each of the player's buff potions in their inventory.

Flasks
These grant the character a temporary melee and whips-specific buff when consumed. Only one flask buff can be active at a time. Crafted at an Imbuing Station.

Other potions
These are potions that do not fit in the other categories.

Trivia
Certain potions' sprites use bottles that correlate with real-life . These include:
All of the Lesser potions, using an .
The Strange Brew, using a .
The Ironskin, Regeneration, Swiftness, and Gills Potions all use s.
The in-game flasks are slight modifications of the  with custom stoppers.
All other potions appear to be variations of s or s.
All potions (excluding the Recovery potions) use a glass stopper.
The sprite for the Wiesnbräu is a reference to a popular drinking glass, and has been seen in the 2006 American comedy film .
On , if the player quickly uses a potion right after they leave their inventory, the potion will be used, but not consumed. However, the buff/debuff from it will only last 1 minute. Note that this might not work every time.
Some potions can only be crafted in worlds with the proper evil biome:
Note that since 1.4.0.1 update, you can place a Dryad in graveyard biome and get opposite evil's seeds, so all of the following potions are available to craft on every world.
Thorns and Wrath potions can only be crafted in worlds with Corruption ingredients.
Heartreach and Rage potions can only be crafted in worlds with Crimson ingredients.
Both the Heartreach and Thorns Potions can be found in Chests, regardless of world type. Additionally Heartreach potions can be found in Crates.  However, this represents a limited (non-renewable) supply for Thorn Potions.
Of these four potions, only the Heartreach potion can be obtained from Crates.

History

---

# 文档：Potions/vi

- 类别：potions
- 标识：potions/vi
- 来源：wiki/Potions/vi

Thuốc là những vật phẩm tiêu hao có thể phục hồi máu và/hoặc mana/vi hoặc cho một số buffs tạm thời. Thuốc thường được tìm thấy trong tự nhiên như rương và Bình và có thể rớt ra từ Kẻ địch, mua từ các NPCs, hoặc tự chế tạo. Hầu như tất cả mọi loại thuốc đều có thể được chế tạo tại Placed Bottle hoặc Alchemy Table, nhưng 'bình thuốc' chỉ có thể được chế tạo tại Imbuing Station. Hiệu ứng của thuốc có thể bị hủy bất cứ lúc nào bằng cách nhấp chuột phải vào biểu tượng buff (), bằng cách chọn biểu tượng buff và hủy nó trong menu trang bị (), bằng cách nhấn đúp vào biểu tượng buff (), hoặc bằng cách hủy buff ra khỏi màn hình buff (). Tất cả mọi loại thuốc yêu cầu 20 để mở khóa chức năng nhân bản, nhưng 30 là cần thiết cho bình phục hồi.



Thuốc phục hồi
Những loại thuốc này sẽ hồi máu và mana/vi, hoặc thậm chí là cả hai. Khi dùng chúng sẽ gây ra Potion Sickness/vi hoặc Mana Sickness/vi debuffs với những loại tương ứng để giới hạn số lần sử dụng của người chơi. Thời gian debuff của Potion Sickness có thể giảm xuống bởi Charm of Myths/vi hoặc Philosopher's Stone/vi.

Thức ăn và đồ uống
Tiêu thụ thực phẩm sẽ nhận được các hiệu ứng như Well Fed/vi, Plenty Satisfied/vi, hoặc Exquisitely Stuffed/vi, trong khi đồ uống sẽ cho hiệu ứng Tipsy/vi.

Buff potions/vi
Những thứ này cấp cho người chơi một số buff tạm thời, thường nhận được buff trong khoảng thời gian sau khi sử dụng. Chúng được chế tạo tại Placed Bottle/vi hoặc Alchemy Table/vi.  Nhấn phím buff ( theo mặc định) sẽ dùng tất cả các bình thuốc hỗ trợ của người chơi trong kho của họ.

Flasks/vi
Những loại này cho người chơi buff cận chiến tạm thời sau khi dùng. Mỗi lần chỉ có thể dùng một flask buff được kích hoạt. Chế tạo tại Imbuing Station/vi.

Các loại thuốc khác
Các lọai thuốc này không thuộc bất kì trong các loại vừa liệt kê trước đó.

Mẹo
Certain potions' sprites use bottles that correlate with real-life . These include:
All of the Lesser potions, using an .
The Strange Brew, using a .
The Ironskin, Regeneration, Swiftness, and Gills Potions all use s.
The in-game flasks are slight modifications of the s with special stoppers.
All other potions seem to be extreme modifications of the  or Erlenmeyer flask.
All potions (excluding the Recovery potions) use a glass stopper.
The sprite for the Wiesnbräu is a reference to a popular drinking glass, and has been seen in the 2006 American comedy film .
On , if the player quickly uses a potion right after they leave their inventory, the potion will be used, but not consumed. However, the buff/debuff from it will only last 1 minute. Note that this might not work every time.
Some potions can only be crafted in worlds with the proper evil biome:
Thorns and Wrath potions can only be crafted in worlds with Corruption.
Heartreach and Rage potions can only be crafted in worlds with Crimson.
Both the Heartreach and Thorns Potions can be found in Chests, regardless of world type. Additionally Heartreach potions can be found in Crates.  However, this represents a limited (non-renewable) supply for Thorn Potions.
Of these four potions, only the Heartreach potion can be obtained from Crates.
Note that since 1.4.0.1 update, you can place a Dryad in graveyard biome and get opposite evil's seeds, so all of these potions are available to craft on every world.

Lịch sử

The Builder Potion is a buff potion which grants the Builder buff upon consumption. The buff increases tile and wall placement speed by 25% and extends placement range by one tile.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
The increase in tile/wall placement speed additively stacks with the bonus from either the Brick Layer/Portable Cement Mixer or the Architect Gizmo Pack for a total increase of 75%, respectively.
The Builder buff's placement range increase stacks with the effects of both the Toolbelt and Toolbox for a total increase of 3 tiles.

# 文档：Burger

- 类别：potions
- 标识：burger
- 来源：wiki/Burger

The Burger is a food item with a  chance to be dropped by Crimeras and Eaters of Souls which gives the Exquisitely Stuffed buff.

Tips
This is one of the few food items in the game that provide the Exquisitely Stuffed buff.
If farming for the Burger, it is advisable to farm it on a Pre-Hardmode Surface Corruption world, since Surface Corruption has much less enemy variety than Crimson, making it easier to farm Burgers.

Trivia
The Burger's tooltip "...but wait! It was 99 cents." is a lyric from the song "Thrift Shop" by American hip hop duo .
Burgers dropping from Crimeras and Eaters of Souls may be a reference to this conversation in the webcomic Homestuck, in which Rose and Dave discuss hamburgers being related to horrorterrors, which are physically similar to both Crimeras and Eaters of Souls.

History

# 文档：Calming Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：calming potion
- 来源：wiki/Calming_Potion

The Calming Potion is a buff potion which grants the Calm buff when consumed. While under its effects, the enemy spawn rate is reduced by 17% and the max amount of enemies on screen is reduced by 20%.

This potion does not reduce enemy spawn rates during the Pumpkin Moon, Frost Moon, or Frost Legion events.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
The Calming Potion buff will stack with the buff provided by Peace Candles and the Happy! buff from Sunflowers, further reducing enemy spawn rates.
The Battle Potion has the opposite effect.

Tips
If the player wants to loot the chests in the Dungeon and already has a sufficient number of Golden Keys, using this potion before entering may be a good idea.
While not directly helpful against bosses themselves, it can be useful to use against some like the Queen Bee or the Wall of Flesh whose biomes typically have numerous enemy spawns which could make the fight harder, or when mining Meteorite to curb Meteor Head spawns.

History

is traditionally served for dinner on Christmas. It originated in England.

# 文档：Cooked Marshmallow

- 类别：potions
- 标识：cooked marshmallow
- 来源：wiki/Cooked_Marshmallow

A Cooked Marshmallow is a food item that grants the Well Fed buff.  The Cooked Marshmallow is created by holding a Marshmallow on a Stick over a Campfire for 15 real-world seconds (15 in-game minutes). The Marshmallow on a Stick must be held by the player, either by attaching it to the cursor in the inventory or selecting it on the hotbar, for the entire 15 seconds over the campfire in order for it to be cooked. Selecting a different item in the hotbar or simply moving away from the campfire resets the timer.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
As Marshmallows on Sticks do not stack, this food item is a bit difficult to mass-produce. Still, one may do this by filling their inventory, leaving one slot with a Cooked Marshmallow and one Marshmallow on a Stick on the hotbar. Put the Marshmallows on Sticks on the ground, select the one on the hotbar, and stand at the fire. When the held Marshmallow is cooked, it stacks in the inventory, and a Marshmallow on a Stick on the ground is picked up. 29 Cooked Marshmallows can be created at one time this way, since they stack to 30.
The amount of time of cooking the Cooked Marshmallow will not change if the time is sped up in the Journey Mode settings.

Trivia
It is the only food item that grants the Well Fed buff for the same duration on all platforms.
The tooltip is a play on "S'mores", a popular food item that requires a cooked marshmallow. It is also a reference to the movie .

History

---

# 文档：Damage Booster

- 类别：potions
- 标识：damage booster
- 来源：wiki/Damage_Booster

A Damage Booster is one of the three power-ups occasionally dropped when striking an enemy while wearing a full set of Nebula armor. When picked up, it grants the Damage Nebula buff which immediately provides the player with a 15% damage boost for 8 seconds. When a Damage Booster is picked up while the Damage Nebula buff is already in effect, it stacks with the previous Damage Nebula, for up to 3 stacked boosts. Every 8 seconds, one stacked Damage Booster expires, and the Damage Nebula buff decreases by one level.

Damage Boosters, like all Nebula boosters, float in the air and have a pickup range of approximately five tiles. They cannot legitimately appear in a player's inventory.

Notes
At three stacks, the player's damage will be increased by 45%.
In multiplayer, any player can pick up Damage Boosters and benefit from the Damage Nebula buff, even if they are not wearing Nebula armor. Additionally, picking up a Damage Booster will grant all nearby players (within a 43.75-tile radius) one instance of Damage Nebula. If the player acquiring the Damage Booster is on a team, only nearby players within that team will obtain the buff.

Trivia
If a Damage Booster is placed into the player's inventory using an inventory editor, it will appear as its frames of animation instead of being animated.

History

The Eggnog is a type of healing potion dropped from Presents obtained during the Christmas event, with a chance of 8.101% in pre-Hardmode and 7.561% in Hardmode. It heals 80 health points. Because it can be potentially found so early on, Eggnog can be used as a viable alternative to Lesser Healing Potions. These, along with a bunch of Star Anises, are great when fighting the Eye of Cthulhu.

are a type of flowering plant that are native to North America.

# 文档：Endurance Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：endurance potion
- 来源：wiki/Endurance_Potion

The Endurance Potion is a buff potion which grants the Endurance buff when consumed. The buff reduces taken damage by 10%.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Endurance Potion, Beetle armor at its 45% state and Worm Scarf (17%) will reduce damage by about 60%

Frozen Turtle Shell (25%) will further increase it to 74%.

Tips
This potion works well with the Ironskin Potion, reducing a good deal of damage.
The Endurance Potion is more effective than the Ironskin Potion against attacks greater than 40 damage (60 damage in Expert mode, and 80 damage in Master mode), however, it is even better to use both at the same time.

# 文档：Featherfall Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：featherfall potion
- 来源：wiki/Featherfall_Potion

The Featherfall Potion is a buff potion which grants the Featherfall buff when consumed. The buff reduces the player's fall speed and gravity by 1/3, allowing them to jump higher than normal.

The player can hold the  key to cancel the effects or the  key to further reduce fall speed and gravity to 90%. While under the effects of both Featherfall Potion and Gravitation Potion, holding the  key will make the player fall up at normal speed. The player will fall up at a slowed speed when holding no direction.

The effects of the Featherfall Potion can also be used to jump higher. By holding , grappling a platform above you, and releasing the grapple before it is complete, one may achieve jumping heights of several hundred blocks.

Fall damage is also negated, even when falling normally. It is worth noting that the Featherfall Potion also increases the distance players are knocked back when hit by an attack.

It is a fairly useful potion in Pre-Hardmode, as it gives the player a high amount of aerial maneuverability and control, similar to that of wings. This makes it a significantly easier to fight bosses, giving the player a significant advantage against them.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
This potion and the Djinn's Curse are the only items that can prevent fall damage while the Stoned debuff is active.

History

---

# 文档：Flask of Cursed Flames

- 类别：potions
- 标识：flask of cursed flames
- 来源：wiki/Flask_of_Cursed_Flames

The Flask of Cursed Flames is a Hardmode flask that, when applied, causes melee weapons (and Whips) to inflict the Cursed Inferno debuff for 3–6 seconds on a successful hit. Melee weapons that fire projectiles will also inflict the debuff on enemies hit by the projectiles.

The Flask of Cursed Flames cannot be used in conjunction with other flasks, and using it will remove any existing flask effects. However, like other flasks, the effect will last across death.

The Flask of Cursed Flames will not inflict the Cursed Flames debuff in PvP scenarios.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
The Flask of Cursed Flames can prove to be very useful when mining, as it gives more light than torches, and both pickaxes and drills are affected by the buff.

History

---

# 文档：Flask of Fire

- 类别：potions
- 标识：flask of fire
- 来源：wiki/Flask_of_Fire

The Flask of Fire is a flask that will cause any melee weapons (and ) to inflict the On Fire! debuff. On the , this effect will not be lost when the player dies.

The Flask of Fire cannot be used in conjunction with other flasks, and using it will remove any existing flask effects.

This item currently does not apply the debuff in PvP scenarios. The debuff lasts for 3–6 seconds.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
The Flask of Fire is a cheap way of providing light when exploring underground in early Hardmode. While the Hellstone cost may be high, the buff lasts for quite a long time and persists after death.

Trivia
It was previously the only non-renewable flask currently in the game, since Hellstone was generated in a finite amount per world and could not be fished from crates, but 1.4.5 changed this to allow Hellstone to be obtained from Obsidian Crates.

History

---

# 文档：Flask of Gold

- 类别：potions
- 标识：flask of gold
- 来源：wiki/Flask_of_Gold

The Flask of Gold is a Hardmode flask that increases the number of coins dropped by enemies. While under the influence of a Flask of Gold, melee (and Whip) attacks will inflict enemies with Midas, causing them to drop 10%-50% more coins.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
The Flask of Gold also makes your pickaxe and melee weapons provide light, making it a sidegrade to the Shine Potion, since it also improves money drops but requires constant use of your weapons or tools.
The Lucky Coin and its upgrades operate independently, and can make hunting and events even more profitable.
All enemies and bosses are affected by any melee weapon, including "ranged" weapons such as Boomerangs -- As long as it deals "Melee" damage ( or as long as it is a Whip), it will be applied to it no matter what the attack is. One can tell which ones will apply the effect by the glowing golden sparkles the weapons/projectiles will emit upon use.
When using the Flairon, only the Flail applies the buff, but not the bubbles.
The total cost of buying the Gold Dust is . So, even with the lowest benefit possible (10%), you will get a return on investment after earning just  . Killing a Mimic, the Queen Bee, or any hardmode boss will do, but mass-killing pre-Hardmode bosses may be very profitable, and the Solar Eclipse is ideal.
Note that in Expert Mode bosses drop their money inside Treasure Bags, which are not affected by Midas.

See also
Golden Bullet
Lucky Coin

History

---

# 文档：Flask of Ichor

- 类别：potions
- 标识：flask of ichor
- 来源：wiki/Flask_of_Ichor

The Flask of Ichor is a Hardmode flask crafted at an Imbuing Station. While under the influence of a Flask of Ichor, melee  and whip attacks will inflict the Ichor Debuff for 10 to 19 seconds, decreasing the defense of enemies by  15 /  20, therefore increasing the damage of the player's weapon by  7-8 /  10.

It will make the weapon glow when swung. If used with yoyos, it can be a good lighting source in dark areas.

This item does not apply the debuff in PvP scenarios.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
The Flask of Ichor offers the greatest DPS increase of any flask as long as the player deals damage at least  4 /  0.6 times per second.
Ichor stacks with and can be used alongside Betsy's Wrath for a huge reduction of defense against the vast majority of enemies and bosses, as very few enemies are immune to Ichor or the Betsy's Curse debuff. The combination leaves most enemies and bosses entirely without defense.

History

---

It currently doesn't provide any effect in PvP scenarios.

Effective at controlling large crowds of enemies, and can reduce the difficulty of the post-Plantera Dungeon and the Jungle Temple.

- 类别：potions
- 标识：flask of party
- 来源：wiki/Flask_of_Party

The Flask of Party is one of the eight flasks that provide melee (and summoning) "bonuses" for the consumer. This particular flask provides a buff that creates confetti when the player attacks with their melee weapon or whip.  The confetti created is purely cosmetic and does not provide damage bonuses.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Besides crafting, there is a chance to obtain a Flask of Party from breaking a , which can be bought during a Party from the Party Girl.
In  worlds, the Flask of Party can be purchased from the Princess for .

Tips
Since flasks apply to all weapons that deal melee damage, it is possible to create large amounts of confetti using this flask with weapons such as Vampire Knives, Light Discs, Bladed Glove or the Scourge of the Corruptor.

Trivia
The Flask of Party produces significantly less confetti on the .
Using a drill while under the buff will make the player appear to "shoot" confetti.

# 文档：Flipper Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：flipper potion
- 来源：wiki/Flipper_Potion

The Flipper Potion is a buff potion which grants the Flipper buff when consumed. The buff allows the player to move horizontally and vertically through all three liquids (water, lava, and honey) without slowing down, and also allows the player to swim without any swimming accessory equipped. It also nullifies the Inner Tube's effects; causing the player to sink.

.

The fact that the Sand Poacher may drop a Fried Egg upon death could be a play on words, as one way to cook eggs is to "poach" them.

# 文档：Fries

- 类别：potions
- 标识：fries
- 来源：wiki/Fries

Fries are a food item dropped by Flying Fish which grants the player the Plenty Satisfied buff.

Trivia
Its tooltip, "Where's the Ketchup!?" references the American tradition of eating fries with ketchup. It may also reference "Where's the Lamb Sauce?!", a famous quote from celebrity chef Gordon Ramsay.
The icon is inspired by  Fries. The yellow 'T' on the container is a reference to McDonalds' logo being a yellow 'M', which is the first letter of the company's name, and T being the first letter in 'Terraria'.
The fact that the item drops from Flying Fish may reference the meal fish and chips.

History

# 文档：Gender Change Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：gender change potion
- 来源：wiki/Gender_Change_Potion

The Gender Change Potion is a potion which allows the player to change their sprite from male to female and vice versa. Hurt sounds and equipment sprites change accordingly. The effect is permanent, lasting infinitely unless another Gender Change Potion is used to change gender again.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Although some NPC dialogue lines do change depending on the player's gender, like the Stylist's dialogue, gender otherwise has no significant impact on game mechanics or gameplay, and as such the effects of this potion are purely cosmetic.
On use, a helix of relatively long-lasting blue and pink particles will surround the player.

Tips
Due to the variety of crafting ingredients required, it may be easier and more practical for an early-game player to simply create a new character with the other gender instead. The potion is primarily useful for later-game characters, for when it would be more efficient to gather all of the herbs than to make a different character which needs new Life Crystals, Mana Crystals, Life Fruits, and/or a Demon Heart.
Gathering all of the ingredients can be made a trivial task with a herb farm.

Trivia
The Gender Change Potion is the only potion which requires all seven herbs to craft.

History

---

# 文档：Gills Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：gills potion
- 来源：wiki/Gills_Potion

The Gills Potion is a buff potion which grants the Gills buff when consumed. The buff allows the player to breathe underwater, preventing them from drowning.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
The Gills Potion will allow the player to survive while wearing a Fish Bowl.
The player cannot drown in lava, thus negating this potion's usefulness for that purpose.

Tips
For swimming in lava, the player should use Obsidian Skin Potions instead of Gills Potions.
Despite the tooltip states that player can "breathe water instead of air", they can still breathe air. This is because prior to 1.2, players could not breathe in the air if they used a Gills Potion. As such, this tooltip appears to be outdated.
This still, however, happens in a For the Worthy world.

Trivia
It has the same recipe as the Sonar Potion.

History

---

# 文档：Gingerbread Cookie

- 类别：potions
- 标识：gingerbread cookie
- 来源：wiki/Gingerbread_Cookie

The Gingerbread Cookie is a food item dropped from Presents obtained during the Christmas event, with a chance of 3.601% in pre-Hardmode and 3.361% in Hardmode. When consumed, it provides the Exquisitely Stuffed buff for 4 minutes  / Well Fed buff for 30 minutes  / Well Fed buff for 15 minutes .

Trivia
Its tooltip is a reference to the movies  & , in which the Gingerbread man says the same line.

History

---

# 文档：Golden Delight

- 类别：potions
- 标识：golden delight
- 来源：wiki/Golden_Delight

The Golden Delight is a consumable potion crafted at the Cooking Pot which gives the Exquisitely Stuffed buff.

It is the most potent Food/drink potion in the game, granting the longest lasting of the highest tier Well Fed buffs.

Crafting
Recipes
Achievement
Tips
In Journey Mode, Golden Delight is easily obtained if one researches Cans Of Worms which only require two cans to be researched. By duplicating Cans of Worms, one will effectively have an endless source of Gold Worms to spare.
Golden Delight only requires one of any Golden Critter to craft. However, given their overall rarity, it may not be preferable as there are other food items (examples include Steak/Bacon/BBQ Ribs which all last 24 minutes) with the same effects at a reduced duration that are arguably easier to find.

Trivia
The tooltip '(Au)some!' is a pun on the interjection "Awesome!" and the chemical symbol for gold, Au.
The Golden Delight has the longest buff duration of any food item.

History

# 文档：Grapes

- 类别：potions
- 标识：grapes
- 来源：wiki/Grapes

Grapes are a Hardmode food item that grant the Plenty Satisfied buff.

They have a  chance to drop from Derplings and Giant Flying Foxes.

Crafting
Tips
It is advised to collect 2 grapes and craft the Grape Juice, as it gives the best variation of the Well Fed buff and is one of the easiest ones to get.

Notes
Despite real-world grapes being clearly a fruit, Grapes in Terraria do not fit into the Fruits category. They cannot be used to craft Fruit Juice or Fruit Salad, are not dropped from Trees, and both they and their juice give far better buffs than the members of that category. Also, researching Grapes requires 10 of them, unlike all the other fruits that require only 5.

Trivia
Its tooltip is a reference to the 1939 novel "" by John Steinbeck.
The item dropping from Giant Flying Foxes is likely a reference to the fable of "The Fox and the Grapes", in which a fox attempts to reach some Grapes on a vine, but ultimately decides that they are undesirable.
In the real world, s originated from the Mediterranean and Central Asia and are a major ingredient in wine.

History

---

# 文档：Gravitation Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：gravitation potion
- 来源：wiki/Gravitation_Potion

The Gravitation Potion is a buff potion which grants the Gravitation buff when consumed. This buff allows the player to invert gravity with  making the screen appear upside-down, fall upwards, and walk on top of the ceilings. The effect can be toggled while in midair.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
While flipping gravity resets the player's fall distance, this potion does not negate fall damage on its own. Using a Featherfall Potion, wings, a Lucky Horseshoe, an Obsidian Horseshoe or any Horseshoe Balloon will make using Gravitation Potions a lot safer.
To negate fall damage using the Gravitation Potion alone, the player can flip gravity rapidly to cancel their momentum before landing.
Falling upwards to the map's height limit will not damage the player, but it is possible to die by smacking into the underside of a Floating Island, or any other ceiling. However, in the "dontdigup" or "getfixedboi" world seeds, falling into the sky will instantly kill the player.
This is less of an issue in , as Cloud Blocks prevents fall damage.
Falling to a map's height limit will automatically flip the player right-side up.
In the , there is the option to turn off "World Flips Upside Down", making it so only the player flips.
While flipping gravity on  and using the Hoverboard's horizontal speed boost while having Hermes Boots or any equal or upgraded version equipped will cause the boots speed boost to allow the user to run forever. If using the Hoverboard's fixed hover mode then the user will stay in place forever.
Fishing is possible on reverse gravity; the fishing bobber will fall "upward" into the water. However, golf cannot be done on reverse gravity; the player will not even be able to swing the club.
Players falling upwards will not be stopped by most platforms, and will instead fall through them. Hammering a platform three times will allow the player to "land" on it upside down, but this usually does not reset the flight time.

Tips
It is helpful for finding Floating Islands by hovering up and down at the right altitude.
Gravitation effectively surpasses the combination of Rocket Boots and wings as the air travel method with the most range, though it is more difficult to control as a flight method.
It can be used during the Wall of Flesh fight to walk on the roof of the Underworld, avoiding the lava pools, abandoned houses, elevation changes, and Lava Slimes in Expert Mode.

Trivia
Walking effects, such as the snowy effect when wearing a full set of Frost armor, will not be flipped.
The sound effect of the player flipping gravity has the same sound effect of the Shadow Orb (item), Vilethorn, as well as the teleports of a Fire Imp.

See also
Gravity Globe

History

---

Used in

# 文档：Greater Mana Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：greater mana potion
- 来源：wiki/Greater_Mana_Potion

The Greater Mana Potion is a potion that restores 200 mana upon use and is third to the Super Mana Potion and Strange Brew, respectively, in potency of mana restoration items. Unlike most potions, Greater Mana Potions cannot be crafted. Instead, they can be purchased from the Wizard for   /  , or dropped by . On the , they can additionally be obtained from Golden Crates, but on the , they cannot be obtained outside of Hardmode.

Equipping a Mana Flower lets a player consume any type of mana potion automatically. Consuming any mana-restoring potion will enact the Mana Sickness debuff, decreasing magic damage for a short duration.

Crafting
Used in
Notes
Greater Mana Potions have a maximum stack of , which is the same as Mana Potions and 1.5 times the max of Lesser Mana Potions. It would cost  to purchase a full stack from the Wizard.
Using the Greater Mana Potion gives the player a unit price of   /   per 1 mana.

Trivia
Even though the Mana Potion is used to craft the Mana Flower, the Mana Flower's sprite shows both the shape and two yellow bands of the Greater Mana Potion.
The Greater Mana Potion is identical in name and fairly similar in appearance to Mana Potions in the action role-playing hack-and-slash computer video game Diablo 2.

History

In addition to being crafted, Healing Potions can be found naturally in pots after Hardmode activation and pots in the Underworld before Hardmode. Several are also dropped by the Wall of Flesh, Deerclops and Skeletron.

It is the second tier of healing potions, the first being the Lesser Healing Potion, the third being the Greater Healing Potion and the fourth being the Super Healing Potion.

Used in

# 文档：Heartreach Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：heartreach potion
- 来源：wiki/Heartreach_Potion

The Heartreach Potion is a buff potion which grants the Heartreach buff when consumed. The buff increases the pickup range of life recovery Hearts by 15.625 tiles, from 2.625 tiles to a total of 18.25 tiles.

This potion can only be crafted with access to a Crimson biome due to the Crimson Tigerfish requirement, but can be also be obtained as a random reward from fished crates in any world. Wooden Crates drop buff potions at a rate of 1 in 7, Iron at 1 in 4, and Golden at 1 in 3.

Crafting
Recipes
Trivia
Like the Rage Potion to the Wrath Potion, the Heartreach Potion may be seen as a counterpart to the Thorns Potion, as both give defensive buffs with imagery which references their respective biomes.

History

# 文档：Honeyfin

- 类别：potions
- 标识：honeyfin
- 来源：wiki/Honeyfin

The Honeyfin is a consumable fish that can be fished from honey found in Bee Hives within the Underground Jungle. Other than the Bumblebee Tuna found during Angler quests, Honeyfins are the only fish that can be caught in honey. They are used to craft Seafood Dinners.

Crafting
Used in
Tips
Honeyfins are the most effective healing potions that can be obtained without entering Hardmode. It can be worth spending some time fishing to build up a supply of them.
On the , Greater Healing Potions, which restore more health than Honeyfins, have a chance of being obtained from Golden Crates, also obtained by fishing, without entering Hardmode.
Because they are the only non-quest fish obtained from fishing in honey, they can be an excellent way of stocking up on Seafood Dinners.

Trivia
The Honeyfin is one of three fished consumables, the other two being the Frost Daggerfish and the Bomb Fish.

History

---

# 文档：Horn o' plenty

- 类别：potions
- 标识：horn o plenty
- 来源：wiki/Horn_o'_plenty

The Horn o' plenty is a Healing item that is never consumed when used. Using it will still cause Potion Sickness, so pauses are required between uses. It is always dropped by Turkor the Ungrateful.

Tips
Because the Horn o' plenty heals only 30 less health than the Greater Healing Potion, which can be difficult to acquire, it remains very useful throughout the entire game.
The Horn o' plenty's limitless healing supply renders all weaker healing potions (Lesser and Standard) obsolete, allowing them to be sold freely. It is still useful to retain a stash of Greater Healing Potions for situations where more potent healing is necessary.

Trivia
In real life, a Horn of plenty, or , is a mythical symbol of abundance and nourishment commonly associated with the Thanksgiving season.
It is the only healing item with infinite uses.

History

---

# 文档：Hunter Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：hunter potion
- 来源：wiki/Hunter_Potion

(enemy) and a Goldfish (non-enemy) illuminated by the Hunter buff.]]

The Hunter Potion is a buff potion which grants the Hunter buff when consumed. The buff shows the location of enemies, critters, and undiscovered NPCs on the screen by highlighting them in red (enemies) or green (critters and NPCs).  Although the creatures glow, they do not emit any light to the surrounding area.

Aside from being crafted, Hunter Potions can rarely be found in Pots, chests, and crates.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
Hunter Potions are very useful for locating rare enemies such as Mimics, Nymphs and Cyan Beetles, or critters such as Truffle Worms and Mice.
Bound NPCs like the Goblin Tinkerer and the Tavernkeep and neutral ones such as the Skeleton Merchant will be highlighted green under the effect of this potion, making locating them much easier as well.
The Lifeform Analyzer informs the player of the presence of nearby rare creatures. This helps determining when a Hunter Potion is worth consuming.
The Hunter Potion has great utility when fighting enemies that move through blocks, such as burrowing worm enemies like the Eater of Worlds, or Man Eaters and Angry Trappers. It makes it possible to see them when they would otherwise be hardly visible, and anticipate their attacks.
Hunter Potions can also be used to locate pocket caves underground by revealing enemies that spawn inside them.
Certain enemies  such as Wraiths will not be made any more visible by this potion.
The Hunter Potion allows distinguishing fake Lunatic Cultists from real ones.
It can be quite difficult to craft, as a Shark Fin is needed for each one. Shark Statues can make this an easier process.

History

---

# 文档：Inferno Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：inferno potion
- 来源：wiki/Inferno_Potion

The Inferno Potion is a buff potion that grants the Inferno buff when consumed. It casts a ring of fire around the player that deals 10 damage per second and inflicts the On Fire! debuff for 2 seconds on enemies inside, as well as giving off light. Critters and other passive NPCs are unaffected.

It can be crafted or found in Shadow Chests in The Underworld.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Enemies that are immune to the On Fire! debuff are also immune to damage from the potion's ring of fire.
Crafting this potion requires a Hotline Fishing Hook, a Lavaproof Fishing Hook, or lava critters, as its two fish ingredients can only be caught in lava.
On the , the inferno keeps damaging enemies when going to the armor section in the player's inventory. However, the timer for it will still go down.

Tips
While the ring deals minimal damage to most Hardmode enemies, it is very useful against enemies that throw destructible projectiles, as these are easily destroyed by the ring. Examples include Duke Fishron with his Detonating Bubbles and Plantera with her spores.

Gallery
History

---

# 文档：Invisibility Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：invisibility potion
- 来源：wiki/Invisibility_Potion

An Invisibility Potion is a buff potion that grants the Invisibility buff when consumed, which turns the player invisible.

Using an Invisibility Potion conceals the player entirely, including their armor and vanity items, as well as the player's mount. While hidden, the player's Aggro statistic is reduced, meaning enemies will be less likely to target the player and they will not fire ranged attacks as long the player remains hidden. Attacking while under the stealth effect will cause the player to reappear temporarily, and the buff will be cancelled entirely if the player takes damage. Placing or breaking blocks will also cause the player to reappear. The player's head will still be visible on the minimap to non-hostile players in multiplayer.  Using the potions also reduces enemy spawn rates by 20%.

Using the Invisibility Potion will cause the player's sprite to turn invisible, though accessories and armor will remain visible. A player in default clothing, either through unequipping all items or through use of the Familiar set, will become totally invisible. This has no effect on enemies, but an opposing player in PvP will not be able to see an invisible player with no visible items equipped. However, competing PvP players still see an invisible player's name tag.

Invisibility will never hide wings while flying, nor any lighting or particle effects from armor and other items. For example, the smoke effects from sprinting accessories, and beetles from Beetle armor, will still display. Minions, pets and light pets will also remain visible.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
It can be useful for catching critters that run away from you, especially when farming Truffle Worms as they are normally very quick to burrow.
It can be useful for raiding water chests in the ocean very early in the game.
When building a large structure, fishing, or exploring caves, it can be useful for avoiding enemies.
Its spawnrate reduction stacks with that of the Calming Potion, Peace Candle and Sunflower.

History

---

# 文档：Ironskin Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：ironskin potion
- 来源：wiki/Ironskin_Potion

The Ironskin Potion is a buff potion which grants the Ironskin buff when consumed. The buff increases the player's defense by 8, thus reducing damage by 4 /  / .

It has a small chance to appear as a bonus drop from Slimes.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
The Ironskin Potion is more effective than the Endurance Potion against attacks weaker than 40 damage (60 in expert mode and 80 in master mode); however, it is most effective to use both at the same time.
The Ironskin Potion is a very useful asset during the game as it requires very cheap and easily farmable items, making it easy to craft a large number of them.

Trivia
Although it can be made with Lead Ore, it still has Iron in the name.
Lead in real life is actually toxic when orally ingested.

History

# 文档：Lesser Healing Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：lesser healing potion
- 来源：wiki/Lesser_Healing_Potion

The  Lesser Healing Potion is a healing item that restores 50 health when used. As its name suggests, it heals the least amount of health of all Healing Potions.

In addition to being crafted, Lesser Healing Potions can also be found naturally in pots (only before Hardmode), generated chests, Wooden and Pearlwood Crates, and dropped by most pre-Hardmode bosses.

Lesser Healing Potions can also be purchased for  from the Merchant, or from the  during moon phases 2, 4, 6, and 8:

Naturally-placed Lesser Healing Potions appear frequently on platforms in the Dungeon (along with Lesser Mana Potions). When encountered in placed form, they function as Placed Bottle crafting stations. They also show the "bottle" tooltip on the map. They can be freed with a pickaxe or drill and retrieved, but they cannot be placed by the player.

Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Trivia
The Lesser Healing Potion is one of the very few potions that use Bottles instead of Bottled Water in their crafting recipes.

History

Naturally-placed Lesser Mana Potions appear frequently on platforms in the Dungeon (along with Lesser Healing Potions). They can be freed with a pickaxe and retrieved, but they cannot be placed by the player.

# 文档：Lesser Restoration Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：lesser restoration potion
- 来源：wiki/Lesser_Restoration_Potion

The Lesser Restoration Potion is a potion that has the effects of both the Lesser Healing Potion and Lesser Mana Potion.

On the , they are no longer craftable and have instead been replaced by the Restoration Potion. However, they can still be found inside Shadow Chests in The Underworld.

On the , they are removed partially, and are replaced with Restoration Potions when cheated through inventory editors.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Like almost all other items that restore health, it has a 60-second Potion Sickness cooldown.
Like almost all other items that restore mana, it inflicts the Mana Sickness debuff.
This item cannot be used as a direct replacement for the Lesser Mana Potion as it can only be used every 60-seconds unlike other Mana Potions.

History

---

# 文档：Life Booster

- 类别：potions
- 标识：life booster
- 来源：wiki/Life_Booster

A Life Booster is one of the three power-ups occasionally dropped when striking an enemy while wearing a full set of Nebula armor. When picked up, it grants the Life Nebula buff which immediately increases the player's health regeneration rate by 3/5 health per second for 8 seconds. When a Life Booster is picked up while the Life Nebula buff is already in effect, it stacks with the previous Life Nebula, for up to 3 stacked boosts. Every 8 seconds, one stacked Life Booster expires, and the Life Nebula buff decreases by one level.

Life Boosters, like all Nebula boosters, float in the air and have a pickup range of approximately five tiles. They cannot legitimately appear in a player's inventory.

Notes
At three stacks, the player's health regeneration will be increased by  9 /  15 health per second.
In multiplayer, any player can pick up Life Boosters and benefit from the Life Nebula buff, even if they are not wearing Nebula armor. Additionally, picking up a Life Booster will grant all nearby players (within a 43.75-tile radius) one instance of Life Nebula. If the player acquiring the Life Booster is on a team, only nearby players within that team will obtain the buff.

Trivia
If a Life Booster is placed into the player's inventory using an inventory editor, it will appear as its frames of animation instead of being animated.

History

---

# 文档：Life Crystal

- 类别：potions
- 标识：life crystal
- 来源：wiki/Life_Crystal

A Life Crystal is a consumable item that permanently increases the player's health capacity by 20, adding a red heart to the player's health bar, while also restoring 20health.

Up to 15 Life Crystals can be used per player, increasing health capacity by 300 (15hearts). Added to the 100health new players begin with (5hearts), a total of 400health (20hearts) can be achieved. Additional Life Crystals can no longer be used at that point, and attempting to do so will not consume the item.

Life Crystals can be found naturally placed in any area in the Underground layer and below, though they are fairly uncommon. They cannot be found in The Underworld or the Dungeon, unless on .  They also have a  chance of dropping from a Golden or Titanium Crate.

After a world converts to Hardmode, and after the 400health maximum is reached from consuming Life Crystals, Life Fruits can be used to increase health capacity further, to a maximum of 500 (20Life Fruits).

colspan=2 style="font-size:110%;" | Life Crystals used
align=center | None:
(100 health) ||
align=center | 1 used:
(120 health) ||
align=center | 15 used:
(400 health) ||

.]]

Crafting
Used in
Notes
The maximum number of Life Crystals that can generate is  on a small world,  on a medium world, and  on a large world. Occasionally, a Life Crystal will fail to generate causing there to be slightly fewer than the maximum number in the world, but the total will not exceed these numbers.
The Life Crystal is not a healing item in the traditional sense, but can be used as such in a pinch if under 400 HP.
There are certain maximum health thresholds for specific events to occur. See Health § Notes for details.

Achievements
Tips
Life Crystals acquired after reaching 400health can be sold for coins, or used to craft Heart Lanterns.
An easy way to find Life Crystals is by using a Spelunker Potion, as they are highlighted by the Spelunker buff, and by holding or throwing a Spelunker Glowstick.
The Underground Jungle is a good place to look for Life Crystals, due to the large amounts of open space. The Ice biome or a Glowing Mushroom biome may be a less dangerous alternative.
The Life Crystal allows for the spawning of the Nurse NPC in both Pre-Hardmode, and Hardmode.
Trivia
Life Crystals can be considered the health counterpart to Mana Crystals.
Life Crystals are one of the few tiles which still obviously borrow from the Stone Block sprite, even in the final game version. Other blocks which did this, such as Ebonstone/Pearlstone prior to 1.2 or all original ores prior to , eventually had their sprites revamped.

History
References

---

# 文档：Life Fruit

- 类别：potions
- 标识：life fruit
- 来源：wiki/Life_Fruit

The Life Fruit is a Hardmode plant that grows on Jungle grass below the surface after at least one of the mechanical bosses has been defeated. Once harvested and used, a Life Fruit permanently increases the player's health capacity by 5, turning one of the hearts in the player's health meter golden. A Life Fruit can only be consumed after the player has reached the 400 health cap from Life Crystals. Be sure to look out for them, as they will blend in with the ground. It can be Shimmered into an Aegis Fruit, that can be consumed once per character for extra defense.

On the , Life Fruit will only grow after one mechanical boss has been defeated. They will also grow 25% faster on average in Expert Mode.
On the , Life Fruit can be found prior to defeating a mechanical boss, just in much smaller numbers; defeating a mechanical boss increases the amount that can grow.

A total of 20 Life Fruit are required to reach the maximum of 500 health capacity, at which point all of a player's hearts will be golden.

colspan=2 style="font-size:110%; padding:3px; margin-left:1em;" | Life Fruit Used
align=center | 1 used:
(405 health) ||
align=center | 20 used:
(500 health) ||

Crafting
Used in
Notes
Life Fruit can spawn on any block of Jungle grass (including player-placed ones) below the surface; it does not have to be in an Underground Jungle biome. However, they will never spawn within a  122×122 () /  162×162 tile area (centered on the Life Fruit) of each other, i.e. they will be spaced  60 () /  80 tiles apart at maximum concentration.
When a Life Fruit is used successfully, it restores the player's current health by 5 as well.
Like other plants, Life Fruit can be harvested with any weapon or tool; a pickaxe is not required, and any type of attack can uproot them. They will also be uprooted if the Jungle grass beneath them is replaced.
Life Fruit are highlighted by the Spelunker buff or Spelunker Glowsticks by a yellow glimmer, like ores and treasures. This effect is similar to the Life Crystal highlight.
Life Fruit currently has the highest Metal Detector value. This can make searching for ores or other valuables more difficult once full health has been reached as any errant Life Fruit will show up over everything else in the area.
On the minimap, Life Fruit are 2×2 squares and are a slightly lighter color of green than the rest of the foliage around it.  When hovered over, they are labeled as "".
Using this method doesn't always work as the map image will sometimes blend in with the terrain color.
Note that the plant that the fruit grows on is also called a Life Flower.
For every fruit used, one of your heart icons will turn golden yellow, and your maximum health goes up by 5. This may be the reason why you need 400 health to use one as using 20 will make all hearts golden. If the bar-based ui is used, it will turn a small portion of the bar yellow.
Life Flowers can be found on the world map as showing up as a bright yellow-green square. This can be a handy way of finding them.
There is a bug where the player loses the extra health from the Life Fruit upon death.
Life Flowers can still grow in small numbers before killing any mechanical bosses but will increase in growth when one is defeated.

Achievements
Tips
A Lifeforce Potion can be used to temporarily increase health capacity even beyond 500, to a maximum of 600.
In order to obtain maximum health, 20 Life Fruit are required. Unlike Life Crystals, Life Fruit have no further use after that, beside being sold for  each.
Farms can be made by making long platforms of Jungle grass underground. Jungle grass spreads with Jungle Grass Seeds.
can often lead the player to Life Fruit.
If the player has explored a large area of the Underground Jungle before defeating a mechanical boss, they can use the Metal Detector in conjunction with the map to find Life Fruit very effectively.
Before defeating all three mechanical bosses, a quick and easy way to find Life Fruit is to use the Clentaminator with Dark Blue Solution specifically, as this will break the Life fruit and leave them on the ground, and illuminated by the glowing mushroom for the player to see. After the player is done gathering, the biome may be easily switched back to the jungle via Green Solution. However, this method becomes much more dangerous after Plantera's Bulbs begin to spawn, because the conversion will break them and summon Plantera.

Trivia
There is a possibility of it spawning before a mechanical boss has been killed. This however is a very rare bug and has not yet replicated. It seems to generate during the world's transition into hard mode.
The small golden heart on the Life Fruit represents the golden heart that is obtained when a fruit is consumed.
If the player's max health is less than 500 and more than 399, Life Fruit will always increase the max health by 5, even if the current max health isn't a multiple of 5. This means, if the player sets their max health to 499 using an inventory editor and then uses a Life Fruit, the player's max health will be permanently increased to 504, which is 4 beyond the normal maximum.

History
References

# 文档：Lifeforce Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：lifeforce potion
- 来源：wiki/Lifeforce_Potion

The  Lifeforce Potion is a buff potion that grants the Lifeforce buff when consumed, which increases the player's health capacity by 20%.

The Lifeforce Potion can sometimes be found in Shadow Chests and Lihzahrd Chests.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
If the player has used a total of 15 Life Crystals and 20 Life Fruits to upgrade their health capacity to the 500 point maximum, Lifeforce Potion will temporarily boost the player's health capacity by +100, to a total of 600, the highest amount of health one can legitimately reach.
As the player's natural health regeneration rate is related to the maximum health, it will also slightly increase player's regeneration rate.
Possibly due to how the game handles rounding, it actually gives 20 maximum health per 100 maximum health total. (for example, player with 280 maximum health will get 320 maximum health rather than 336)

Tips
The Prismite can only be acquired by fishing in the Hallow. Though it can be caught at any depth, the player will have a greater chance to catch the Prismite if they fish on the surface, because only two special fish can be caught within the surface Hallow (the Prismite and the Princess Fish).
Despite the Prismite being available on the  and having "Material" listed in its tooltip, the Lifeforce Potion cannot be crafted due to a lack of Shiverthorn in the game. However, it can still be found in Shadow Chests in the Underworld.
Using the Lifeforce Potion can greatly increase a player's chances of winning any major boss battle, especially in Expert Mode and Master Mode, since the player needs all the buffs and health they can get.

History

---

# 文档：Love Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：love potion
- 来源：wiki/Love_Potion

The Love Potion is a Hardmode thrown potion that functions similarly to Thrown Water, but inflicts no functional effect. Hitting a solid block will inflict all players, NPCs, enemies, and critters with the Lovestruck debuff, causing them to emit red hearts for 30 seconds, similar to the Water Gun's dripping. This effect is purely cosmetic.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Despite the Princess Fish being available on the  and having 'Material' listed in its tooltip, the Love Potion cannot be crafted due to a lack of Shiverthorn in the game.
It can be created and used in Pre-Hardmode worlds, if an artificial Hallowed sea or lake is created for fishing - or if either Princess Fish or the Love Potion itself is transferred to the world by a Hardmode player.

Tips
Since the Princess Fish () sells for more than the Love Potion (), it is better to sell the fish than it is to sell the potion.

Trivia
Lovestruck is considered a debuff because it cannot be terminated manually and the Nurse charges money for removing it, similar to other debuffs. Despite this, the debuff icon has a blue background, which usually indicates a buff.
This might be because the Love Potion is used to "make someone fall in love", which could imply that the target is unable to remove the effect.
It applies the same particle-emitting visual effect as the Rapid Healing buff.

See also
Stink Potion

History

---

# 文档：Luck Potions

- 类别：potions
- 标识：luck potions
- 来源：wiki/Luck_Potions

The Luck Potions are three types of potions which grant the Lucky buff, increasing the player's luck.

The only difference between the three potions is the duration of the Lucky buff. The Lucky buff is unique, however, in that its effect depends on the remaining duration. The Greater Luck Potion gives +0.3 luck for 5 minutes, followed by +0.2 luck for the next 5 minutes (the equivalent of a "regular" Luck Potion), and +0.1 luck for the final 5 minutes (the equivalent of the Lesser Luck Potion).
Types

# 文档：Magic Power Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：magic power potion
- 来源：wiki/Magic_Power_Potion

The Magic Power Potion is a buff potion which grants the Magic Power buff when consumed. The buff increases damage done by magic weapons by 20%.

The buff stacks with any bonuses granted from equipped armor.

The Magic Power Potion can be crafted, but can also be found in Shadow Chests and Pots in the Underworld.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
This potion is very useful when using the hood version of the Spectre armor, as the hood will reduce magic damage by 40% (25% after the bonuses of the other armor pieces). Thus this potion can reduce the penalties of the armor down to only 5% less magic damage, causing greater damage and thus greater healing potential.

History

---

# 文档：Mana Booster

- 类别：potions
- 标识：mana booster
- 来源：wiki/Mana_Booster

A Mana Booster is one of the three power-ups occasionally dropped when striking an enemy while wearing a full set of Nebula armor. When picked up, it grants the Mana Nebula buff which immediately provides the player with an increased mana regeneration rate for 8 seconds. When a Mana Booster is picked up while the Mana Nebula buff is already in effect, it stacks with the previous Mana Nebula, for up to 3 stacked boosts. Every 8 seconds, one stacked Mana Booster expires, and the Mana Nebula buff decreases by one level.

Mana Boosters, like all Nebula boosters, float in the air and have a pickup range of approximately five tiles. They cannot legitimately appear in a player's inventory.

Notes
Unlike other sources of mana regeneration, the regeneration provided by the Mana Booster is independent of current mana supply or consumption, and will work even while continuously using a magic weapon.
At three stacks, the player's mana will regenerate at a rate of approximately 30 mana per second.
In multiplayer, any player can pick up Mana Boosters and benefit from the Mana Nebula buff, even if they are not wearing Nebula armor. Additionally, picking up a Mana Booster will grant all nearby players (within a 43.75-tile radius) one instance of Mana Nebula. If the player acquiring the Mana Booster is on a team, only nearby players within that team will obtain the buff.

Trivia
If a Mana Booster is placed into the player's inventory using an inventory editor, it will appear as its frames of animation instead of being animated.
History

---

# 文档：Mana Crystal

- 类别：potions
- 标识：mana crystal
- 来源：wiki/Mana_Crystal

A Mana Crystal is a consumable item that increases maximum mana by 20 permanently, up to a maximum of 200 total. It takes 9 Mana Crystals to reach this maximum. It also restores 20 mana when used, but only as long the character's maximum mana is below 200. Mana Crystals are created by combining  5 /  3 Fallen Stars. It would take  45 /  27 Fallen Stars to maximize your mana.

On the , it can be used to convert Panic Necklace into Band of Starpower at a Graveyard.

Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Notes
If used at the maximum amount of mana, the player will complete the normal animation of holding the Mana Crystal up, but it will have no effect, and the item will not be used.
Mana Crystals can be sold for 25-30 silver coins, they can also be sold to other players on multiplayer.
As of 1.4.4, Mana Crystals can be placed.

Achievement
Trivia
Mana Crystals can be considered the mana counterpart to Life Crystals.
However unlike Life Crystals, they cannot be used to craft lanterns or boulders.

History

Consuming any Mana Potion (Super, Greater, Regular, or Lesser) will inflict the Mana Sickness debuff on the player.

Consuming Mana Potions will not activate the Potion Sickness debuff.

Notes
This potion's buff grants the user the same speed of mana regeneration as a player who is standing completely still, and also greatly reduces the user's mana regeneration delay. This allows the user to be more mobile, and lowers any downtime for mana-consuming items.

Using several of these together with a Picksaw, , or Shroomite Digging Claw will achieve very high digging speeds. It is unknown if the potion and other buffs work with hammers.

# 文档：Mushroom

- 类别：potions
- 标识：mushroom
- 来源：wiki/Mushroom

The Mushroom is a crafting material and healing item that spawns randomly on grass or Hallowed grass located on the surface, generally in the Forest. It can be cut with any tool or weapon in order to retrieve the item.

On the , the Mushroom inflicts only 30 seconds of Potion Sickness, which is half the duration inflicted by healing potions. Even so, it can only heal 30 health every minute (15 health every 30 seconds,) compared to Lesser Healing Potion, which heals 50 health every minute.

Crafting
Used in
Tips
Using a Mushroom as a healing item is rather inefficient as it can be crafted into two Lesser Healing Potions which restore  health each.
You can throw Vile Powder or Vicious Powder on a Mushroom to turn it into a Vile Mushroom or a Vicious Mushroom, respectively. This only works on a non-harvested mushroom, though.

Farming
Mushrooms have a high sell value for the early game ( Mushrooms sell for ), plus they are very common and can be farmed.

Mushrooms grow on any free grass tiles in the surface layer, meaning they can be easily farmed by placing grass in strips. The grass can be grown by connecting dirt to existing grass, by planting Grass Seeds, or by using the Staff of Regrowth. Only one tile of vertical clearance is required, so dirt strips can be placed close together in groups of three (more than that and you may not be able to harvest them), one tile apart vertically. Mushrooms will not grow if there is a wall behind them, so make sure the sky is visible behind the dirt strips. They do not require water to grow, and can spawn while on-screen. Such a farm also needs regular clearing — Mushrooms will not replace grass, but require empty tiles to grow.

With the Dart Trap mechanism, farms can be automated to some degree. The Dart Trap(s) can be linked to a Pressure Plate at the farm's entrance or to a Timer. A launched dart will cut all grass and Mushrooms that it comes into contact with, allowing the player to simply run across the farm and pick up the dropped Mushrooms without a single swing of their sword.

Trivia
The mushroom very closely resembles the Super Mushroom power-up from the original Super Mario Bros.
This mushroom may resemble the . However, that mushroom's dots are significantly less apparent.
It is the weakest healing item that only heals in the game, restoring only  health with each Mushroom.
While Life Fruits technically have the lowest healing, healing 5 health per use, they also increase maximum health by 5.

History

---

# 文档：Night Owl Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：night owl potion
- 来源：wiki/Night_Owl_Potion

The Night Owl Potion is a buff potion which grants the Night Owl buff when consumed. The buff improves the player's night vision, and sharply increases the radius of any available light source. This potion has no effect on total darkness.

The ingredients of this potion, the Daybloom and Blinkroot, are exceedingly common and can easily be grown in Clay Pots or Planter Boxes or just farmed naturally within one's base.

Combined with the Shine Potion, it is possible to see about half the screen using nothing but basic, low-level ingredients that far outshine the Shadow Orb or other mobile light sources for exploration.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
The night vision given by this potion is the same as the one provided by Night Vision Helmet and cannot be stacked with it.

History

---

# 文档：Obsidian Skin Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：obsidian skin potion
- 来源：wiki/Obsidian_Skin_Potion

The Obsidian Skin Potion is a buff potion which grants the Obsidian Skin buff when consumed. The buff makes the player immune to lava, Hellstone, Meteorite, and the On Fire! debuff for its duration. Obsidian Skin Potions can be crafted, dropped by Pots in the Underworld, or found in Shadow Chests and crates.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
This item is commonly used to facilitate the mining of Hellstone, which excretes half a tile of lava when mined. Since Hellstone is also found under lava lakes, the potion enables players to dive for it as well.
While the Fireblossom can easily be collected in the Underworld, the Waterleaf will have to first be collected from the Desert biome. Alternatively, the player may use a personal plant garden to grow the required plants.

History

# 文档：Potion of Return

- 类别：potions
- 标识：potion of return
- 来源：wiki/Potion_of_Return

The Potion of Return is a potion that teleports the player back to their spawn point, similar to the Recall Potion, and additionally creates a portal exit at the location where the potion was consumed. A portal entrance will be created at the spawn point, which can be used to teleport back to where the player consumed the potion. The portal can only be used once and will disappear afterwards. In addition to being crafted, the Potion of Return can be found in Shadow Chests and Pots in the Underworld.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Only one portal can be active at any time in the world. If a second Potion of Return is used before using a portal, the first portal will disappear.
The portal will vanish upon exiting the world, and so cannot be used across play sessions.
The entrance and exit of the portal will be displayed on the minimap. Unlike pylons, however, the portal cannot be used from there.
In multiplayer, each player will only be able to use their own portal. This means that overlapping portals are not an issue.
Potions of Return can be used to enter the Dungeon before killing Skeletron, though this may require many potions and can be inconvenient.

Tips
In general, the Potion of Return is useful in remote locations that cannot be easily accessed from the spawn point. For instance, it can be used during exploration to clear a full inventory at spawn or stock up on consumables.
It can be particularly useful during boss fights not rooted to a specific biome, such as the Moon Lord: Consuming the potion to return to the Nurse for healing allows quickly returning back to the fight.
If a player is surrounded by enemies and finds themselves in danger, it can be useful to use a Potion of Return to teleport away and come back a few seconds later. This is likely to cause the enemies to despawn before the player re-enters, and can be especially useful on harder difficulties where the player can very easily become overwhelmed.

Trivia
The tooltip of this item, "", is a nod to many  systems, particularly bus, airplane, or train tickets, which often display text such as "round trip" when referring to the use of the ticket.
The oval-like shape and the highlights of the bottle may be a reference to how pylons look like and how they work. This is not confirmed by any developers and is just a speculation.
The entrance and exit portal sprites are slightly different, with the exit portal sprite having a lighter outline and spiraling outward, whereas the entrance portal appears to be spiraling inward.
The portals, more specifically the entrance, resembles the beta Void Bag portal sprite showcased in a old screenshot.
This item is probably a nod to the Scroll of Town Portal from the Diablo series of games, much like Mana and Healing Potions.

History

---

# 文档：Prismatic Punch

- 来源：wiki/Prismatic_Punch

The tooltip, "Feel the rainbow, taste the crystal!" is likely a reference to Skittles' slogan, "Feel the rainbow, taste the rainbow!"
The Prismatic Punch may also be a reference to the similarly colorful Prismatic Shard mineral from the game Stardew Valley.

# 文档：Pumpkin Pie

- 类别：potions
- 标识：pumpkin pie
- 来源：wiki/Pumpkin_Pie

The Pumpkin Pie is a type of food item crafted at a Furnace and is made out of 10 pumpkins. On the , it grants the player the Plenty Satisfied buff for 8 minutes. On the , it grants the player the Well Fed buff for  45 minutes /  15 minutes.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
Pumpkin Pies, even with seeds bought with the Discount Card, cannot earn profit unless the pumpkins used grew during Halloween.
On the , Pumpkin Pies are a decent source for Well Fed lasting 15 or 45 minutes, and they are fairly easy to obtain. However, in these versions they don't compete well with the results of fishing, Cooked Fish or Sashimi, which are essentially surplus and last longer on most platforms.
On the , however, they provide the more powerful Plenty Satisfied Buff, and their 8-minute duration is comparable to most other sources of the buff.

Trivia
is a dessert pie with a spiced, pumpkin-based custard filling, usually eaten while pumpkins are in season (during the fall and early winter, commonly referred to as "harvest time").  The "pumpkin spice" flavors and scents marketed at these times refer to the spice mix used in this pie.  In the United States and Canada, pumpkin pie is traditionally served at Thanksgiving and sometimes Christmas.
Despite the icon showing a raised and covered top, a traditional pumpkin pie has neither. It is typically a uniform color and texture and the smooth filling sits in an open crust. The in-game icon appears to be a soufflé baked in a natural pumpkin shell.

History

---

# 文档：Rage Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：rage potion
- 来源：wiki/Rage_Potion

The Rage Potion is a buff potion which grants the Rage buff when consumed. The buff increases a player's critical strike chance by 10%.  It is the Crimson counterpart of the Wrath Potion.

This potion can only be crafted in worlds with Crimson due to it requiring the Hemopiranha, although it is possible to make an artificial Crimson biome in a Corruption world by importing either Crimson blocks, Red Solution, Vicious Powder, or Crimson Seeds.

As of version 1.4.0.1, it is possible to naturally acquire a Rage Potion in a Corruption world by purchasing Crimson Seeds from the Dryad in a Hardmode Graveyard biome, and using the Crimson Seeds to make an artificial Crimson biome to fish in.

Crafting
Recipes
History

---

# 文档：Recall Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：recall potion
- 来源：wiki/Recall_Potion

The Recall Potion is a potion that will teleport you to your spawn point when used. It is crafted using a Specular Fish and  Daybloom /  Deathweed. It is identical in function to a Magic Mirror or Ice Mirror, but significantly faster in use time. It can also be found in underground pots and chests.

It has a small chance to appear as a bonus drop from Slimes.

Crafting
Recipes
Used in

# 文档：Recovery potions

- 标识：recovery potions
- 来源：wiki/Recovery_potions

This is a list of potions which restore health, mana, or both.

# 文档：Red Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：red potion
- 来源：wiki/Red_Potion

The Red Potion is a potion which can be found in Chests in drunk worlds ( chance) and "For the worthy" worlds ( chance). It can also be dropped by Jungle Mimics, which spawn in  worlds.

It inflicts 11 of the following debuffs for 1 hour /  /  on the player who consumed it:

Consuming a Red Potion in a "For the worthy" world will instead grant up to three of 18 of the following buffs (a random one is chosen for each checking, so one effect may be chosen multiple times, which results in fewer effects) for 30 minutes each.

Tips
Drinking Red Potions next to the Nurse can be an easy way to get the Frequent Flyer achievement, as the large number of debuffs is rather costly to remove.
As of , the rapidly increasing costs of the Nurse's services make this unnecessary.
With the Ankh Charm or Ankh Shield, and Molten armor, it is possible to remove most of the debuffs. Although Suffocation is not removed by any of the three items, healing at the Nurse will remove it.
Since buffs can be transferred between worlds, loading a "For the worthy" world, drinking Red Potions there, and then returning to one's main world can be a viable strategy.

Trivia
The Red Potion is a testing item which was changed before the release of the 1.2.2 update to test giving ordinary players debuffs instead of buffs.
It replaced the Soul Scythe in the game's code when it was introduced.
Apart from the Flask of Gold, the Red Potion is the only potion with a golden lid.
It is the only potion that inflicts more than two debuffs.
The fact that the Red Potion gives buffs in the "For the worthy" seed is a reference to its tooltip, "". This tooltip was present before the item was made obtainable, and originally, there was no situation in which the game would consider the player "worthy".
The Red Potion directly references Redigit, Terraria's lead developer.
Red's set inflicted a similar set of debuffs prior to 1.3.0.1.
Red's set does not give immunity to debuffs from this potion or other effects.
The "R" on the potion's lid is a reference to its name.

History

---

# 文档：Regeneration Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：regeneration potion
- 来源：wiki/Regeneration_Potion

The Regeneration Potion is a buff potion which grants the Regeneration buff when consumed.

The buff provides health regeneration for a total of  /  (2 health per second). The buff's effect is stackable with the Band of Regeneration. The buff is hypothetically equivalent to equipping  /  Bands of Regeneration.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
The Regeneration Potion is very useful when planning to fight for an extended period of time, such as when dealing with a Goblin Army, Pirate Invasion, or a Boss. In addition to multiplying the healing potential of a Mushroom, it uses ingredients that are fairly common and easy to find, making it convenient to produce.

History

---

# 文档：Restoration Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：restoration potion
- 来源：wiki/Restoration_Potion

The Restoration Potion restores health (and mana on ) when used. Despite its appearance, it is not created by upgrading Lesser Restoration Potions.

Consuming a Restoration Potion has the following effect:
Restores 90 health. Potion Sickness only lasts for 45 seconds.
Restores 80 health and 80 mana. Potion Sickness only lasts for 50 seconds.
Restores 100 health and 100 mana. Potion Sickness lasts for the full 60 seconds.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
The cooldown-reducing effect of the Philosopher's Stone or Charm of Myths applies to the Restoration Potion as well, further reducing Potion Sickness to  33.75 /  37.5 seconds.

Tips
Even though it restores the same amount of health, it could be considered an upgrade from the Lesser Restoration Potion due to the fact that the cooldown is reduced by 10 seconds. This makes it a great beginning healing item if Pink Gel is acquired early.
The Restoration Potion has a confusing place among the tiers of healing potions. Compared to the Healing Potion, it gives almost as much health with a shorter cooldown. Compared to the Honeyfin; it gives ¾ the healing with ¾ the cooldown. Either way, there is also natural healing/regeneration during the cooldown to consider.

Trivia
Although there are both Greater Healing Potions and Greater Mana Potions, they cannot be imbued into a "Greater Restoration Potion". The same goes for Super Healing Potions and Super Mana Potions.
All Lesser Restoration Potions were replaced with Restoration Potions in .

History

---

# 文档：Resurrection Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：resurrection potion
- 来源：wiki/Resurrection_Potion

A Resurrection Potion (lit. 复活药水) is a consumable potion which, if in the player's backpack, is immediately consumed upon death, reviving them to full health. They were sold by the Star Merchant as singles, or in packs of three or nine.

Trivia
Having a large number of Resurrection Potions on hand can allow a player to easily enter and explore the Dungeon before defeating Skeletron, as the Dungeon Guardians deplete a supply of potions somewhat slowly.

- 标识：roasted duck
- 来源：wiki/Roasted_Duck

Roasted Duck is a food item that grants the Plenty Satisfied buff.

Crafting
Used in
Trivia
The tooltip, "Better than chicken from a wall", is a reference to the video game series Castlevania, where whole, roast chickens can sometimes be found inside of walls.
Even though the buff icon for Exquisitely Stuffed depicts a Roasted Duck, the Roasted Duck itself does not provide the buff.
