# 文档：Sake

- 类别：potions
- 标识：sake
- 来源：wiki/Sake

Sake is a potion that gives the player the Tipsy debuff, which increases melee damage, but lowers the player's defense. It lasts twice as long as Ale. It is purchased from the Traveling Merchant for .

The Tipsy debuff has the following effects:

Tips
The Well Fed buff partly negates the -4 defense penalty of Tipsy.
Drinking Saké or ale increases fishing power by 5.

Trivia
is a Japanese alcoholic beverage made from fermented rice.
The saké bottle appears to have お, the Hiragana character for "O", on it. In Japanese, "O" is a particle used to convey respect of value. For example, "sushi" and "sake" are almost always referred to as "osushi" and "osake", respectively.
The tooltip is likely a reference to "" martial art styles. Despite the name, they are not martial arts used by drunken fighters, but are martial arts that imitate the movements of a drunk person.

History

---

# 文档：Shine Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：shine potion
- 来源：wiki/Shine_Potion

The Shine Potion is a buff potion which grants the Shine buff when consumed. The buff makes the player glow brightly, which is useful for exploring caves or exploring underwater.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
It loses some of its usefulness when the Shadow Orb, Crimson Heart, or a Mining Helmet is obtained, as the materials needed to make the potion could be used for more useful potions. However, unlike the Shadow Orb and Crimson Heart, the light source comes from the player, which will not fall behind, and unlike the Mining Helmet, a helmet with more defense can be worn. The light source emitted is also much brighter and the range is larger than the other three, making it slightly more useful.

History

---

# 文档：Smoothie of Darkness

- 类别：potions
- 标识：smoothie of darkness
- 来源：wiki/Smoothie_of_Darkness

The Smoothie of Darkness is a consumable potion that gives the Well Fed buff and increases life regeneration.  Like most foods, it can be displayed on a plate.

Crafting
Recipes
Trivia
Its tooltip "Come to the dark side, we have smoothies" is a reference to the famous movie, , as well as the popular internet meme, "come to the dark side", in which the phrase is usually followed by "we have ___." The item being offered in the meme is usually cookies, but here is smoothies.

History

---

# 文档：Sonar Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：sonar potion
- 来源：wiki/Sonar_Potion

The Sonar Potion is a buff potion which grants the Sonar buff when consumed. When fishing while under the effects of the potion, the name of what will be caught will appear above the lure when it is bitten.

This potion is especially useful, allowing the player to not waste bait on unnecessary fish as the cast can be left in the water until another fish bites at the line. This can be useful for angler quests as well as crate fishing. It also makes it so the player cannot get trash items even if they reel them in.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
The Sonar Potion has a chance to be rewarded by the Angler for completing fishing quests.
If Pickup Text is disabled in the game settings, the buff will not display anything.
The Sonar Potion will display any fishable entity, which includes items, crates, and Blood Moon enemies.
One exception is the Duke Fishron. No text is shown when it is about to be summoned.

Trivia
It has the same crafting recipe as the Gills Potion.

History

---

# 文档：Spelunker Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：spelunker potion
- 来源：wiki/Spelunker_Potion

The Spelunker Potion is a buff potion that grants the Spelunker buff when consumed, which illuminates valuable nearby objects. The highlight color varies between versions.

It has a small chance to appear as a bonus drop from Slimes.

The Spelunker buff illuminates the following:
Ores
Gems
Pots
Herbs
Dye plants
Chests
Life Crystals
Life Fruits

Crafting
Recipes
Treasure colors
On all versions but the , ore and other treasures will be displayed with a yellow tint. This will somewhat alter the natural color of the highlighted treasure, such as making Cobalt turquoise and Orichalcum red. Some ores are not affected, these being Crimtane (unlike its counterpart Demonite), Meteorite, Obsidian, and Hellstone. Meteorite and Crimtane, however, do provide their own light.

In the , every type of ore will be displayed in a specific color and will glow and flash:
has a reddish tone.
is red and blue, flashing.
has a blue shade.
looks like copper, but with a slight yellow tint.
is hard to distinguish from silver. Since it always gives off a glow, Spelunker is not needed to search for it.
is an intense blue, making it very distinct.
looks like silver, only the texture is slightly different and has a hint of green.
is like copper but darker, more red and has less detail.

Notes
The sparkling effect seems to be greatest with the "Retro" lighting setting.
The map displays ores very distinctly, making Spelunker Potions almost obsolete.
Ruby can often be mistaken for Amethyst while using a spelunker potion.

Tips
The Metal Detector informs the player of nearby treasure. This can help the player determine when there's nearby treasure worth spending a Spelunker Potion for.
The effect of the Spelunker Glowstick is similar to that of Spelunker Potions, but potions have the advantage of allowing the player to mine and perform other actions at the same time.
Spelunker Potions are best used while playing in high-resolution displays.
They are very useful for finding rarer ores in Hardmode.

History
Footnotes

Used in

It inflicts the Stinky debuff on enemies, players, and NPCs within a radius of 9 tiles, causing them to emit green fumes for 30 seconds, similar to the Slime Gun's dripping effect. This effect is purely cosmetic.

Its effects are unrelated to that of the Putrid Scent.

# 文档：Strange Brew

- 类别：potions
- 标识：strange brew
- 来源：wiki/Strange_Brew

The Strange Brew is a potion that is purchased from the Skeleton Merchant for  each. Upon use, it restores a random amount of health between 70 and 120, applies a random duration of Potion Sickness between 40 and 70 seconds, and has a significant chance to apply a brief period of damage invulnerability.

The odds of the invulnerability dice roll are as follows:
Effect
Chance
Nothing || 40%
1 second of invulnerability || 30%
2 seconds of invulnerability || 20%
4 seconds of invulnerability || 10%

# 文档：Super Healing Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：super healing potion
- 来源：wiki/Super_Healing_Potion

Super Healing Potion is a post-Lunatic Cultist potion that restores  health. Consumption will trigger the Potion Sickness debuff. Though their crafting cost is fairly significant, they are the highest tier of healing potion in the game.  They are also dropped by the Moon Lord.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
Getting the Alchemy Table is highly suggested before creating these potions due to the high material cost (the Alchemy Table has a 33% chance to not consume potion crafting ingredients).
These potions are obtainable before breaking the fourth Celestial Pillar by crafting the fragments dropped by the remaining tower with the other three types of fragments.
The Super Healing Potion can be very valuable for extended fights, especially in Expert, and  Master where the player must seize every advantage given to them.
They are a good use for the fragments after the Moon Lord has been defeated and all of the other gear has been attained, and, if gathered quickly after the destruction of the pillars, they can come in handy during the player's first encounter with him.

Trivia
The sprite of the Super Healing Potion shares a strong resemblance to the potion with the same name in the action role-playing hack-and-slash computer video game Diablo II.
The Super Healing Potion's  rarity is lower than the  rarity of four of its ingredients, the Lunar Fragments.
This potion heals for the same amount the old Healing Potion used to.
The Moon Lord is the only boss that drops Super Healing Potions. All other bosses drop weaker variants of healing potion.

History

---

# 文档：Super Mana Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：super mana potion
- 来源：wiki/Super_Mana_Potion

The Super Mana Potion is a potion that restores 300 mana and gives the Mana Sickness debuff upon use.

It is fairly easy to obtain, and is the most potent of all mana restoration items ( except the Strange Brew, which restores 400 mana). The next most potent item is the Greater Mana Potion, restoring 200 mana.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Since the maximum mana with Mana Crystals is 200, this item is useful mainly for mages with additional mana reserves provided by armor or the Arcane accessory modifier.
Crafting an entire stack of 99 Super Mana potions would require 105 Greater Mana Potions, 7 Fallen Stars, 21 Crystal Shards, and 7 Unicorn Horns.

History

---

# 文档：Swiftness Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：swiftness potion
- 来源：wiki/Swiftness_Potion

The Swiftness Potion is a buff potion which grants the Swiftness buff when consumed. The buff increases maximum movement speed by 25%.  The effect does not stack with Hermes Boots nor does it affect the speed of mounts.

It has a small chance to appear as a bonus drop from Slimes.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Whilst the Swiftness buff alone will not boost the player's top running speed whilst wearing any sprinting accessory, it will boost top speed further when running on Asphalt Blocks, and also increases airborne mobility.
The Swiftness buff stacks with the effects of the Slice of Cake.
The Swiftness Potion can be used with Sunflowers, Asphalt Blocks, Movement Accessories, and Sprinting accessories to increase the player's top speed even further.

See also
Horizontal movement

History

---

# 文档：Teacup

- 类别：potions
- 标识：teacup
- 来源：wiki/Teacup

The Teacup is a consumable potion-type item which grants the Well Fed buff.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
This item is only obtainable after defeating Skeletron, as Bones, only dropped by Dungeon enemies, are required to craft the Teapot.

Tips
The only root ingredient needed to craft Teacups is sand, provided all the other crafting stations to make Bottled Water (as well as the Teapot itself) are available. This makes it one of the most consistently easily-crafted food items. However, it also has a rather short buff duration of only 5 minutes, making frequent uses necessary.

History

---

# 文档：Teleportation Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：teleportation potion
- 来源：wiki/Teleportation_Potion

The Teleportation Potion is a potion that teleports the user to a random location. It can either be crafted, or found in Chests located in the Caverns Layer.

In choosing a destination, the following rules apply:
The player will always be placed on top of a solid block and never inside blocks.
The player can never be placed in front of a natural, below-surface Dungeon wall before Skeletron has been defeated.
The player can never be placed in front of a natural, below-surface Lihzahrd Brick Wall (i.e. in the Jungle Temple) before Plantera has been defeated.
The player can never be placed in a location where they would take damage from any of the following environmental hazards:
Thorny bushes
Silt, Slush, or any Sand Block
Spikes or Wooden Spikes
Meteorite, Hellstone, or Hellstone Bricks, unless the player is immune to Burning
Lava
Cactus plants in a  world

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
If the player has not explored a significant portion of the map already, the Teleportation Potion usually teleports them to new, unmapped areas, where helpful loot can be found. For instance, it can take them to a Floating Island, to The Underworld, or near an Underground Cabin.
It is recommended to carry a Magic/Ice Mirror, Recall Potion, or  as a means of returning to the base, since the potion can potentially take the player to very remote places. Returning to the spawn point can also be done by exiting and re-entering the world.

History
References

---

# 文档：Thorns Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：thorns potion
- 来源：wiki/Thorns_Potion

The Thorns Potion is a buff potion which grants the Thorns buff when consumed. The buff knocks back enemies that strike the player, dealing 100% () / 33% () of their damage to them.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
This potion cannot be created in worlds with Crimson, as Worm Teeth are only dropped by Devourers in the Corruption. This is likely an unintended issue which arose with the 1.2.3 change. The Fishing feature introduced in 1.2.4 seems however to have balanced this by giving Crimson worlds the Heartreach Potion and Rage Potion while only giving Corruption worlds the Wrath Potion. However, both the Heartreach and Thorns Potions can be found in Chests, regardless of world type.
As of , speaking to the Dryad in a Hardmode Graveyard biome allows purchasing the seeds of the evil biome opposite of the current world's evil biome (i.e. Corrupt Seeds in a Crimson world and Crimson Seeds in a Corrupt world). This means the player can create an artificial Corrupt biome in a Crimson world and therefore allow Devourers to spawn and drop Worm Teeth.
The buff stacks with the Dryad's Blessing buff, but not with the thorns effect from the Turtle armor set bonus, which overrides it.
As of , the buff overrides Dryad's Blessing instead. It does stack with Cactus armor's thorns set bonus, however.
The reflected damage will be decreased by banners (25% in Classic or lower, 50% in Expert or above), rerolled inversely by luck (having negative luck will give a chance to increase incoming damage, and thus increasing thorns damage as a result) and capped at 1000 damage.

Tips
This potion can be useful in fights where body contact is likely, such as the Creepers spawned by the Brain of Cthulhu or the final portion of the Queen Bee fight. It should never be relied on for primary damage, though, as enemies have much more health than the player.

Trivia
Like the Wrath Potion to the Rage Potion, the Thorns Potion may be seen as a counterpart to the Heartreach Potion, as both give defensive buffs with imagery which references their respective biomes.

History

---

# 文档：Titan Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：titan potion
- 来源：wiki/Titan_Potion

The  Titan Potion is a buff potion which grants the Titan buff when consumed, increasing the amount of knockback enemies will take by 50% when attacked by the player.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Throwing the Titan Potion into Shimmer in a world where Skeletron has not been defeated will prevent it from being transmuted into its ingredients. This is likely to prevent the player from acquiring bones before gaining access to the Dungeon.

Tips
The Titan Potion can help deal with large crowds of enemies, pushing them away for a while.

Trivia
The Titan Potion's buff icon could be a reference to the , and how the Titans possessed great power.

# 文档：Warmth Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：warmth potion
- 来源：wiki/Warmth_Potion

The Warmth Potion is a buff potion which grants the Warmth buff when consumed. The buff reduces damage from cold-themed enemies (see below) by 30%.

Crafting
Recipes
Affected entities
While the Warmth buff is in effect, damage from the following enemies will be reduced:
vardefine:coldDamage_enemies|#invoke:Loops|loop|$1|1||²{#if:²{npcinfo¦$1¦coldDamage}²¦₪²{npcNameFromId¦$1}²}²

Additionally, the player's projectiles reflected by a Selenian, an Expert Biome Mimic, or the Dreadnautilus will also inflict reduced damage while Warmth is active. This is applied after the original damage of the projectile is halved upon reflection. The projectiles of the following weapons are affected:

Notes
The Warmth buff does not affect the ice-themed weapons' damage in PvP.
Despite the Frost Minnow being available on the  and having "Material" listed in its tooltip, the Warmth Potion cannot be crafted due to a lack of Shiverthorn in the game.
Its damage reduction stacks with that of the Endurance Potion, granting a total 40% damage reduction from ice enemies.
The Warmth buff does not reduce damage from Frozen Zombies, and Ice Mimics, despite them being cold enemies.

Tips
This item is highly advantageous during the Frost Legion and Frost Moon events, as many of their enemies are affected by the Warmth buff. Additionally, it will last the full duration of the Frost Moon.
Although some of Deerclops' attacks do not use ice, their damage is still reduced by 30%. This makes the potion quite helpful during the fight.

Trivia
Despite its name, this buff does not protect against the Chilled debuff. The only way to do so is with accessories such as the Hand Warmer, Ankh Shield, or Arctic Diving Gear.

History

---

# 文档：Water Walking Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：water walking potion
- 来源：wiki/Water_Walking_Potion

The Water Walking Potion is a buff potion which grants the Water Walking buff when consumed. The buff allows players to walk on water, honey, lava and shimmer. The player can sink in the liquid by pressing .

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
Despite the description, players are also able to walk on lava without taking damage and without the need for the Obsidian Skin Potion. This makes it a viable alternative to Obsidian Skin Potions when traversing the Underworld, as the ingredients required are generally safer to obtain (though more time-consuming to gather in bulk). Players should still take care when dropping long distances onto lava, as they may find themselves partially submerged and take damage from the lava.
This potion can be used to make the Ocean into a makeshift arena, for bosses like the Dreadnautilus or Duke Fishron.
While Lava Waders partially combine the functionalities of this potion and the Obsidian Skin Potion, they require an additional accessory slot.
The buff icon appears to depict the Guide.
Falling onto water will still prevent fall damage.

History

---

# 文档：Wiesnbräu

- 类别：potions
- 标识：wiesnbräu
- 来源：wiki/Wiesnbräu

The Wiesnbräu is a consumable buff potion that grants the Drunk buff when consumed. Under the effects of the buff, the damage the player takes and deals is halved (rounded up).  Consuming a Wiesnbräu also activates the Potion Sickness debuff, and it cannot be used while the debuff is active. It is purchased from the Merchant for , during Oktoberfest.

Tips
Wiesnbräu is fairly expensive for its short duration and has negative effects, so some may find it unwise to spend money on it.

Trivia
Wiesnbräu is depicted as being served in a , a "beer boot". In , Germany, this boot-shaped mug is used for serving beer occasionally.
Beer served during Oktoberfest is better known in reality as "Wiesnbier", which roughly translates to "meadow beer". "Bräu" means "brew" in German.
Even though it reduces the player's damage dealt, Drunk is considered a buff instead of a debuff.
It is the only consumable to cause the Potion Sickness debuff without restoring any health.

See also
Ale, a consumable of similar appearance whose granted debuff acts oppositely.

History

---

# 文档：Wormhole Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：wormhole potion
- 来源：wiki/Wormhole_Potion

The Wormhole Potion is a potion which allows a player to instantly teleport to another team member's location in multiplayer, by selecting their player icon in the full-screen map (accessible by pressing the  key). It is only possible to teleport to players of the same team, and the teleport will be announced in a status message. It is not possible to use Wormhole Potions if the player has the minimap disabled.

Wormhole Potions can drop from Pots and slimes, but only in multiplayer. Note that other players do not have to be online for the potion to drop, so just selecting multiplayer and hosting the game can allow collecting the potions. Wormhole Potions can be crafted in a singleplayer world as well.

It has a small chance to appear as a bonus drop from Slimes.

In 1.4.4 the recipe changed to 3 bottled water instead of 1, effectively tripling the number of Wormhole Potions crafted.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
It is very useful for multiplayer boss fights, especially those that are restricted to a specific location, such as the Wall of Flesh fight. Dead players can quickly rejoin the fight after respawning by teleporting to another player near the boss.

Trivia
The Wormhole Potion was added in a  version, most likely for testing, but was removed shortly after. Since Mobile 1.3.0.7, it is permanently and legitimately available on Mobile.
Prior to 1.3.0.3, the Wormhole Potion was named  internally.

History

---

# 文档：Wrath Potion

- 类别：potions
- 标识：wrath potion
- 来源：wiki/Wrath_Potion

The Wrath Potion is a buff potion which grants the Wrath buff when consumed. The buff increases damage done with weapons by 10%.  It appears to be the Corruption variant of the Rage Potion.

This potion can only be crafted in worlds with Corruption due to it requiring the Ebonkoi, although it is possible to make an artificial Corruption biome in a Crimson world by importing either Corruption blocks, Purple Solution, or Corrupt Seeds.

As of version 1.4.0.1, it is possible to naturally acquire a Wrath Potion in a Crimson world by purchasing Corrupt Seeds from the Dryad in a Hardmode Graveyard biome, and using the Corrupt Seeds to make an artificial Corruption biome to fish in.

Crafting
Recipes
History

---

# 文档：Weapons

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：weapons
- 来源：wiki/Weapons

Weapons are essential items used for combat against enemies, bosses, critters, and even other players during PvP games. Some weapons can be crafted at a Work Bench or a Pre-Hardmode/Hardmode Anvil, while others can only be found in Chests, as enemy drops, or purchased from NPCs. Terraria has a wide variety of weapons and weapon classes, each suited to particular play styles or specific tasks.

Most tools can also be used as weapons, despite it not being their primary purpose. Traps, self-defending NPCs , and even lava, although not primarily weapons, can be considered a weapon if somehow used in combat.

Melee weapons
Swords
Swords are melee weapons which can be divided into 2 basic categories: shortswords and broadswords.
Shortswords have very limited range, and they attack with a horizontal stabbing motion instead of an arc. On , they act more similarly to spears, being able to stab in more directions. Broadswords are swung overhead, which makes it easier to defeat flying and jumping enemies.
infocard|class=terraria compact|text=

Yoyos
are flail-like melee weapons that follow the player's cursor when thrown.

Spears
Spears are melee weapons that have a longer range than most swords, but attack with a stabbing motion. Spears also can hit targets more than once, making them especially useful for dealing with  large enemies.

Boomerangs
Boomerangs are melee weapons that return to the player after being thrown. Together with yoyos, they provide the earliest distance attacks that benefit from melee bonuses.

Flails
Flails are melee weapons that consist of an object linked to the player by a chain, which can either be thrown or fired. Thrown flails can be suspended around the player indefinitely, whereas launched flails immediately return to the player.

Other
These are melee weapons that possess unique means of attack. Some also launch projectiles.

Ranged weapons
Bows
Bows are ranged weapons that fire arrows as ammunition. Although several bows deal no knockback, most arrows do.

Repeaters
Repeaters are ranged weapons that fire arrows as ammunition, similar to bows. All repeaters are Hardmode-exclusive.

Guns
Guns are a type of ranged weapon that use bullets as ammo.

Launchers
Launchers are Hardmode-exclusive ranged weapons that fire rockets and similar explosive ammunition.

Consumables
Consumable ranged weapons do not require ammunition and are consumed upon use.

Grenades
Others
Note: The Confetti Gun, Slime Gun, and Water Gun are novelty weapons, causing no damage or knockback. The Confetti Gun is a consumable, and can only be used once. The Slime Gun and Water Gun cause purely aesthetic debuffs and cannot be placed on a Weapon Rack.

Magic weapons
Wands
Wands are magic weapons that are mainly used to deal damage via a projectile attack.

Magic guns
Spell books
Others
Summoning weapons
Minion-summoning weapons
These weapons summon minions to attack enemies.

Sentry-summoning weapons
These weapons summon sentries to attack enemies, though sentries do not move like minions.

Whips
Whips are weapons that when used swing out at certain distances similar to the Solar Eruption.

Other weapons
Placeable weapons
Explosives
Other weapons
Unimplemented weapons
Removed weapons
Notes
In total, there are  unique weapons (and damaging tools).
If an enemy is under the effect of a piercing attack (including explosions), it will cause brief invulnerability to it, making blows that hit during this interval fail to inflict damage.
Unlike most games, no matter how far an enemy is from the center of the blast radius, they always receive full damage from an AoE attack.

---

# 文档：Weapons/ja

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：weapons/ja
- 来源：wiki/Weapons/ja

Weapon (武器)とは、敵と戦うためのアイテムである。武器はクラフトしたり、敵のドロップとして入手したり、宝として入手したり、NPCから買ったりできる。

ほとんどのツールは主要な目的ではないが、武器としても使用できる。広い意味では、トラップ、自己防衛型NPC、さらにはマグマさえも武器として使用できる。

近接武器
剣
剣は近接武器であり、2つの基本的な剣は Shortsword (短剣)と Broadsword (ブロードソード)である。短剣は範囲が非常に限定されており、弧の代わりに水平の突き刺し動作で攻撃する。デスクトップ版では、探検は槍に似ており、より多くの方向に刺すことができる。ブロードソードは頭上で振られる。これにより、飛んでいる敵やジャンプしている敵を倒しやすくなる。

ハードモード前の剣
ハードモード後の剣
フェイズセイバーでは、ハードモード後の武器であり、ではハードモード前の武器である。

ヨーヨー
ヨーヨーは、投げたときにプレイヤーのカーソルに追従するフレイルのような近接武器である。

ハードモード前のヨーヨー
ハードモード後のヨーヨー
槍
槍は、ほとんどの剣よりも射程が長い近接武器ですが、刺すような動きで攻撃する。スピアはターゲットを複数回攻撃することもできるため、特に大きな敵を扱う場合に役立つ。

ハードモード前の槍
ハードモード後の槍
ブーメラン
ブーメランは、投げられた後にプレイヤーに戻る近接武器である。ヨーヨーと一緒に、ブーメランは近接ボーナスの恩恵を受ける最も早い距離攻撃を提供する。

ハードモード前のブーメラン
ハードモード後のブーメラン
フレイル
フレイルは近接武器であり、チェーンによってプレイヤーにリンクされた物で構成され、投げたり発射したりできる。投げられた殻竿は無期限にプレイヤーの周りに一時停止することができるが、発射された殻竿はすぐにプレイヤーに戻る。

ハードモード前のフレイル
ハードモード後のフレイル
その他
これらは独自の攻撃手段を備えたハードモード近接武器である。一部は発射体も発射する。

遠距離武器
弓
弓は矢を弾薬として発射する遠距離用の武器である。いくつかの弓はノックバックを与えないが、ほとんどの矢はノックバックを与える。

ハードモード前の弓
ハードモード後の弓
Repeaters
Repeaters are ranged weapons that fire arrows as ammunition, similar to bows. All repeaters are Hardmode-exclusive.

Guns
Guns are a type of ranged weapon that use bullets as ammo.

Launchers
Launchers are Hardmode-exclusive ranged weapons that fire rockets and similar explosive ammunition.

Others
Note: The Confetti Gun, Slime Gun, and Water Gun are novelty weapons, causing no damage or knockback. The Confetti Gun is a consumable, and can only be used once. The Slime Gun and Water Gun cause purely aesthetic debuffs and cannot be placed on a Weapon Rack.

Magic weapons
Wands
Wands are magic weapons that are mainly used to deal damage via a projectile attack, though the [Ice Rod] does not deal any damage.

Magic guns
Spell tomes
Others
Summoning weapons
Minion-summoning weapons
These weapons summon minions to attack enemies

Sentry-summoning weapons
These weapons summon Sentries to attack enemies, though sentries don’t move like Minions

Whips
Throwing weapons
Grenades
Other weapons
Placeable weapons
Explosives
Other weapons
Unimplemented weapons
Removed weapons
Notes
In total, there are  unique weapons (and damaging tools).
If an enemy is under the effect of a piercing attack (including explosions), it will cause brief invulnerability to it, making blows that hit during this interval fail to inflict damage.
Unlike most games, no matter how far an enemy is from the center of the blast radius, they always receive full damage from an AoE attack.

# 文档：Abigail's Flower

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：abigails flower
- 来源：wiki/Abigail's_Flower

. Notice the passive damage dealt to the Target Dummy.]]

Abigail's Flower is a pre-Hardmode summon weapon that summons the Abigail minion. Abigail attacks by floating to enemies and flashing a light on them, similar to the Medusa Head.  When idle, the summon will still do damage towards entities.

Like the Stardust Dragon and the Desert Tiger, Abigail can only be summoned once but will gain damage and movement speed the more times she is summoned. Each time you re-summon her, an additional empty minion slot will be occupied.

Abigail's Flower can be uncommonly found as a  growing on grass near a placed Tombstone, regardless of world progression. Its spawn chance is increased in a Constant world.

Its best modifiers are either Mythical, Furious, or Godly in Pre-Hardmode. Due to its low base damage, all damage-improving modifiers of +10% or greater improve its damage by the same amount. The Ruthless modifier has no lasting advantage over the others mentioned, as it deals less knockback.

Notes
Abigail's base damage starts at  and will increase each time she is summoned thereafter. It will increase by 3–4 damage (55% of the base damage) in pre-Hardmode and by 7–8 damage (130% of the base damage) in Hardmode. For example, using Abigail's Flower five times will cause Abigail to deal 19 damage in pre-Hardmode and 37 damage in Hardmode.
Abigail also increases in movement speed, acceleration, and max number of enemies she can damage at once with each summon.
Growth requirements:
In regular worlds, Abigail's Flower can only grow on the surface and in Space.
In don't dig up and get fixed boi worlds, it can grow at any layer.
It grows on most types of grass (see below) that is no more than 10 tiles away (horizontally and vertically) from any type of Tombstone.
It cannot grow within a 240×240-tile rectangle centered on another Abigail's Flower (i.e., multiple flowers need at least 120 tiles horizontally or vertically between them).

Tips
It may help to make a large area of Tombstones evenly spaced out in large areas while trying to obtain this item, as it will have more potential locations to grow.
It is best to be used with another item that can counter an enemy's movement, as Abigail cannot move while attacking.
Despite being a pre-Hardmode summon weapon, the damage of Abigail's Flower when buffed by Summoner armors and accessories will scale in Hardmode, and can be a good substitute to Hardmode summon items, particularly due to the fact that it gains additional damage per summon in Hardmode. This is especially noticeable when paired with the Firecracker.
Conversely, avoid using Abigail's Flower with weapons that apply Frostburn like the Cool Whip. As its damage over time is not multiplicative unlike On Fire!.

Trivia
Despite being a plant, Abigail's Flower cannot be grown by walking over grass blocks with Flower Boots equipped, like other plants.
The flavor text of the item is a reference to 's examination quote for Abigail's Flower in Don't Starve Together.
Abigail's Flower is the only instance of a weapon that gains stronger damage once Hardmode has begun.
Abigail's Flower is tied with the Blade Staff for the lowest base damage of any summon weapon.
Abigail's Flower and the Terraprisma are the only minion-summoning weapons that do not have the word staff in their name.
Abigail's Flower is the only summon weapon whose sprite is not rendered at a 45-degree angle.
Abigail will occasionally make the sound of a laughing , an entity in Don't Starve Together.
Abigail's Flower can be obtained in Hardcore, due to the fact that killing NPCs with a hardcore character will create a tombstone.

See also
History
References

---

# 文档：Adamantite Chainsaw

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：adamantite chainsaw
- 来源：wiki/Adamantite_Chainsaw

The Adamantite Chainsaw is a Hardmode chainsaw that is obtainable after crafting an Adamantite Forge or Titanium Forge, and is the chainsaw alternative to the Adamantite Waraxe. Along with its slightly stronger alternate, the Titanium Chainsaw, it is stronger than the Mythril and Orichalcum Chainsaws, making it one of the strongest chainsaws that can be obtained before defeating a mechanical boss. Like all axes and chainsaws, it is used to cut down trees, cacti and giant glowing mushrooms.

Crafting
Recipes
History

---

# 文档：Adamantite Drill

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：adamantite drill
- 来源：wiki/Adamantite_Drill

The Adamantite Drill is a Hardmode drill, and is the drill alternative to the Adamantite Pickaxe. Appearances and sounds aside, the two tools are nearly functionally identical. The drill, however is half a second faster. But, unlike pickaxes, most drills cannot be reforged with size, speed, or knockback modifiers.

It can mine any block except Chlorophyte Ore and Lihzahrd Bricks.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
Early into Hardmode, it is usually preferable to save Adamantite for armor and weapons, as the Adamantite Drill does not allow mining any new blocks that the Mythril and Orichalcum pickaxes and drills would not mine.

History

---

# 文档：Adamantite Glaive

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：adamantite glaive
- 来源：wiki/Adamantite_Glaive

. Note its piercing abilities.]]

The Adamantite Glaive is a Hardmode spear crafted solely using Adamantite Bars. It lies at the highest tier of Hardmode Ore spears along with its counterpart, the Titanium Trident, and the Chlorophyte Partisan.

While it has less base damage than its sword counterpart, it gains effectiveness because of its ability to attack through multiple enemies, at a very long range, and while moving in any direction.

Crafting
Recipes
History

---

# 文档：Adamantite Pickaxe

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：adamantite pickaxe
- 来源：wiki/Adamantite_Pickaxe

The Adamantite Pickaxe is a Hardmode pickaxe, and is the pickaxe alternative to the Adamantite Drill, having a slower mining speed but longer range and benefiting from mining speed bonuses. Its alternate ore counterpart, the Titanium Pickaxe, is faster than the Adamantite Pickaxe.

It can mine any block except Chlorophyte Ore and Lihzahrd Bricks.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
Early into Hardmode, it is usually preferable to save Adamantite for armor and weapons, as the Adamantite Pickaxe does not allow mining any new blocks that the Mythril and Orichalcum Pickaxes and Drills would not mine.

History

---

The Adamantite Repeater is a Hardmode repeater crafted solely using Adamantite Bars. It is one of the strongest of the Hardmode ore repeaters, only beaten by the Titanium Repeater and Chlorophyte Shotbow.

Crafting
Recipes

- 类别：weapons

- 来源：wiki/Adamantite_Sword

The Adamantite Sword is a Hardmode sword crafted solely using Adamantite Bars, and lies at the highest tier of Hardmode ore swords along with the Titanium Sword. It is the second best ore-crafted sword before the player is able to mine Chlorophyte.  Like other Hardmode ore swords, it is capable of autoswing, despite having a rather broad appearance.

# 文档：Adamantite Waraxe

- 类别：weapons

- 来源：wiki/Adamantite_Waraxe

The Adamantite Waraxe is a Hardmode axe that is obtainable after crafting an Adamantite Forge or a Titanium Forge, and is the axe alternative to the Adamantite Chainsaw. Along with its slightly stronger alternate, the Titanium Waraxe, it is stronger than the Mythril or Orichalcum Waraxe, making it one of the strongest that can be obtained before defeating the mechanical bosses. Like all other axes and chainsaws, it is solely used to cut down trees, cacti and Giant Glowing Mushrooms.

# 文档：Aerial Bane

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：aerial bane
- 来源：wiki/Aerial_Bane

. Note how the initial arrow splits down into five more.|alt=]]

The Aerial Bane is a Hardmode, post-Golem bow which is dropped by Betsy during the Old One's Army event. It converts any type of arrow into six special arrows every time the bow is fired, and deals 50% bonus damage to airborne enemies.

The special arrows drop off sharply and will disappear in midair if they fall enough distance. Upon hitting an enemy, an arrow splits into five, angled downward in a symmetrical 90-degree spread with the center arrow firing straight downward, regardless of what angle the enemy was hit at. These five new arrows will not split again when they hit an enemy.

Notes
The bow determines whether an enemy is airborne or not by checking if there is a solid block within 12 tiles below the point of impact. As such, if a "non-airborne" enemy is more than 12 tiles above a solid block, it will be considered airborne and the bow will deal extra damage.
Shimmer Arrows will not fly upwards with this bow

Tips
It is a good alternative to other powerful bows like the Pulse Bow, Tsunami, and Phantasm because of its unique ability.
This bow should be angled somewhat upward to maximize range and . It generally will not reach enemies off the screen.
Using wings can help compensate for the bow's lack of range and sharp drop-off.
The Aerial Bane is very useful against most bosses, as they are typically airborne. It is particularly efficient during events, where there are a lot of ground enemies that can be hit by the additional projectiles.
On Console versions (citation needed), both the splitting and special arrows can hit the same target, resulting in absurdly high damage. For the best results, fire from an upwards position.

History
References

---

# 文档：Amarok

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：amarok
- 来源：wiki/Amarok

debuff.]]

The Amarok is a Hardmode yoyo which has a chance to inflict the Frostburn debuff whenever it hits an enemy. It has a reach of 18 tiles, and a spin duration of 15 seconds. The Amarok has a  chance to drop from any enemy defeated in a Snow biome. It can also drop in a Corrupted/Crimsoned or Hallowed Snow biome.

Achievements
Tips
This is a weapon worth farming for. It can be acquired immediately upon entering Hardmode, helps defeating tougher enemies, and is the strongest yoyo available prior to defeating a mechanical boss. If playing on Expert Mode or Master Mode, the player should have some extra money for reforging to Godly (each reforge costs between 5–).
King Slime is an excellent enemy to spawn, as each slime spawned can drop the Amarok. By Hardmode, the player should have little trouble handling the boss, as well as an abundant supply of Gold/Platinum for crafting the Slime Crown.
Another good way to get the Amarok is to craft a Goblin Battle Standard and use it in a Snow biome. This provides a lot of enemies to kill, and as most of them are very weak pre-Hardmode enemies (minus the Goblin Warlock).
A good synergy can be made with the Magma Stone (or any of its upgrades), to inflict both the Frostburn and On Fire! debuffs at the same time, and perhaps even adding on a Flask for good measure.

Trivia
In , the  is a gigantic wolf god, a lone hunter that attacks those hunting alone at night.
It is possible to get the Amarok from Deerclops.

History

---

# 文档：Amazon

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：amazon
- 来源：wiki/Amazon

The Amazon is a yoyo that is crafted from materials found in the Underground Jungle biome. It has a spin duration of 8-9 seconds and can reach up to 13 tiles.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
If players are willing to take the risk of navigating the Underground Jungle early on, the Amazon can be acquired within the first few days of starting a new world.
Due to the Amazon's high base damage and long flight time, this provides them with a very powerful starting weapon. It is even useful for the first bosses, like the Eye of Cthulhu and the Brain of Cthulhu.
The Amazon even outperforms both the Malaise and the Artery in damage, making it an ideal weapon for after Eye of Cthulhu.
In order to gather Stingers, Spiked Jungle Slimes can be trapped behind walls and killed safely. When going for such a strategy, the player is advised to be on the lookout for Man Eaters and Hornets, as they are able to quickly kill a new character.
In , Stingers are a guaranteed drop from the enemies that drop it. The Amazon can thus be obtained more quickly than on normal mode.
Jungle Spores are usually found in small caves surrounded by mud blocks. Man Eaters can move through blocks and cause considerably high damage; to avoid this problem, new players should stay in open spaces that allow some movement to dodge the attacks.
Its high sale price makes it a decent source of money when the required materials are available. While the yoyo is worth , its ingredients only sell for  (% of the yoyo's value).  In 1.4 versions, other crafts with the same materials can be more profitable; see the Guide to Making Money for more details.

History

---

# 文档：Amber Staff

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：amber staff
- 来源：wiki/Amber_Staff

The Amber Staff is a pre-Hardmode magic weapon. It functions as a gem staff, firing a light-emitting projectile that can pierce 1 enemy. Like the other high-tier gem staves, it can autofire.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Amber Staff's statistics are identical to the Ruby Staff.

Tips
On the , Amber Staffs are prohibitively difficult to obtain, as they require at least a Nightmare/Deathbringer Pickaxe to mine the Desert Fossils and an Extractinator to convert them. However, other gem types (and the metals to go with them) are available much earlier and in higher quantities; by the time a player can make an Amber Staff, they will likely already have a Ruby or Diamond staff, if not some more powerful weapon.

Trivia
The Amber Staff is the only gem staff that does not require metal bars to craft; instead it requires Sturdy Fossils.

History

- 类别：weapons

The Amethyst Staff is the weakest of the gem staves. It shoots a single bolt. It has slow speed and low knockback, but even with the low starting base mana of 20, the staff can fire  consecutive shots.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
If the world generated with Tin Ore, the player will have to make the Topaz Staff instead, unless they can gather enough Copper Ore with the Extractinator, collect it from another world, collect it from slimes, or get copper through fishing crates.
Due to its somewhat slow projectile speed, this weapon suffers when facing moving aerial targets, such as the Demon Eye.

# 文档：Aqua Scepter

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：aqua scepter
- 来源：wiki/Aqua_Scepter

.]]

The Aqua Scepter is a magic weapon that can be obtained from Locked Gold Chests within the Dungeon, or from  found in Dungeon Crates from fishing. It auto-fires an arc of water particles which can pierce an infinite number of enemies.

The Aqua Scepter can be continuously used to keep enemies juggled. However, the sprite of the Aqua Scepter itself deals no damage. Therefore, an enemy too close to the wielder will not get hit by the water, and simply move past the outstretched wand to hit the player.

Notes
The horizontal projectile range is about 64 tiles from the sprite, if angled correctly.
Despite being a water-themed weapon, the range is reduced when the player is in water.
The Aqua Scepter is also unable to extinguish enemies who are lit On Fire!.
The Aqua Scepter's attack consists of two separate streams of water superimposed on top of each other, similar to the Golden Shower which has 3.
In Remix and Everything worlds, its damage is increased to 96. It swaps places with the Bubble Gun, and instead drops from Duke Fishron.

Tips
Its particles emit light, so the Aqua Scepter can be useful in scouting out caverns.

Trivia
The Golden Shower can be considered a Hardmode upgrade of the Aqua Scepter.
It makes the same sound effect as the Rocket Boots.
Due to the blue color of the staff portion of the scepter, it can be assumed it is made from cobalt, like most other Dungeon items, i.e. Muramasa, Magic Missile, and of course, the Cobalt Shield.

History

---

# 文档：Arkhalis

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：arkhalis
- 来源：wiki/Arkhalis

The Arkhalis is a rare melee weapon that appears to be a sword, but does not present an actual blade when used. Instead of an overhead broadsword swing or a shortsword stab, the Arkhalis autoswings and appears as a series of blurred slashes aimed in the direction of the cursor. Anything within this area takes constant damage at a rate far exceeding the weapon's stated use time. This damaging area has a rather wide spread, even attacking behind the player for a very short distance.

On , it is part of Arkhalis' set, and is only obtainable through Hardmode Treasure Bags in Expert Mode. It ignores up to 20 enemy defense. It has a weaker variant known as the Terragrim, which replaces it as a potential drop from Enchanted Sword Shrines.

On , it is obtainable from the unique background object found in an Enchanted Sword Shrine or very rarely in the Caverns layer. This background object has a  chance to contain a "true" breakable sword item rather than a discolored "false" sword. When this item is broken, it has a  chance to drop the Arkhalis rather than an Enchanted Sword. This means that on average, the chance of an Enchanted Sword Shrine dropping an Arkhalis is . The sword that the item gives is determined at the time the background object is broken, so there is no way to determine which sword the player will be given.

The Arkhalis's unique means of attack is unaffected by any size and speed modifiers.

Notes
The Arkhalis and  are the only swords unable to obtain the Legendary modifier.
The Arkhalis is also the only pre-Hardmode weapon to be able to achieve the highest rarity in the game, that being Purple.
The color of the Arkhalis changes to the color of the player's undershirt and the span is larger if the player is wearing Arkhalis' Bodice. This effect is purely visual and has no effect on the size of its hitbox.
Any sword shrines in worlds that were created before the Terragrim was added will not drop the Arkhalis.

Tips
The Arkhalis emits a large amount of white light-emitting particles for a short distance, making it useful for peeking through cave walls.
Though its base damage is low, its high attack speed and multiple attacks per hit make it effective well into Hardmode.
Because of its high attack speed and its ability to hit everything around the player, it can hit any projectile that can be destroyed before it reaches the player, making it fairly useful against the Goblin Sorcerer and Goblin Summoner's Chaos Balls and any other enemy with destructible projectiles.
Knockback should be balanced on this weapon, to keep enemies inside the damage zone. Consider using a Ruthless modifier for pre-Hardmode enemies, and Godly or Demonic for Hardmode enemies.
Utilizing the fast speed of the Arkhalis combined with its small knockback, you can easily stun-lock enemies like the Unicorn.
As it has a wide angle, it can be useful against worm enemies.
The best world size for finding the Arkhalis is medium, due to there being 1 sword shrine in small worlds, 3 in medium worlds, and 4 in large worlds. Despite there being more sword shrines in large worlds, it is easier to find them in medium worlds due to there being less space in between each shrine.
Since the presence of the Arkhalis is determined when the background object is destroyed, it is possible to force its appearance by "save scumming": Having found an Enchanted Sword Shrine with an enchanted sword in stone, switch autosave off, and then keep breaking the background object and force-quitting Terraria (NOT pressing save and exit in the pause menu, but closing the Terraria application itself) until you get an Arkhalis instead of an Enchanted Sword. While this method may be considered cheating by many, it can be useful for some players.
It has been replaced by the Terragrim due to it being moved to Arkhayla's developer set.
Due to this weapon's short range and extremely rapid attack speed, it synergizes very well with Titanium armor, spawning many shards and dealing a large amount of extra damage. The shards can also provide an extra source of knockback.

Trivia
The weapon is named for Arkhayla, a former Quality Director at Re-Logic. Its tooltip, "I didn't get this off of a Schmoo", refers to the means of obtaining a Crissaegrim in Castlevania: Symphony of the Night. Crissaegrim is Arkhayla's favorite sword, and her namesake weapon's appearance and function are modeled after it.
}}

---

# 文档：Axe of Regrowth

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：axe of regrowth
- 来源：wiki/Axe_of_Regrowth

The Axe of Regrowth is a pre-hardmode weapon crafted from a Staff of Regrowth. Like the Staff of Regrowth, it increases the yield of any herbs and seeds harvested. When used on Dirt Blocks, Grass will be planted without the need for seeds. When used to chop down trees, an acorn will automatically be planted in the same spot.

When harvesting plants from Clay Pots or Planter Boxes, only blooming herbs that would drop seeds can be cut. When harvesting plants from natural blocks in Singleplayer mode, immature sprouts will be destroyed and plants that are fully grown but not in bloom will drop seeds.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Planting Trees with the Axe of Regrowth does not consume Acorns from the player's inventory.
The Axe of Regrowth does not grow Jungle Grass on Mud Blocks.
If grass is grown near lava, it will revert to dirt after a few moments.
Using the Axe to collect Glowing Mushrooms will not yield extra Glowing Mushrooms nor Mushroom Grass Seeds.
Grass placed below depth zero will not naturally spread and will not grow tall grass or fuzz.
The Axe of Regrowth only harvests fully-grown Pumpkins.
Chopping down Gem Trees will not automatically replant Gemcorns.

Tips
The Axe of Regrowth is the tool of choice to increase herb yields, especially from Farming.
The Axe of Regrowth may grow grass on any Dirt Block, no matter how deep it is. Unfortunately, grass has limited function underground. Past a certain point, Grass will not grow weeds or Dayblooms, and Trees will not mature.
The Axe of Regrowth can be used to indirectly spread Hallowed, Corrupted, or Crimson grass underground by growing grass near Pearlstone, Ebonstone, or Crimstone respectively.
The Axe of Regrowth is useful for placement of Sunflowers in arenas, homes, etc. Sunflowers can be placed anywhere with just 2 grass-covered dirt blocks, and enough vertical space.
The rate at which grass is created with the Axe of Regrowth is increased by the Brick Layer.

History
References

The Axearang is a pre-hardmode axe and boomerang obtained by throwing an axe of regrowth in shimmer. it chops down any tree in its path when thrown but does not chop trees when it is returning to the player. it can chop down all trees including gem trees and Glowing mushroom trees but it does not cut down cactuses

The Guide to environmental preservation will prevent the Axearang from chopping down trees

Its best modifier is Godly or Demonic both increase the damage by the same amount

Trivia
The Axearang is a reference to the Great Tree vs Axe War of 2023, a community ran event that many Terraria developers participated in. It was added to the game as a reward for the supporters of Team Axe.
The Axearang's sprite may be based on the kukri, a style of Nepali machete often used to clear thick plant growth.

# 文档：Ballista sentry summons

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：ballista sentry summons
- 来源：wiki/Ballista_sentry_summons

The Ballista Rod, Cane, and Staff are special sentry summon weapons.  Each Ballista sentry is a large automated crossbow device on the ground that fires piercing spears at approaching enemies. The Rod is sold by the Tavernkeep for , the Cane for , and Staff for .

The Rod is always available. The Cane becomes available after defeating one Mechanical Boss, and the Staff becomes available after defeating Golem. The three different versions cause an identical effect, but with varying statistics, with the Staff being the most expensive and the most powerful.

These weapons can only be used outside of the Old One's Army event after the event has been successfully completed. Completing the event on any difficulty allows Rods, Canes, and Staves alike to be used outside of the event.

During the Old One's Army event, Etherian Mana can be collected, which allows the placement of multiple Ballistas that remain permanently until the event is over. Etherian Mana cannot be used outside the event.

Ballista sentries are boosted by the Squire armor and Valhalla Knight armor set bonuses, which increase the velocity of the ballista projectiles, increase penetration from 4 to 7, and grant Ballista Panic! when the player takes damage. Ballista Panic! doubles the fire rate of Ballista sentries for 5 seconds after taking damage. Additionally, the Valhalla Knight armor increases the firing rate from every 3 seconds to every 1.66 seconds.

Its best modifier is Ruthless. The Mythical modifier presents a wider array of stat bonuses, but most of these apply to the initial summoning, and not to the resulting Ballista. Additionally, minions cannot deal critical hits. The only significant advantage the Mythical modifier has over Ruthless is in knockback.

Tiers
Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Notes
Completing the Old One's Army event on any difficulty sets a hidden attribute for both the world it was completed on and the character who completed it. Any characters, including newly made ones, who visit that world may then use Rods, Canes, and Staves on any world. The character who completed the event may also use Rods, Canes, and Staves on any world.

Tips
Despite piercing it does not add invincibility frames.
Re-summoning the Ballista will reset its firing cooldown, allowing for rapid fire shots as long as the player has enough mana.
The Ballista is slower than the other towers, but its strength, range, and piercing capability make it one of the best sentries in the game, especially at higher levels. When combined with the boosts and healing power of the Valhalla Knight armor and an arena with good positioning, they can tear through bosses even up to the Moon Lord.
An effective strategy against the Old One's Army is to set up Lightning Aura sentry summons near the portals and Ballistas closer to the center.
Ballistas are often inaccurate against fast-moving targets. If you're having trouble hitting quick enemies, the Flameburst sentry summons may be more effective.
In addition, flying enemies during Old One's Army may cause the placed summon to aim up to attack them, usually missing due to travel time and wasting shots that could be used on incoming enemies. To counter this, place a few turrets behind the Mysterious Portal so ballista shots land within the path of incoming enemies.
If you buy the Ballista Rod and sell it back to the Tavernkeep without leaving the conversation, he will buy it back for around 5 Gold (depending on happiness, the less happy the better!), making this a good way to turn defender medals into cash in Pre-Hardmode.  This actually works for most of his wares.
For Pre-Hardmode summoners trying to fight the wall of flesh; An effective strategy is to stay away from the boss during the early stages and spam summon the Ballista near one of its eyes or mouth. By summoning the Ballista repeatedly it fires at an effective rate and will function more like a magic weapon, thus for this purpose the Mythical modifier will out preform the Ruthless modifier as the decreased mana consumption, higher knock back, and faster use speed now becomes relevant.

Gallery
See also
Flameburst sentry summons
Lightning Aura sentry summons
Explosive Trap sentry summons

History

# 文档：Bananarang

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：bananarang
- 来源：wiki/Bananarang

.]]

The Bananarang is a Hardmode boomerang. It returns to the player once it hits an enemy, comes in contact with a block, or reaches its maximum range. The Bananarang is dropped by Clowns during a Blood Moon, with a chance of   /  .

The Bananarang is not stackable, but can be autofired, creating a maximum of 10 Bananarang projectiles at the same time.

Bananarangs can be stacked up to 10 in one slot, and can be thrown rapidly, similarly to Light Discs.

Notes
The Bananarang has autofire.
The Bananarang does not autofire.

Tips
In contrast to Light Discs, Bananarangs do not ricochet and do not travel quite as far. However, the low delay between throws means that a player can keep a greater number of projectiles in the air at once.
This makes them very good at dealing with flying enemies, as well as keeping crowds at bay. Along with their autofire, they are quite powerful from early to mid-Hardmode, especially during events.
The Bananarang can be obtained as early as the first Blood Moon in Hardmode, yet inflicts more damage than most Adamantite and Titanium weapons.

History

The Bat Bat is a pre-Hardmode melee weapon dropped by any type of bat with a  chance. On The Constant seed, the drop chance is increased to a  chance. Hitting an enemy with the Bat Bat always restores 1 health to the user.

The tooltip is a reference to 's examination quote for the Bat Bat in .
The Bat Bat is one of 3 methods in Terraria to gain lifesteal, the others being the Spectre armor with Hood and the Vampire Knives. Of these, it is the only one that is available pre-Hardmode.
The Bat Bat is not only the slowest sword in Terraria, but is also one of the slowest melee weapons in the game, being as slow as a thrown flail.

# 文档：Bat Scepter

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：bat scepter
- 来源：wiki/Bat_Scepter

and a Mimic with the Bat Scepter. Note the homing abilities of the bat projectiles.]]

The bat scepter is a Hardmode, post-Plantera magic weapon that auto-fires  1–3 /  2–3 homing bat projectiles per shot. The projectiles will not bounce off blocks, but will dissipate upon contact instead, unlike the Bee Gun and Wasp Gun. The Bat Scepter is dropped by Pumpking during the Pumpkin Moon event.

Notes
Though the Bat Scepter is superior to the Wasp Gun in terms of base damage and has higher  against a single target, the Wasp Gun may be prefered in enclosed places, (such as the Dungeon), or against big hordes of enemies, (such as an event).
Bats will not start homing in on an enemy immediately; they must travel a certain distance first. Since they are fired in a random cone pattern and disappear rather than ricochet on contact with blocks, this restricts the number of bats that can hit an enemy in confined areas like the Dungeon.
The projectiles cannot enter water or lava, but can enter honey.
On the 3ds version the Bat Scepter cannot get mythical.

Trivia
It can be considered the "Halloween version" of the Bee Gun and Wasp Gun.
The Bat Scepter makes the same sound as wings when fired.
On the , it makes no sound when fired, but sounds like wings when impacting.
On the , it makes a subtle sound similar to that of wings when fired.

History

---

# 文档：Beam Sword

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：beam sword
- 来源：wiki/Beam_Sword

The Beam Sword is a rare Hardmode sword that fires a gold-colored projectile which also emits a small amount of light. The projectile deals full damage of the weapon. It can be swung more frequently than its projectile can fire. It has a  chance of being dropped by Armored Skeletons.

Notes
The beam cannot penetrate blocks or enemies. This allows it to ignore any piercing invincibility frames that enemies gain from being hit by the sword.
The beam has an internal cooldown of 35 frames, which is approximately 0.58 seconds. The weapon cannot be switched to another item while the beam is on cooldown.

Tips
The Beam Sword can hit an enemy twice in a single swing, once with the sword itself and another with the beam, dealing double damage while doing so.

Trivia
The Beam Sword's name is based off a common video game trope of heroic swordsmen having the ability to generate an energy projectile from their weapon, granting them an option for engaging enemies outside of their sword's range. The Beam Sword generates a sword beam that shares a resemblance to those generated by Link in The Legend Of Zelda when at full health.
The Frostbrand is a slightly weaker alternative to the Beam Sword that can pierce one enemy with its projectile, but with lower damage and swing speed.
The Beam Sword is very similar to the Enchanted Sword, in appearance and in effect.
It is statistically a direct upgrade to the Enchanted Sword, it can autoswing.

History

---

# 文档：Bee Gun

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：bee gun
- 来源：wiki/Bee_Gun

and a Skeleton.]]

The Bee Gun is a magic weapon which fires 1-6 piercing bee projectiles. These projectiles can pierce enemies or ricochet up to three times before dissipating, and home in on enemies within close enough range. They do not deal any knockback, unless enhanced by the . The Bee Gun has a 33% chance to be dropped by Queen Bee.

Notes
Bees will dissipate on contact with water or lava, but not honey. Bees will not home in on enemies in water or lava.
The Bee Gun is not affected by the Bee armor, despite the achievement.
Like all homing weapons, the bees will not attack players in PvP, but will knockback other players if hit directly.
The bees deal  base damage normally, and - base damage when enhanced by the Hive Pack.
The Bee Gun will spawn 1-4 bees by default, with two extra 16% chances to summon another bee. With the Hive Pack, there is also an extra 33% chance to summon a final bee. The average amount of bees summoned is 2.82, or 3.15 with the Hive Pack equipped.

Achievements
Tips
Despite having a base damage of only , the Bee Gun can easily overwhelm enemies if using appropriate gear.
The Bee Gun provides excellent crowd control especially when focusing the bees against enemies in a confined space, such as the Dungeon.
It is effective for  when combined with the Lucky Coin.
The  slightly increases the damage and knockback of the bees, possibly keeping the Bee Gun viable until Hardmode.
The Bee Gun can be convenient in the Underground Jungle because the bees clear vines when bouncing around, aiding player vision. Note that they will likely also break Beehives.
Due to the relatively low base damage of the Bee Gun, the  can be extremely useful in conjunction with it.

See also
Wasp Gun, a similar Hardmode weapon dropped from Plantera

History

# 文档：Betsy's Wrath

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：betsys wrath
- 来源：wiki/Betsy's_Wrath

Betsy's Wrath is a Hardmode magic weapon which is dropped by Betsy during the Old One's Army event. Using the weapon fires 3 fireballs that arc through the air, exploding upon impact. Any enemies hit will receive the Betsy's Curse debuff for 10 seconds, lowering defense by 40. The debuff cannot be applied to other players.

Notes
The debuff does not reduce an enemy's defense below 0.
The fireballs themselves do not pass through tiles, but their resulting explosions can.
Betsy's Wrath can be considered an upgrade to the Golden Shower, as its debuff, compared to , reduces defense by more than twice as much and has much higher base damage. Ichor stacks with Betsy's Curse, reducing  55 /  60 defense from an enemy.
Betsy herself is not immune to the debuff, removing her  defense completely as a result.

. Note the special particle effect on Dummies.|alt=|none]]

Immune NPCs
History
