# 文档：Blade Staff

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：blade staff
- 来源：wiki/Blade_Staff

.]]

The Blade Staff is a Hardmode summon weapon that summons an Enchanted Dagger minion suspended above the player's head, attacking enemies within range by flying toward and through them repeatedly at a fast pace. Its attacks ignore 25 enemy defense, making it more effective against Hardmode enemies than its damage stat would indicate. (Including the ignored enemy defense, the Blade Staff's effective base damage is around 18.)

On the , the Blade Staff is dropped by Queen Slime with a drop rate of  /

Its best modifiers are Demonic, Deadly, Mystic, or Hurtful. Unlike most minions, the Enchanted Dagger deals no knockback, so the weapon cannot obtain the Ruthless modifier. Due to its low base damage, all damage-improving modifiers +10% or greater improve its damage by the same amount.

Notes
The Blade Staff's minions attack at an incredibly fast rate compared to other summon weapons, hitting around four times per second. Each dagger triggers custom invulnerability frames on the target, maximizing damage output from the minions.
Unlike most minions, the daggers will immediately return to the user's side, preventing them from getting stuck behind blocks and blocking the minion's field of vision.
This weapon's defense-ignoring ability works by adding half the enemy's base defense (up to 12.5) to its damage, before being reduced by half of the enemy's actual defense. This means that it can deal more than its listed base damage against enemies that have had their defense reduced by Ichor and/or Betsy's Curse.
This ability also stacks with other defense-cutting buffs and accessories. In tandem with all possible options, the player can ignore an extra  65 /  70 defense with Ichor, Betsy's Curse, a Shark Tooth Necklace, and a Stinger Necklace, causing the daggers to ignore a total of  90 /  95 defense. While holding a weapon that deals melee damage, the Sharpening Station's buff will add an additional 12 armor penetration.
However, extra sources of armor penetration will increase damage output only if the target actually has that amount of defense. In other words, damage per hit cannot be increased by more than half of the enemy's base defense. This can ironically cause the Blade Staff to deal less damage to enemies with 0 defense, such as Duke Fishron in his third phase.
Although it has no base knockback, it can gain knockback from accessories like the Papyrus Scarab or buffs like Well Fed. This drastically increases its effectiveness against enemies that are affected by knockback, as several daggers can lock an enemy in place.

Tips
The Enchanted Daggers benefit massively from whips that increase minion damage, as most of them can more than double its  due to the low base damage but high attack rate. However, this also means it has very poor synergy with the Firecracker.
Daggers synergize very well with Dark Harvest due to Dark energy it applies, dealing high DPS against multiple targets.
Like the Sanguine Staff and Terraprisma, the Blade Staff's precise movement, tracking and fixed idle position make it an ideal choice for assisting the player with faster bosses and for crowd control.
Thanks to its ability to ignore a portion of enemy defense, the Blade Staff is a great weapon for summoners in early-to-mid Hardmode, as it is available as soon as Hardmode is initiated. However, considering the Blade Staff's difficulty to obtain as a summoner, the Spider Staff may be a better option to obtain first.
The Blade Staff does not benefit much from percentage-based damage bonuses, but it is still useful on dedicated summoner sets because it does not suffer from piercing invincibility frames and benefits greatly from whips. It is also a decent choice for melee users, who can passively increase its damage output with the Sharpened buff and Flask of Ichor. On the other hand, non-summoners will not be impressed with its damage output at all, though it can still be very useful for stun-locking enemies.
The Blade Staff's defense-cutting ability makes it very effective against the mechanical bosses, as the most defensive boss (The Destroyer) has only  defense. This also causes the daggers to lose very little  while Skeletron Prime's head is spinning, as they will ignore most of the additional defense the head gains during this phase ( total defense).
On the other hand, the daggers fare poorly against certain bosses, as some of them feature very high defense levels and will lower the dagger's damage to 1 damage per hit even with access to other defense-lowering buffs. Some examples include the Flying Dutchman and Santa-NK1, which feature  and  defense, respectively.
Because of its rapid attack rate, it is an effective summon for killing a Dungeon Guardian. Note that despite its defense-ignoring abilities, the dagger will still do 1 damage per hit (as the Dungeon Guardian has 9999 defense, while the daggers ignore only 25 defense).

Trivia
Despite being a Hardmode drop, the Blade Staff has the second lowest base damage out of all summon weapons, the lowest being the Lightning Aura Rod.
Along with the Stardust Dragon Staff, Desert Tiger Staff, and Raven Staff, the Blade Staff is one of four summon weapons whose buff description is not "&lt;name of minion&gt; will fight for you", instead being "".
"", or Lingchi, was a torture and execution method used in China from roughly 900 CE until it was banned in 1905.
It is also the title of a song by popular American singer .
The Blade Staff seems to have a similar color scheme to the Hallowed Mimic's drops, most closely resembling the Flying Knife.
The internal name for the Blade Staff is “Smolstar”.

History

---

# 文档：Bladed Glove

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：bladed glove
- 来源：wiki/Bladed_Glove

. Note the short range, but fast attack speed.]]

The Bladed Glove is a rare fast-hitting melee weapon with a very short range that can drop only during the Halloween seasonal event. It can be a very effective weapon when fighting pre-Hardmode melee enemies due to the high speed, and therefore, damage output. The  can be considered its Hardmode upgrade.

How to obtain
The Bladed Glove has a  chance of being dropped by any weak enemy during the Halloween seasonal event. Weak enemies are enemies with less than 20 defense, 40 damage and up to  of loot. These will be mostly pre hardmode enemies. When playing in expert mode all enemies are much stronger and therefore these conditions limit the number of enemies that can drop the Bloody Machete and Bladed Glove to just a couple, especially during hardmode. See the table below for clarification.

Tips
With its incredibly fast attack speed and powerful knockback, it is a safe and effective weapon for fighting numerous enemies as the knockback practically creates a shield around the player. It is not advised to use it on enemies that are immune to knockback, nor on enemies that have high defense, such as most Hardmode enemies, unless the player is farming them for money with the Lucky Coin or its upgrades.
Using the Goblin Invasion is a good way to farm this weapon, as all enemies qualify for its drop and they spawn constantly.
Another way to farm this item, as with all rare drops, is to kill enemies during a Blood Moon. A Water Candle and Battle Potion will increase the spawn rate even more.

Trivia
This item is a reference to Freddy Krueger's glove in the 1984 American supernatural slasher movie A Nightmare on Elm Street, compliant with the situation in which it drops (Halloween).
On the , the blade part of the Bladed Glove cycles through rainbow colors. On the , this used to be the case as well, but only if the item had the Legendary modifier.

History

---

# 文档：Bladetongue

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：bladetongue
- 来源：wiki/Bladetongue

.]]

The Bladetongue is a Hardmode autoswinging sword that is obtained by fishing in the Crimson biome. When it strikes an enemy, an Ichor stream is fired on an upwards arch, penetrating and damaging multiple enemies, bouncing against solid blocks, and applying the Ichor debuff. Chance of getting one is 0.5%

It is the melee Crimson counterpart to the Corruption's Toxikarp and the Hallow's Crystal Serpent.

Notes
Similar to The Horseman's Blade and Bee Keeper, the Bladetongue only creates projectiles when the sword strikes an enemy. This is unlike most other projectile swords, which fire projectiles regardless of whether or not an enemy is hit.
Among the projectile melee weapons, these three are sometimes considered as "true" melee weapons, since the player still needs to move into melee range in order to deal damage.
The Bladetongue fires one Ichor stream each time it hits an enemy (with the sword itself), i.e., hitting 3 enemies in single swing will cause 3 spits to be shot.
The Ichor stream will bounce 3 times and will disappear upon hitting a 4th block. If the stream does not hit any blocks, it will last for approximately 2 seconds.
The sword itself also inflicts the Ichor debuff, and can be used in conjunction with other weapon imbues.
The Bladetongue is roughly on par with the higher end Hardmode ore swords. The Ichor streams are piercing however, and can reduce damage somewhat due to the increased invincibility frames on enemies.

Trivia
The Bladetongue resembles the Oculus Reaver in Starbound.

History

---

# 文档：Blizzard Staff

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：blizzard staff
- 来源：wiki/Blizzard_Staff

The Blizzard Staff is a Hardmode, post-Plantera magic weapon that summons a storm of non-piercing icicles from above that travel in the general direction of the cursor. Four projectiles are fired per "swing" (10 frames). The Blizzard Staff is dropped by the Ice Queen during the Frost Moon event.

Notes
Projectiles spawned within blocks travel through them to an extent, similar to the Star Veil or Starfury, but not as reliably. The Blizzard Staff's projectiles are more likely to be obstructed by ceilings before they reach the player's depth. It is therefore much more effective in open areas.
The velocity of the projectiles is reduced when passing through liquids.
On the , the Blizzard Staff's icicles fall over a slightly larger area, rendering it less effective against smaller targets.
On the , due to the inability to bring the cursor farther than a few tiles away from the character, the icicles will go a lot farther in any given direction, and can make the icicles travel horizontally.
The Blizzard Staff can be considered an upgrade of the Meteor Staff, though the Blizzard Staff's projectiles do not pierce or explode. The Lunar Flare can be considered a direct upgrade of the Blizzard Staff, as its projectiles travel through blocks, are more precise, and have a higher DPS.
On the , opening the inventory moves the player's screen up several tiles. If they tap the top-center of the screen while it is open, they are tapping at the exact point the icicles spawn and they will fly off on random directions. Because it is trying to reach anywhere near that point (within the margin of error of this weapon's accuracy), which is entirely surrounding the spawn point of the icicles, they can fly off in any direction.
Aiming lower increases this weapon's accuracy, although this is less noticeable the lower the player aims it.

History

---

# 文档：Blood Lust Cluster

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：blood lust cluster
- 来源：wiki/Blood_Lust_Cluster

The Blood Lust Cluster is an axe crafted from Crimtane Bars. It is stronger than both the Gold and Platinum Axe, but is weaker than the Meteor Hamaxe. It has a Demonite counterpart, the War Axe of the Night, which deals less damage but attacks faster. Like all Axes, it can be used to cut down trees, cacti and Giant Glowing Mushrooms.

While it is useful as a melee weapon, its slow speed means that it is equally if not less powerful than the Gold and Platinum Broadsword, and especially the Blood Butcherer in regular combat situations.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
Despite the fact that its slow attack speed makes it inferior to the Blood Butcherer combat-wise, its ability to autoswing and to be used as an axe makes it a good choice for players who want a decent melee weapon and an axe upgrade in one.

Trivia
It shares the same sprite as the Crimson Axe enemy, except for the red glow.

History

---

# 文档：Blood Rain Bow

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：blood rain bow
- 来源：wiki/Blood_Rain_Bow

.]]

The Blood Rain Bow is a pre-Hardmode bow which rains 1–3 streams of blood from the sky instead of arrows. Each shot consumes one arrow from the player's inventory. The streams fall in a widespread area where the cursor is pointed, but are blocked by solid blocks. It has a  chance to drop from a Wandering Eye Fish or Zombie Merman.

Tips
While using this bow, unique arrow effects are not applied. It is advised to use arrows with high damage, which carries over to the blood streams.
The Blood Rain Bow can be useful for fighting the Eater of Worlds due to the large hitbox of its long body, which mitigates the projectiles' inaccuracy.

Trivia
The bow can be considered a pre-Hardmode counterpart to the Daedalus Stormbow due to its similar ability to rain projectiles from the sky.
If Blood and Gore is disabled in the Settings, the bow rains down greyish clouds instead. Its functionality is unaffected.
The tooltip  is a reference to the thrash metal band Slayer's

History

---

# 文档：Blood Thorn

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：blood thorn
- 来源：wiki/Blood_Thorn

The Blood Thorn is a Hardmode magic weapon that has a  chance of being dropped by the Hemogoblin Shark. When used, it summons penetrating Blood Thorn projectiles.

Notes
Projectiles spawn from the block nearest to the cursor, facing in the direction of the cursor relative to the player. If no blocks are nearby, the thorns will be summoned from the player. Background blocks, and actuated blocks do not count.
The thorns can pierce through 4 enemies, disappearing when hitting the fifth.

Tips
The Ice Blocks summoned by the Ice Rod can serve as roots for the Blood Thorns.

History

---

# 文档：Blood Water

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：blood water
- 来源：wiki/Blood_Water

Thrown Water refers to a set of three consumable weapons that each convert a small area into a biome (Hallow, Corruption, or Crimson for Holy Water, Unholy Water, and Blood Water respectively) when thrown. This conversion affects a roughly circular area with a radius of four tiles from the center. Enemies within this radius are also damaged slightly; this damage is not classified as any of the four damage types and therefore cannot be modified by any buffs or equipment.

Holy Water is only available in Hardmode. It is possible for Corrupted or Crimson Deserts to be generated naturally with the world, making the other two throwable waters available from the beginning of the game, however these biomes will not spread onto sand until Hardmode begins, therefore worlds generated without Corrupted/Crimson Deserts will not have Ebonsand/Crimsand until then. On the , however, Vile Powder or Vicious Powder can be used to convert sand into its corrupt counterpart.

.]]

Variants
Crafting
Recipes
Tips
The effects of Thrown Water can be reversed with Thrown Powder.
For creating large areas of Hallow, Corruption or Crimson, a Clentaminator may be a better choice. It fires a stream of solution with a range of about 60 tiles.

Trivia
The Holy Water, including its sprite and the way it is thrown, is possibly a reference to the item of the same name in the action-adventure video game series Castlevania.

See also
Guide:Artificial biomes

History

---

# 文档：Bloody Machete

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：bloody machete
- 来源：wiki/Bloody_Machete

The Bloody Machete is a rare and unique boomerang-type weapon that can drop only during the Halloween seasonal event. When thrown, as it falls downward after reaching a maximum range, and returns to the thrower upon impacting a block or enemy. Its physics are similar to the Magic Dagger, but it must return to the player before being thrown again.

How to obtain
The Bloody Machete has a  chance of being dropped by any weak enemy during the Halloween seasonal event. Weak enemies are enemies with less than 20 defense, 40 damage and up to  of loot. These will be mostly pre hardmode enemies. When playing in expert mode all enemies are much stronger and therefore these conditions limit the amount of enemies that can drop the Bloody Machete and Bladed Glove to just a couple, especially during hardmode. See the table below for clarification.

Notes
At the correct angle, its maximum horizontal range is 46 feet, or 23 tiles.

Tips
To farm this item, it is recommended to be in a Blood Moon with a Water Candle while using any kind of minions. Build a box and get in. Spawn your minion of choice outside the box. They will kill any enemies coming close and eventually one will drop the item. This might take some time, and if the Blood Moon is completed without the item in your inventory, you have to wait for another Blood Moon.
If you have a standard wooden platform arena, a Bee Gun and a Water Candle, place the candle on a wooden platform, and use the Bee Gun as much as possible during a Blood Moon with the Hive Pack. This will increase the chance for any drops.
It lasts exactly 60 seconds after being thrown.

Trivia
This is the iconic weapon of Jason Voorhees, the hockey-mask-wearing serial killer from the American horror film series . However, Jason mostly uses it in melee combat, whereas in Terraria, the machete is thrown.

History

---

# 文档：Blowgun

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：blowgun
- 来源：wiki/Blowgun

to attack Target Dummy.]]

The Blowgun is a pre-Hardmode ranged weapon, a stronger version of the Blowpipe, and is purchased from the Witch Doctor for . It looks similar to the Blowpipe, but has a red ribbon on the top and is flared on one end and is used by the Witch Doctor to defend himself.

It can shoot Seeds, Poison Darts, Cursed Darts, Ichor Darts and Crystal Darts. Carrying a Blowgun in the inventory causes any vegetation growing on normal grass blocks to occasionally yield some Seeds when destroyed. Flowers growing in Clay Pots and Planter Boxes can also yield Seeds. Both wild and cultivated flowers typically drop 4 Seeds at a time. Long grass typically drops 1-2 Seeds.

Notes
It does not receive any specialized boosts from Shroomite armor, though it still receives the stealth boost.
The Seeds used by the Blowgun cannot be planted.
Inversely, seeds that can be planted cannot be used as ammo for the Blowgun.
A useful combo in early-hardmode is the Blowgun and Cursed Darts due to the relatively low use time and decent damage. Ichor Darts are a handy alternative, but as the corruption World Feeder spawns on the surface, and that crimson players may have trouble fighting Ichor Stickers, which spawn underground, this strategy may not be as useful.

See also
Blowpipe
Dart Pistol
Dart Rifle

History

---

# 文档：Blowpipe

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：blowpipe
- 来源：wiki/Blowpipe

.]]

The Blowpipe is a gun that auto-fires Seeds and Darts as ammunition. When a Blowpipe is in a player's inventory, there is a chance that any vegetation growing on normal grass blocks will yield some Seeds. Flowers growing in Clay Pots and Planter Boxes can also yield Seeds. Both wild and cultivated flowers typically drop 4 Seeds at a time; tall grass typically drops 1–2 Seeds.

The Blowpipe can be found randomly in Wooden Chests near the Surface or in the Underground layer.

Notes
The Seeds used by the Blowpipe cannot be planted. Conversely, seeds that can be planted cannot be used as ammo for the Blowpipe.
It cannot be obtained from Wooden Crates, despite the fact that many other rare items found in Wooden Chests can.
When the Blowpipe is in the player's inventory, grass will drop Seeds if hit by a Fallen Star.

Tips
The Blowpipe is an efficient earlygame weapon for eliminating distant foes, with significantly higher range than the Wooden Boomerang. One advantage it has over bows is that its ammunition can be obtained for free.
In Hardmode, the Blowpipe and Blowgun are among the first weapons that can inflict the Ichor debuff since Ichor Darts can be crafted by hand. This allows them to be viable support weapons for inflicting Ichor against bosses, and the base damage of Ichor Darts is high enough to make the Blowpipe and Blowgun competent weapons in their own right. While the Blowgun is stronger overall, the Blowpipe fires faster and can be obtained without a Witch Doctor.

See also
Blowgun
Dart Pistol
Dart Rifle

History

---

# 文档：Blue Moon

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：blue moon
- 来源：wiki/Blue_Moon

.]]

The Blue Moon is a flail that can be found in Gold Chests within the Dungeon, or  found in  and . When used, it will emit light around it. The Blue Moon is stronger than the Ball O' Hurt or The Meatball, but is weaker than the Sunfury.

Notes
In  worlds, the Blue Moon can be found with the normally-incompatible Legendary modifier. The size boost will not take effect, though it is still the best modifier possible.

Tips
The Blue Moon is especially effective in dealing with goblins, clumps of enemies, and large enemies.

Trivia
It appears to match the Water Bolt spell and the Muramasa in appearance, likely because all these weapons are acquired from the Dungeon.
The item's name refers to a , a natural phenomenon.

History

# 文档：Blue Phaseblade

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：blue phaseblade
- 来源：wiki/Blue_Phaseblade

A Phaseblade is a pre-Hardmode broadsword. There are  seven /  six different color variations with identical statistics, with the color determined by the type of gem used to craft it. All Phaseblades emit a small amount of light when swung.

The Phasesaber is the Hardmode upgrade of the Phaseblade, being directly crafted from it.

Crafting
Recipes
Used in
Tips
The different colors can be used to differentiate teams in PvP games.
Despite all Phaseblades having identical stats, the Purple Phaseblade is usually the easiest and fastest to acquire, as Amethysts are the most common gems.

Trivia
Both the weapon's visual appearance and its sound effect bear a striking resemblance to the fictional energy sword  featured in the American epic space-opera media franchise . This reference is further reinforced by the requirement of crystals and gems in the crafting of Phaseblades and Phasesabers, alluding to the colored kyber crystals which are used in the construction of lightsabers.
The Phaseblade can be considered the Meteorite-tier ore sword, due to there being no other designated "Meteorite sword", and the fact that it requires Meteorite Bars to craft.

History

---

# 文档：Blue Phasesaber

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：blue phasesaber
- 来源：wiki/Blue_Phasesaber

.]]

A Phasesaber is a  Hardmode /  pre-Hardmode broadsword which is a direct upgrade to the pre-Hardmode Phaseblade. It is very similar to its predecessor, except dealing about twice as much damage, being capable of autoswing, and having slightly modified visuals. As with Phaseblades, there are  seven /  six different color variations with identical statistics, with the color determined by the color of the Phaseblade used to craft it. All Phasesabers emit a small amount of light when swung.

Because  drop Crystal Shards on the  and only a pre-Hardmode Anvil is required for crafting it, the Phasesaber is available pre-Hardmode on this version.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
The different colors can be used to differentiate teams in PvP games.

Trivia
Both the weapon's visual appearance and its sound effect bear a striking resemblance to the fictional energy sword  featured in the American epic space-opera media franchise . This reference is further reinforced by the requirement of crystals and gems in the crafting of Phaseblades and Phasesabers, alluding to the colored kyber crystals which are used in the construction of lightsabers.
The sprites for Phasesabers are visibly brighter than Phaseblades.
In order to fix a bug where Phasesabers placed on Weapon Racks would be unbreakable, the developers remade all Phasesabers into new items (IDs 3764 through 3769), ones presumably that are impervious to the bug. Therefore, the "old" Phasesabers that had negative IDs (IDs -19 through -24) are no longer obtainable; instead, a new one is received. This is most likely the reason the fix is not retroactive yet.

History

---

# 文档：Bone Javelin

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：bone javelin
- 来源：wiki/Bone_Javelin

. Note how the damage over time increases once more javelins are attached.]]

The Bone Javelin is a consumable ranged weapon crafted from Sturdy Fossils. Upon hitting an enemy, it will stick to the enemy and inflict the Penetrated debuff, dealing 3 damage per second. Up to six Bone Javelins can be pierced onto an enemy, dealing a total of 18 damage a second. Bone Javelins stuck in an enemy will dissipate after 15 seconds.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Even though the base damage can be amplified with armor, accessories, and buffs, the continuous damage of one Bone Javelin will still be 3 damage per second.
While not being a melee weapon, it is affected by flasks as well as the Magma Stone.
When using the Flask of Fire, the duration of the first hit's Penetrated debuff is increased.
Bone Javelins appear to have a strange effect on Granite Golems. When the Granite Golem turns invincible by crossing its arms, Bone Javelins will not do damage until the invincibility wears off, doing the damage via many 1s. It appears that the damage stacks over time, even while it is invincible.

Tips
The Bone Javelin is a powerful pre-Hardmode weapon with its high base damage of  and long sticking effect. Combined with Fossil armor, this weapon could easily two-shot most pre-Hardmode enemies and is still viable in early Hardmode. However, it lacks the piercing ability of the Bone Throwing Knife, making it most effective against single enemies.

Trivia
The Penetrated debuff can never be applied to players, so it lacks a correct debuff icon. It instead uses a duplicate of the Distorted debuff's icon.
Bone Javelins act similar to a Daybreak which both stick to an enemy and inflict a stacking damage debuff.

Immune NPCs
See also
Javelin

History

---

# 文档：Bone Pickaxe

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：bone pickaxe
- 来源：wiki/Bone_Pickaxe

The Bone Pickaxe is a pickaxe that is capable of mining all ores up to  Meteorite /  Demonite/Crimtane. It has a   /   chance of being dropped by Undead Miners, uncommon enemies found in the Cavern layer. It is tied with the Silver Pickaxe as the fastest pre-Hardmode pickaxe by mining speed, but has higher pickaxe power equal to that of the Gold Pickaxe, allowing it to mine Meteorite and destroy Dirt Blocks in a single hit. It is the fastest pickaxe for mining weak blocks, such as dirt and snow, until Hardmode, although that function is rendered largely obsolete by the .

Trivia
The Bone Pickaxe appears to be a counterpart to the Bone Sword, as it is similarly colored and dropped by Skeletons.
It is the only pickaxe that can drop from a non-boss enemy. This, along with fast mining speed, the low drop chance and spawn chance of Undead Miners, makes it quite a valuable find.
Its tooltip is a reference to the same phrase and a play on words on its name.

History

---

# 文档：Bone Sword

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：bone sword
- 来源：wiki/Bone_Sword

.]]

The Bone Sword is a melee weapon that has a  chance to drop from Skeletons and Spore Skeletons in the Cavern layer. While rare, it can be a useful weapon if obtained early in the game. Compared to other early-game swords, it swings slower and has slightly less knockback, but deals significantly more damage.

Notes
The Bone Sword appears to be a counterpart to the Bone Pickaxe, as it is similarly designed (dropped by Undead Miners in the Cavern layer).
Using Skeleton Statues can make obtaining the Bone Sword easier.

History

---

# 文档：Bone Throwing Knife

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：bone throwing knife
- 来源：wiki/Bone_Throwing_Knife

. Note how it pierces through multiple enemies.]]

Bone Throwing Knives are consumable piercing ranged weapons similar to Throwing Knives. Compared to their counterpart, Bone Throwing Knives can pierce up to five enemies; combined with the Fossil armor, they can be an effective weapon until Hardmode due to their ability to pierce multiple enemies, low use time, and reasonable damage.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Unlike its Javelin counterpart, it does not stick to enemies or inflict the Penetrated debuff.

Tips
Crafting and selling Bone Throwing Knives is the most efficient way to get money from Sturdy Fossils.

History

---

# 文档：Book of Skulls

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：book of skulls
- 来源：wiki/Book_of_Skulls

and a Skeleton. Note how the projectile homes in on enemies, and explode on impact with blocks.]]

The Book of Skulls is a magic weapon that fires slow flaming skull projectiles. Each skull can pierce twice, dissipating on its third enemy impact. The skulls do not ricochet, and disappear upon colliding with objects, but they have a fairly strong homing ability on the . They also emit a fair amount of light. The Book of Skulls has a  /  chance to drop from Skeletron.

A Shadowflame variant of the Book of Skulls is used by the Clothier NPC to protect himself on the .

Notes
Briefly after being fired, the skulls will partially turn towards the closest player (including the caster), regardless of the presence of enemies and whether or not PvP is enabled. They will not turn if the nearest player is directly behind them. They also turn around if the caster presses any movement keys.

Tips
Due to its high damage and piercing ability, the Book of Skulls remains a viable weapon through early Hardmode.
The skulls tend to hit the same enemy multiple times, giving it both good crowd control and single target damage.

History

---

# 文档：Boomstick

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：boomstick
- 来源：wiki/Boomstick

s to attack Target Dummies.]]

The Boomstick is a pre-Hardmode gun. It can occasionally be found in Ivy Chests (22.5% chance) in the Underground Jungle, and also in  (16.67% chance). It fires a spread of  3-4 /  3 shots when fired, which only consumes one unit of ammo.

The Boomstick has a stronger and less accurate pre-Hardmode upgrade, the , and a Hardmode upgrade, the Shotgun.

Tips
Due to the spread of the shots and the slow weapon speed, the Boomstick loses efficiency at long range. It is highly effective at close range, and hitting an enemy with all shots can deal enough damage to easily kill most pre-Hardmode enemies in Classic Mode.
Against single targets, it is inadvisable to use this weapon with piercing ammo like Meteor Shots. The bullets' piercing damage will cause the target to become invulnerable for a few frames, which means only one bullet will hit.
This weapon has a chance to drop from Jungle Crates found via fishing in the Jungle. By setting up an outpost viable for fishing there, the player can gain a great advantage without the need to defeat bosses or venture into deadly biomes.
This gun is strong enough to last through almost all of pre-Hardmode.

Trivia
The term ' is a reference to the 1992 American dark fantasy comedy movie ' from the  horror movie franchise, in which the protagonist, Ash, refers to his shotgun as a boomstick. The word has since become a common name for shotguns, particularly short-barreled, or "" double-barreled shotguns, which this appears to be.
The Boomstick bears a slight resemblance visually to the real-world gun Remington M1889.

History

---

# 文档：Bouncy Bomb

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：bouncy bomb
- 来源：wiki/Bouncy_Bomb

The Bouncy Bomb is an explosive crafted from a Bomb and a Pink Gel. It bounces off surfaces before exploding. It can be used as a mining tool or a weapon, although generally the former. When used, it is thrown a few blocks away from the player.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
Its sticky counterpart, the Sticky Bomb, can be easier to handle and to use for mining ores like Demonite and Crimtane , otherwise requiring an upgraded pickaxe.
The Bouncy Bomb can be very useful in PvP, as it is unpredictable and can easily confuse opponents.

History

---

# 文档：Bouncy Dynamite

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：bouncy dynamite
- 来源：wiki/Bouncy_Dynamite

Bouncy Dynamite is the opposite of Sticky Dynamite, and will bounce much more than ordinary Dynamite before it explodes. Its bouncing makes it much more difficult to hit specific enemies or tiles in contrast to Sticky Dynamite, but if utilized correctly, can be used to hit targets much farther away.

Bouncy Dynamite has the same explosive properties as Dynamite, and also has the same use time and throw distance (not including bounces).

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
Within an enclosed explosion-proof arena, it can be used to great effect.
If thrown in a way that it does not bounce, Bouncy Dynamite can roll without losing speed until it explodes.

Trivia
The tooltip appears to be referring to the fact that Bouncy Dynamite is both hard to control and very destructive at the same time.

History

---

# 文档：Bouncy Grenade

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：bouncy grenade
- 来源：wiki/Bouncy_Grenade

A Bouncy Grenade is a consumable explosive  ranged /  throwing weapon made of Pink Gel and Grenades. Instead of rolling or sticking in place upon contact with tiles, it will bounce and retain much of its momentum.

Like normal Grenades, they will detonate upon touching an enemy, or the player that threw it.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
The Bouncy Grenade does 5 more damage than the classic Grenade, though the Pink Gel can prove expensive for players without a sufficient supply.
It can also be useful in certain boss fights as it can travel much further than normal.

History

---

# 文档：Brand of the Inferno

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：brand of the inferno
- 来源：wiki/Brand_of_the_Inferno

, and parrying a Mimic's attack.]]

The Brand of the Inferno is a Hardmode sword dropped by the Ogre mini-boss during the Old One's Army event. It has a  chance of inflicting the On Fire! debuff for 5 seconds.

With any equipped shield (including the Squire's Shield, but not Huntress's Buckler) and holding this sword, pressing the  button will cause the character to brace for the next attack (like Sergeant United Shield). When timed correctly, just before taking a hit, the enemy's attack will be parried, dealing some damage to the attacker and granting the Striking Moment buff for the next 5 seconds. This allows the next melee attack to deal 5 times the damage. Alternatively, if the shield is held in place, 20 defense is temporarily gained; this lasts until the shield is once again lowered.

Notes
The buff tooltip claims that damage is increased by 500% (i.e., multiplied by 6), which is incorrect.
The Brand of the Inferno's damage reflection ability can only be used to deflect contact damage from enemies. Fall damage, lava damage, enemy projectiles, and traps will still harm the player.
It can, however, reduce damage from these sources with the 20 defense gained while pressing the  button.

Parrying in  with Godmode enabled will do nothing.
Successfully parrying an attack prevents all damage to the player, grants 30 / 60 (with Cross Necklace equipped) immunity frames and applies knockback.
Parrying an attack deals the same damage as attacks reflected by the Thorns Effect of Turtle armor, which is twice that of a Thorns Potion.
The damage dealt by parrying an attack is decreased by banners (25% in Classic or lower, 50% in Expert or above), rerolled inversely by luck (having negative luck will give a chance to increase incoming damage, and thorns as a result) and capped at 1000 damage.
The player must be facing an enemy to parry its attack.
Tips
The act of gaining the attack buff and switching to a more powerful weapon like Meowmere has potential for massive damage, especially if somehow timed perfectly with the Psycho Knife's buffs.

Trivia
In the inventory, the sword's loops appear hollow. However, while being used, the loops are filled with flame. This is purely a graphical difference.
The Brand of the Inferno has similarities to the  in regard to blade design and name (Tizona means firebrand).

History

---

# 文档：Breaker Blade

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：breaker blade
- 来源：wiki/Breaker_Blade

and a Mimic. Note the long reach and extra damage on the Mimic's first hit.]]

The Breaker Blade is a Hardmode broadsword. It is currently the third largest sword in the game, giving it a great melee reach. It has a  /  chance to be dropped by the Wall of Flesh. On , it will also deal +150% damage to enemies with over 90% health.

Tips
The Breaker Blade fares well as a "first hit" weapon for entering combat, as enemies above 90% health will take +150% damage.
In tandem with the Feral Claws or an upgrade of it, the Breaker Blade becomes an effective weapon due to it gaining autoswing and even higher knockback. Its knockback will be boosted to the point where enemies can be juggled in midair; on the other hand, this makes it very difficult to hit a target multiple times without chasing them down.
Due to the Breaker Blade's massive range and relatively high knockback, it is a good choice for use if the player is being swarmed. This can be relatively common during early Hardmode, and gives the player enough time to properly prepare or run away.
As knockback is multiplied by 1.4 on a critical hit, having modifiers that increase the player's crit chance makes the weapon extremely capable of knocking back enemies.
Because of its slow swing, the Breaker Blade can hit the same enemy multiple times in a single swing, somewhat compensating for its slow use time. However, its aforementioned knockback makes it unlikely for this to occur unless the enemy is cornered.
The Breaker Blade's reach is especially useful for gathering Glowing Mushrooms in compact farms.

Trivia
The Breaker Blade is a reference to the Buster Sword from the game , wielded by the protagonist . The resemblance can be seen in the synonymous naming (Breaker Blade/Buster Sword), and the weapon's similar designs, including its red handle, very large size and wedge shape, and the two red and green spots near the base of the blade, which reference Final Fantasy VII's "Materia" orbs that can be inserted into weapons.
The spots being red and green means that in the Final Fantasy VII, they would respectively be "Summon" and "Magic" materia.
The Flying Dragon sword is also very large, actually surpassing the Breaker Blade in terms of image area, although it is held higher up the handle when used, therefore not surpassing it in overall swing distance. The hitbox extends negligibly farther horizontally by about a pixel, but falls short in vertical distance.
The Keybrand can be seen as the opposite of Breaker Blade in terms of bonuses to damage output: the Breaker Blade deals more damage to enemies that are at full health, while the Keybrand deals more damage to enemies that are below full health. Fittingly, both are references to swords found in franchises by Square Enix.
It is the third largest sword in the game, only behind the Volcano and the Blade of Grass.

History

---

# 文档：Breathing Reed

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：breathing reed
- 来源：wiki/Breathing_Reed

The Breathing Reed is an item which aids underwater travel. It can be found in Water Chests and Ocean Crates. On the , it is also a melee weapon.

For the Breathing Reed to take effect, it must be held, i.e. selected in the hotbar or being attached to the cursor. When held, the reed appears above the player and the rate at which their breath meter depletes as well as the rate at which they lose health while drowning is halved. The reed's tip does not need to be above the surface of the water for this to take effect. Additionally, the breath meter refills as soon as the tip of the reed is outside of water, but not within a block.



Notes
Holding the Breathing Reed prolongs the underwater breath to 46.67seconds or, if paired with diving equipment, to 3minutes and 6.67seconds. In addition, the rate at which health is lost while drowning is reduced to 1/2 or 1/8, respectively.
Though the Breathing Reed can have modifiers that change its size, this is only applies to combat, and does not increase the depth at which the player's breath meter can refill underwater.
Holding it while having a Fish Bowl in the armor slot will prevent the player from drowning.

History

---

# 文档：Butcher's Chainsaw

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：butchers chainsaw
- 来源：wiki/Butcher's_Chainsaw

debuff.]]

The Butcher's Chainsaw is a Hardmode, post-Plantera chainsaw that has a 2.5% /  (1/40 / ) chance to drop from Butchers during a Solar Eclipse. Upon striking an enemy or critter, it will emit sparks akin to the projectiles produced by the Wand of Sparking, dealing damage to nearby enemies/extra segments as well. These sparks deal  melee damage /  magic damage, meaning that any  melee /  magic buffs, accessories, or armor will boost them. They also have a chance to inflict the Hellfire debuff.

The Demonic modifier lacks the 15% knockback gain.

Notes
Because its sparks deal magic damage, it can trigger set bonuses of the Spectre armor, either healing the player or doing more damage.
Due to its high damage and the presence of an on-hit effect, some may consider this primarily a melee weapon instead of a tool. It's not a bad choice for one, either.

Tips
Due to its high damage per hit and ability to hit enemies multiple times in rapid succession, the Butcher's Chainsaw has a high . With damage boosting modifiers/accessories, the weapon has a higher potential  than other weapons available after Plantera, including the Terra Blade.
As the Hellfire debuff only applies to enemies hit by the sparks, not by the chainsaw itself, careful positioning is required to make full use of the weapon's damage potential.
While this weapon's best modifier is technically Godly or Demonic, it is recommended that you use it with Ruthless or another knockback-reducing modifier, as it allows for you to more frequently hit enemies.

Trivia
The Butcher and the Butcher's Chainsaw are both a tribute to the 1974 American slasher film .
The Butcher's Chainsaw has the highest base damage and knockback of any tool, due to being designed for combat.
Enemies immune to the On Fire! debuff do not resist the damage from the sparks.
It is the only melee weapon that can deal magic damage, and one of the only few melee weapons able to inflict a debuff without using Flasks.
Like all chainsaws, it will stop functioning after a minute and has to be re-started.

History
References

---

# 文档：Cactus Pickaxe

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：cactus pickaxe
- 来源：wiki/Cactus_Pickaxe

The Cactus Pickaxe is an early-game pickaxe. It has the same pickaxe power as the Copper and Tin Pickaxes, but is slightly slower in terms of mining speed. Like other low-tier pickaxes, it can only mine blocks and ores weaker than Meteorite (which requires a pickaxe with at least 50% pickaxe power, e.g. a Tungsten Pickaxe).

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
The Cactus Pickaxe does not require ores or an Anvil to craft, which means that it can be obtained even before venturing underground.

History

---

# 文档：Cactus Sword

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：cactus sword
- 来源：wiki/Cactus_Sword

.]]

The Cactus Sword is an early-game sword. It deals more damage compared to early weapons (dealing the same damage as the Copper Broadsword) and it has more range. It appears much more robust and dangerous compared to wooden and other early-game swords, and can be very useful until one finds Silver or Tungsten to forge a more effective sword.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
Selling a Cactus Sword is × as profitable as selling its ingredients.

History

# 文档：Candy Cane Pickaxe

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：candy cane pickaxe
- 来源：wiki/Candy_Cane_Pickaxe

The Candy Cane Pickaxe is a third-tier pickaxe that has the same pickaxe power as the Gold Pickaxe, and therefore is capable of mining Demonite and Crimtane Ores. It is obtained from Presents dropped during the Christmas event, with a chance of 0.634% in pre-Hardmode and 0.592% in Hardmode. It has the same mining speed as the Gold Pickaxe, therefore it is a good replacement of all pickaxes below Platinum Pickaxe with the exception of the Bone Pickaxe.

Trivia
It is misspelled as "CnadyCanePickaxe" in the game's source files.
Opening presents to get the Candy Cane Pickaxe is a way to get a third-tier pickaxe before getting Gold/Platinum ore.

History

# 文档：Candy Cane Sword

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：candy cane sword
- 来源：wiki/Candy_Cane_Sword

. Note how the player's hands barely touch the sword during its swinging animation.]]

The Candy Cane Sword is a Christmas-themed sword that has a 0.639% (pre-Hardmode) / 0.596% (Hardmode) chance to be obtained from Presents dropped during the Christmas event.

Notes
Its pickaxe equivalent is the Candy Cane Pickaxe.

Tips
This sword could be quite useful early on, as it deals  base damage and has a large range compared to other pre-Hardmode swords.

# 文档：Candy Corn Rifle

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：candy corn rifle
- 来源：wiki/Candy_Corn_Rifle

The Candy Corn Rifle is a Hardmode post-Plantera gun that auto-fires Candy Corn as ammunition. It is dropped rarely (1.79–14.29% chance) by the Pumpking during the Pumpkin Moon event. Candy Corn drops along with the weapon, and can be purchased from the Arms Dealer when a Candy Corn Rifle is in the player's inventory.

Candy Corn shots pierce up to two enemies and ricochet up to four times, dissipating on their fifth impact or third enemy hit. The gun has a high fire rate, but unlike most guns, its projectiles are affected by gravity and do not remain airborne for long, though their ricochet ability helps them gain distance.

The Candy Corn Rifle has a base  critical strike chance as opposed to the usual 4%.

Tips
Its piercing ability may help on worm-type enemies, such as The Destroyer, and crowds of enemies. It is also a good weapon to use against the Frost Moon.
It is especially useful on gun-only playthroughs. The second-best option, High Velocity Bullets, deal less damage to the second and third enemies hit on the , and do not pierce enemies at all on the other versions.

Trivia
This weapon appears to be based off of the , a real-life pistol used during World War I and II, with candy corns attached to the top and on the magazine.

History

---

# 文档：Cascade

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：cascade
- 来源：wiki/Cascade

debuff.]]

The Cascade is a pre-Hardmode yoyo. With a base reach of 15.5 tiles and a maximum spin duration of 16 seconds before returning, it is the most powerful yoyo available pre-Hardmode. Upon striking an enemy, it has a chance to inflict the On Fire! debuff.

The Cascade has a  chance of being dropped by any enemy in or near The Underworld after Skeletron has been defeated. Once Hardmode begins, it will no longer drop, as the stronger Hel-Fire will drop instead.

Tips
When used, it will emit embers which can be used as a light source stemming 6 blocks away (13×13 tiles sized light; 6 tiles from all sides, excluding the source).
Trivia
The name Cascade comes from one of the many different yoyos sold by . Their logo is the symbol listed at the bottom of the item's tooltip. There is a notable difference in color, though: While the real-life original is light blue, the Cascade appears to look like a CODE2 Dragon Slayer.
Unlike many other items, the Cascade's availability is very limited, as it can only be obtained between defeating Skeletron and the Wall of Flesh.
It is one of the only weapons that becomes completely unobtainable in Hardmode, the other being the Gray Zapinator.
The Wall of Flesh can drop the Cascade.

History

---

# 文档：Celebration

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：celebration
- 来源：wiki/Celebration

.]]

The Celebration is a Hardmode  post-Golem /  post-Moon Lord launcher that fires two rockets at once (but only consumes one unit of rocket) that travel a short distance before exploding. When the rockets detonate, they display patterns and colors similar to fireworks. On the , it has a  chance to be dropped by the Moon Lord. On the , it is sold by the Party Girl after defeating Golem, and its successor is the Celebration Mk2, which takes its place as the new launcher-type Moon Lord drop.

The Celebration has a base 4% critical strike chance, similar to most ranged weapons. On the , it has a base 14% critical strike chance opposed to the usual 4%.

Notes
The Celebration disables the unique functions of nearly all rockets; Rocket II and Rocket IV do not destroy tiles, liquid rockets do not place/remove liquids, and Cluster Rocket I / Cluster Rocket II do not fragment.
The player cannot be damaged by their own rocket explosions while firing this weapon.
Enemies will take damage from both the rocket and the explosion created by it.

Tips
The numerous glowing particles that are created by the Celebration's explosions travel through walls, which can make them useful for finding ores.
It is very effective at harvesting plants like Glowing Mushrooms and Life Fruits, as well as destroying thorny bushes and clearing out Spider Caves due to its large explosion radius.

Trivia
The explosions bear a heavy resemblance to those from Firework Rockets. Additionally,  it is internally referred to as .

History

---

# 文档：Celebration Mk2

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：celebration mk2
- 来源：wiki/Celebration_Mk2

The Celebration Mk2 is a Hardmode, post-Moon Lord launcher that is an upgraded version of the Celebration. Upon use, it fires one of 7 different special rocket types, regardless of the type of ammunition used. While it retains the destructive properties of its ammunition, it cannot place or delete liquids with liquid rockets. The weapon has a  chance to be dropped by the Moon Lord.

Notes
Explosions created by this weapon will not damage the player who fired it.
The projectiles from this weapon cannot be reflected by enemies like the , as they will instantly explode on contact.
The seven different rocket types are distinguished by color. Although their damage, critical strike chance, and knockback is identical, they behave differently:
The red rocket pierces enemies that are not at point-blank range.
The orange rocket fires in an arc which is affected by the relative position of the cursor vertically, and has a huge area-of-effect as a ground shockwave. It also increases in size as it flies.
The yellow rocket travels in a straight line (visually zig-zagging) until it begins to taper off due to gravity.
The green rocket homes onto enemies similarly to Chlorophyte Bullets.
Two blue rockets are fired at once, in a helix pattern, traveling straight.
Three purple rockets are fired at once, in a shotgun-spread pattern.
The pink rocket appears to have no special behavior.
All rocket types are affected by gravity to varying extents, unlike the Celebration's projectiles.
When the  button is pressed, the type of rocket that is fired is determined randomly. When it is held, however, the rockets have a fixed cycle: Red → Orange → Yellow → Green → Blue → Purple → Pink → Red.

Tips
Because of its fast use time, mining large areas out can be accomplished extremely quickly with Rocket II, Rocket IV, or Mini Nuke II--upwards of a thousand blocks every 5-8 seconds depending on ammo. Though it is faster at digging than traditional tools, it suffers greatly in accuracy when compared to the Shroomite Digging Claw, the Drill Containment Unit, or even a less powerful rocket launcher such as the Snowman Cannon.
The erratic rockets in conjunction with the quick fire rate make the Celebration Mk2 excellent at clearing waves of ground enemies (such as in the Solar Eclipse or Pumpkin Moon).
It can take down mini-bosses such as the Ice Queen in seconds.

Trivia
The Celebration Mk2 was first teased on March 22, 2018, in celebration of Yoraiz0r's birthday.
It can be considered a pseudo-upgrade of the Snowman Cannon and Stynger.
The weapon's sprite slightly resembles the Vortex Beater.
Despite being the formal successor to the Celebration, the Celebration Mk2 is generally less effective against distant enemies.

History
References

---

# 文档：Chain Guillotines

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：chain guillotines
- 来源：wiki/Chain_Guillotines

.]]

The Chain Guillotines are a Hardmode autoswinging flail dropped by Corrupt Mimics. The weapon fires two blade projectiles attached to chains in rapid succession, with a maximum range of 22 tiles.

It can also pierce multiple enemies when standing inside them or very close, therefore being a lot more effective at close quarters.

Tips
The weapon can be easily reforged for a few Silver, making it cheap and easy to achieve good and effective modifiers.
If riding a fast mount, such as the Pirate Ship Mount or the Unicorn Mount, while firing in front of the player, it will cause a large number of guillotines to be thrown.
The Chain Guillotines are quite effective against The Twins since their movement pattern can set up easy hits for this weapon.

Trivia
The weapon can be considered to be the Hardmode upgrade to the Chain Knife, sharing similar characteristics and a similar design.
The name of the weapon can be a reference to the , an apparatus designed to carrying out executions by beheading.
It has an extraordinarily low sell price for a Hardmode weapon.

History

---

# 文档：Chain Gun

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：chain gun
- 来源：wiki/Chain_Gun

. Notice how the bullet spread varies.]]

The Chain Gun is a Hardmode, post-Plantera Gun, and is currently the fastest gun by rate of fire to date. It has a 50% chance of not consuming Ammunition, but fires in random directions of about a 30 degree spread, similar to the Gatligator. It is dropped sometimes (5.55%-12.5% /  chance) by Santa-NK1 during the Frost Moon event.

Its best Modifier is Rapid. However, Godly may be preferred to save ammo.

Tips
Chlorophyte Bullets pair well with the Chain Gun, as their homing ability negates the gun's singular weakness of accuracy.
Note that it may cause framerate drops on low-end computers, due to its streak effect.
Crystal Bullets also can deal large amounts of damage to larger, stationary enemies and bosses such as the Golem.
When forged with the Rapid or Frenzying prefix, the Chain Gun will gain 33% speed. This happens due to rounding down its already insane shooting speed (4 to 3). The Godly prefix is better for players preferring to save ammo, at the cost of slightly less damage.  for Godly: . The  for Rapid: .
This weapon may be a good option for damaging enemies very quickly to trigger abilities like the Nebula Armor's.
Because of its absurd rate of fire, the Endless Musket Pouch should be used in regular combat to save your more valuable shots for boss fights.
This is made especially evident when considering, firing at 15 shots per second means that even a full stack of 999 bullets at 50% ammo consumption will still be entirely used, on average, within 2 minutes and 13 seconds. Considering a stack of chlorophyte bullets needs 17 chlorophyte bars (85 ore, it would need 15 bars (90 ore) on earlier versions), this could be seen as very expensive and impractical or not a full return on investment in terms of time spent obtaining the ammo compared to time utilising the ammo.

Trivia
The gun appears to be a miniature version of Santa-NK1's arm.
A Chain Gun with Rapid or Frenzying as a prefix will have a use time of 3, which is the fastest of any ranged weapon in the game. The S.D.M.G. does more damage yet fires slightly slower.
The Chain Gun fires 3 more shots per second than the S.D.M.G. with no speed modifiers.
A Chain Gun fires 15 shots/second which translates to 900 shots/minute.
With the Rapid or Frenzying Modifier, the Chain Gun fires 20 shots/second, which translates to 1200 shots/minute.
The Chain Gun is one of eleven guns in Terraria to share its name with a type of real gun, the others being the Uzi, Shotgun, Sniper Rifle, Revolver, Red Ryder, Flintlock Pistol, Tactical Shotgun, Musket, Handgun and the Quad-Barrel Shotgun.
In real life, a chain gun is a single barreled weapon powered by an external power source.

History

---

# 文档：Chain Knife

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：chain knife
- 来源：wiki/Chain_Knife

. Note how the Knife immediately returns when hitting a block.]]

The Chain Knife is a pre-hardmode melee weapon that throws a Knife-like projectile attached to a chain, similar to Flails, but the attack button cannot be held to wield it continuously. It only extends a short distance of 12 tiles, though its quick use time and velocity may make it a good early to mid-game weapon. It has a  chance of being dropped by a Cave Bat.

Tips
It is good at killing quick flying monsters. It is also good at killing large groups of Wall Creepers, and keeping the second form of the Brain of Cthulhu at bay.
A Bat Statue can be used to easily farm bats to obtain the Chain Knife. Note that drop rates from statue enemies are reduced by a factor of 5.
If the player finds a Bat statue underground before beating skeletron, they can put in onto a wire (in between a trap and its pressure plate) and step on the pressure plate to make a crude way of spawning more bats.
Although the weapon attacks quite quickly, it is very difficult to stunlock enemies with it due to its low knockback.

Trivia
Unlike most flails, this flail will not spin around the player, but instead attack the target directly before returning to the player.
Despite having worse stats overall, it sells for more than the Chain Guillotines on the s.

See also
Chain Guillotines, its Hardmode counterpart

History

---

# 文档：Chik

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：chik
- 来源：wiki/Chik

The Chik is a Hardmode yoyo. It is relatively easy to craft when first entering Hardmode.

It has a spin duration of 16 seconds, and can reach up to 18 tiles.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
The Chik is a decent melee weapon for early Hardmode, although it does significantly less damage than the Night's Edge. However, the yoyo is able to hit enemies at a further distance than the Night's Edge, so for some players it could be a better weapon. The Chik can also serve as an alternative to the Crystal Storm.
The Chik is statistically the weakest yoyo available before fighting one of the 3 Mechanical bosses. Compared to the Amarok or the Hel-Fire, it has a much lower damage output but its accessibility at the beginning of Hardmode helps make it a good choice before obtaining better yoyos.

Trivia
The name Chik comes from one of the many different yoyos sold by , and is the signature model of their sponsored player, Igor Korzhev. Their logo is the symbol listed at the bottom of the item's tooltip.
The Chik is the only craftable One Drop yoyo.

History

---

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：chlorophyte chainsaw
- 来源：wiki/Chlorophyte_Chainsaw

The Chlorophyte Chainsaw is a Hardmode chainsaw that is crafted from Chlorophyte Bars, and is the chainsaw alternative to the Chlorophyte Greataxe. Like all axes and chainsaws, it is used to cut down trees, cacti and giant glowing mushrooms.

Crafting
Recipes
History

---

# 文档：Chlorophyte Claymore

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：chlorophyte claymore
- 来源：wiki/Chlorophyte_Claymore

.]]

The Chlorophyte Claymore is a Hardmode sword. It is the slower but more powerful of the two Chlorophyte swords, the other being the Chlorophyte Saber. Upon swinging, it fires a Chlorophyte Orb in the direction of the cursor which deals the same damage as the sword itself. The orb does not consume any mana, but has a one second cooldown. The Chlorophyte Claymore can be swung regardless of whether the orb is ready, but is incapable of autoswing.

The Chlorophyte Orb behaves the same way as the Magic Dagger, but has a larger hitbox. Like the Magic Dagger, the orb travels in a straight line at a constant speed for a short distance before dropping steeply and falling faster. The orb can pierce through infinite enemies. Upon impact, the orb implodes and emits a light, sparkly-green particle effect.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Increasing melee speed will increase the speed and range of the orb, but not the rate at which it can be fired.
Versions prior to 1.4.1 the Chlorophyte Claymore is a useful weapon before you have the True Excalibur or True Night's Edge.

Trivia
All existing Tizonas in 1.3.0.1-equivalent worlds were replaced by Chlorophyte Claymores upon updating.
While many people associate the word 'claymore' with the , the word actually originates from the .

History

---

# 文档：Chlorophyte Drill

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：chlorophyte drill
- 来源：wiki/Chlorophyte_Drill

The Chlorophyte Drill is a Hardmode drill crafted using Chlorophyte Bars. It has equivalent power to the Drax and Pickaxe Axe. It is an alternative to the Chlorophyte Pickaxe. Appearances and sounds aside, the two tools are nearly functionally identical. The drill, however, is half a second faster. But, unlike pickaxes, most drills cannot be reforged with size, speed, or knockback modifiers. The Chlorophyte Drill can mine any block except Lihzahrd Bricks.

Crafting
Recipes
History

---

# 文档：Chlorophyte Greataxe

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：chlorophyte greataxe
- 来源：wiki/Chlorophyte_Greataxe

The Chlorophyte Greataxe is a Hardmode axe that is obtainable after defeating all three mechanical bosses, and is the axe alternative to the Chlorophyte Chainsaw. It is crafted from Chlorophyte Bars.

Crafting
Recipes
History

---

# 文档：Chlorophyte Jackhammer

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：chlorophyte jackhammer
- 来源：wiki/Chlorophyte_Jackhammer

The Chlorophyte Jackhammer is a Hardmode hammer crafted with 18 Chlorophyte Bars at a Mythril or Orichalcum Anvil. Even though it has a lower hammer power than other hammers, it destroys fairly quickly and serves practically all hammer purposes.

Mechanical tools can only have universal prefixes, because they can't have their speed modified.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
This tool functions much like drills or chainsaws, in that it mines via a "machine" with constant directed contact, rather than being swung repeatedly at targets. While there are several drills and chainsaws fulfilling pickaxe and axe duties via a machine method, the Chlorophyte Jackhammer is the only such tool fulfilling hammer duty.
On the , the use time of the Chlorophyte Jackhammer is significantly slower. Other hammers, such as the Hammush or Spectre Hamaxe, are able to break walls faster.

Achievements
Trivia
In real life, s are actually digging tools rather than hammering tools.
The Chlorophyte Jackhammer is the only jackhammer in-game.

History

---

# 文档：Chlorophyte Partisan

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：chlorophyte partisan
- 来源：wiki/Chlorophyte_Partisan

The Chlorophyte Partisan is a Hardmode spear crafted using Chlorophyte Bars. It beats all of the "standard" ore spears, dealing  more damage than the best of the standard Hardmode ore spears, the Titanium Trident. When thrust, it emits a cloud of spores from the tip of the spear at the apex of the thrust, which looks exactly the same as the Chlorophyte Saber's projectile. The cloud travels a short distance in the direction the spear was thrust before fading out, and it is able to penetrate through solid blocks and multiple enemies, but it does not inflict any knockback.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Contrary to what its inventory sprite suggests, the Chlorophyte Partisan is actually quite long. Because it additionally has a spore cloud that follows its thrust, the Partisan has a massive range, possibly the second largest (after the North Pole) range of spear-type weapons.
The spore cloud has the same damage range as the spear itself, and is able to deal critical hits.
When using the Magma Stone or Fire Gauntlet, the spores do not have the flame particles, and cannot inflict the On Fire! debuff.

Tips
It can be used to damage enemies through blocks via the spore cloud it emits, but not by the actual spear. Because of this, the user must be at a specific distance in order for the spore cloud to make contact. The cloud will not make contact if the enemy is too close, since it is only released at the tip of the spear during the apex of the thrust.
Because of the damage and knockback, it is a useful weapon against groups of enemies. It can knock back several enemies at once, this also makes it useful for defense.

Trivia
Its spores are exactly the same as the Chlorophyte Saber's.
All existing Tonbogiris in 1.3.0.1-equivalent worlds were replaced by Chlorophyte Partisans upon updating.

History

---

# 文档：Chlorophyte Pickaxe

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：chlorophyte pickaxe
- 来源：wiki/Chlorophyte_Pickaxe

The Chlorophyte Pickaxe is a Hardmode pickaxe, and is the pickaxe counterpart to the Chlorophyte Drill, having a slower mining speed but longer range and benefiting from mining speed bonuses. It can mine any block except Lihzahrd Bricks.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Just like the Picksaw, it has a slight increased range.
The pickaxe is only beneficial for +1 range, making the Pickaxe Axe a better tool overall.

History

# 文档：Chlorophyte Saber

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：chlorophyte saber
- 来源：wiki/Chlorophyte_Saber

. Note how the projectile can pierce walls.]]

The Chlorophyte Saber is a Hardmode autoswing sword, and is the less powerful, but faster sword of the two that can be crafted from Chlorophyte Bars, with the other being the Chlorophyte Claymore. When swung, it emits a damaging short-ranged spore cloud, which looks exactly the same as the Chlorophyte Partisan's projectile. The spore cloud is able to penetrate through solid blocks and multiple enemies, but it does not inflict any knockback and can only be cast once per second.

Recipes

# 文档：Chlorophyte Shotbow

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：chlorophyte shotbow
- 来源：wiki/Chlorophyte_Shotbow

The Chlorophyte Shotbow is a Hardmode repeater crafted using Chlorophyte Bars. It shoots  2–3 /  2–4 arrows per shot but only consumes one arrow, as with all other multi-shot bows. Arrows fired with the Chlorophyte Shotbow cannot be recovered regardless of the type of arrow used.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
When used with arrows that deal piercing damage (Unholy Arrows, Jester's Arrows, Hellfire Arrows,  Chlorophyte Arrows), only one arrow will deal damage to each enemy per shot, making the shotbow far less effective.
The average number of arrows fired per shot is  2.33 /  2.7.
Due to variance in the number of arrows fired per shot, the effective base damage of the Shotbow ranges between  and   /   damage. It is also more strongly affected by the base damage of arrows than single-shot bows or repeaters.

Tips
The Shotbow's lower base damage can be compensated for by using Ichor Arrows, which decrease defense.
The Chlorophyte Shotbow is a devastating weapon when used with Holy Arrows, providing great single-target  while also summoning storms of 6 to 12 stars per shot, which offer crowd control. It is one of the best weapons to use against Plantera.
While the Shotbow has less base damage per arrow than the Hallowed Repeater, the spread is tight enough that targets will usually be hit with more than one arrow per shot, resulting in higher damage output overall.
However, the Hallowed Repeater remains superior to the shotbow when using piercing arrows (with the possible exception of Hellfire Arrows against dense crowds), due to the latter's anti-synergy with piercing arrows.

Trivia
Shotbow is a portmanteau of the words ' and '.
All existing Vulcan Repeaters in 1.3.0.1-equivalent worlds were replaced by Chlorophyte Shotbows upon updating.

History

---

# 文档：Chlorophyte Warhammer

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：chlorophyte warhammer
- 来源：wiki/Chlorophyte_Warhammer

The Chlorophyte Warhammer is a Hardmode hammer that can be crafted from Chlorophyte Bars. It is one of the strongest hammers in the game, and like all hammers, can be used to remove background walls and to create half-blocks and slopes.

Crafting
Recipes
Achievements
Trivia
The Chlorophyte Warhammer has the highest damage from all obtainable hammers, bested only by the unobtainable .

History

# 文档：Christmas Tree Sword

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：christmas tree sword
- 来源：wiki/Christmas_Tree_Sword

The Christmas Tree Sword is a Hardmode sword that fires ornament projectiles when swung. It has a chance to drop from Everscreams during the Frost Moon event.

On the , swinging the Christmas Tree Sword hurls a star ornament projectile in a straight line in the direction of the cursor which releases four randomly colored ornaments on its way. These ornaments move a few tiles, linger in the air for 50 ticks (0.83 seconds), and then become affected by gravity and drop to the ground. They break upon contact with enemies or blocks. All projectiles deal 100% of the sword's damage ( base damage), and the colored ornaments only inflict a quarter of the sword's knockback ().

On the , swinging the Christmas Tree Sword launches a single ornament in the direction of the cursor which is affected by gravity. It deals 50% of the sword's damage (  /   base damage) and breaks upon contact with enemies or blocks. Unlike most other late-game swords, the Christmas Tree Sword is not capable of autoswing in these versions.

Notes
The range of the star projectile is 18.58 tiles. It releases an ornament every 10 ticks (0.167 seconds) and breaks 100 ticks (1.67 seconds) after firing. Unlike the four other ornaments, it does not drop to the ground and instead vanishes in place.
The first and third ornaments will be released to the right (in the direction of flight) and the second and fourth to the left. Since each consecutive ornament has slightly less range, the five projectiles usually align in a roughly triangular shape.
Like the North Pole, the Christmas Tree Sword can be used to carpet bomb areas with projectiles. Unlike the North Pole, this sword's projectiles are not affected by wind and do not have a projectile cap. An effective strategy for many events is to use Flasks and rain down projectiles from up high.
Since most of this sword's damage comes from many smaller projectiles, its damage is significantly enhanced by Ichor or Betsy's Wrath debuffs.

Tips
The ability to drop Ornaments can be useful during events.

Trivia
Prior to , this item was also referred to as Christmas Sword in the source code.

See also
Christmas Tree, a decorative item sold by Santa Claus.

History
References

---

The Classy Cane is a Hardmode melee weapon that has a  chance to be dropped by the Tax Collector NPC, who also uses it to protect himself. It is swung like a broadsword.

Although it can only be obtained in Hardmode, it does low damage when compared to other hardmode weapons. It may not be useful as a practical weapon.

# 文档：Clinger Staff

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：clinger staff
- 来源：wiki/Clinger_Staff

and a Unicorn.]]

The Clinger Staff is a Hardmode magic weapon that has a  chance to be dropped by a Corrupt Mimic. It generates a 15-tile-high wall of Cursed Flame, which deals damage and  inflicts the Cursed Inferno debuff to any enemy that walks through it. The point at which the wall is generated must be in a direct line of sight; otherwise, the wall will not generate, though still consume mana. The Clinger Staff's wall lasts for 5 minutes. Only one wall may be generated at a time; if another is summoned, the first one will vanish.

Notes
The Clinger Staff can work on platforms and on solid blocks. It does not work on angled blocks or Planter Boxes.
It does work with either of the set bonuses of the Spectre armor.
Despite having a listed critical strike chance of 4%, the Clinger Staff cannot deal critical hits.

Tips
Due to its high knockback, this weapon is excellent at keeping enemies away from the player.
The Clinger Staff excels at killing worms, as multiple segments can be hit by the wall.
This weapon can be useful while in the Dungeon for keeping enemies away due to its narrow passageways.
This weapon is useful during the Old One's Army event, as many enemies cannot pass through due to its high knockback.
Since the critical strike chance of a projectile is not based on the weapon that fired it but rather the one the player is currently holding, this weapon pairs very well with magic weapons with a higher base critical strike chance than usual, most notably .
The Clinger Staff can be seen as an alternative to the Nimbus Rod, as both weapons summon a vertical "wall" that damages enemies. However, the Clinger Staff is less effective against flying bosses such as Skeletron Prime, as its wall doesn't have unlimited height.

Trivia
Despite its name and appearance, the staff is not dropped by Clingers.
This weapon has the highest mana usage of all magic weapons in a single use.

History

---

# 文档：Clockwork Assault Rifle

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：clockwork assault rifle
- 来源：wiki/Clockwork_Assault_Rifle

.]]

The Clockwork Assault Rifle is a Hardmode gun that fires in three-round bursts. It has a  /  chance to be dropped by the Wall of Flesh. Only the first shot out of the three will consume ammunition. The  button can be held for repeated fire.

Notes
The Clockwork Assault Rifle fires three shots, each one with a delay of 4 ticks, for a total of 12 ticks. The delay between one burst and the next is 14 ticks. One complete use of the weapon (including the delay) takes 26 ticks.
On the , the Clockwork Assault Rifle has a knockback stat of Very Weak.

Tips
This weapon's high rate of fire and low ammo consumption make it ideal for crowd control. These same strengths also make it effective against bosses, especially for some of the mechanical bosses.
This weapon can be easily obtained early in Hardmode. This makes it an ideal weapon to get after killing the Wall of Flesh.
This weapon's low ammo consumption makes it ideal for people who use expensive ammo, even well into the late-game.
When using Crystal or Ichor Bullets as ammo, the player can easily defeat Plantera and even Golem.
Having the Rapid modifier will greatly increase its fire rate, making it fire faster than the Minishark and doing as much  as the Megashark.

Trivia
On the  and  Nintendo Switch Version, the Clockwork Assault Rifle used to not have the delay between ticks, making it very viable far into Hardmode. However, this has been removed with the 1.3 and 1.4.0.1 updates respectively.
The Clockwork Assault Rifle, Star Cannon, Flintlock Pistol and the Minishark are some of the only guns without knockback.
The Clockwork Assault Rifle is potentially a reference to the  assault rifle, a real-world prototype rifle that fired in three-round bursts and had a clockwork feeding system for the weapon, however the G11's three-round "hyperburst" is fired at 2,100 RPM.

History

# 文档：Cobalt Chainsaw

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：cobalt chainsaw
- 来源：wiki/Cobalt_Chainsaw

The Cobalt Chainsaw is a Hardmode chainsaw that is obtainable after defeating the Wall of Flesh, and is the chainsaw alternative to the Cobalt Waraxe. Although the Cobalt Waraxe and Cobalt Chainsaw have equal axe powers, the waraxe chops down trees at a faster rate (assuming no buffs/modifiers are in effect). It is one of the earliest available axe tools upon entering Hardmode, along with its slightly stronger ore alternative, the Palladium Chainsaw. Like all axes and chainsaws, it is used to cut down trees, cacti, and Giant Glowing Mushrooms.

Crafting
Recipes

# 文档：Cobalt Drill

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：cobalt drill
- 来源：wiki/Cobalt_Drill

The Cobalt Drill is a Hardmode drill, and is the drill alternative to the Cobalt Pickaxe. Appearances and sounds aside, the two tools are nearly functionally identical. The drill, however is half a second faster. But, unlike pickaxes, most drills cannot be reforged with size, speed, or knockback modifiers.

It can mine any block except Adamantite Ore, Titanium Ore, Chlorophyte Ore, and Lihzahrd Bricks.

Crafting
Recipes

# 文档：Cobalt Naginata

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：cobalt naginata
- 来源：wiki/Cobalt_Naginata

.]]

The Cobalt Naginata is a Hardmode spear crafted using Cobalt Bars. It lies at the lowest tier of Hardmode ore spears along with the Palladium Pike, and is the weaker of the two, making it the weakest Hardmode exclusive spear.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
The Dark Lance not only has slightly better stats, but also is somewhat easily obtainable Pre-Hardmode, making it a good alternative. However, the Naginata has a slightly higher range.

Trivia
A  is a traditional Japanese pole weapon, used by samurai. In reality, a naginata is intended to be swung rather than thrusted.

# 文档：Cobalt Pickaxe

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：cobalt pickaxe
- 来源：wiki/Cobalt_Pickaxe

The Cobalt Pickaxe is a Hardmode pickaxe, and is the pickaxe alternative to the Cobalt Drill, having a slower mining speed but longer range and benefiting from mining speed bonuses. Its alternate ore version, the Palladium Pickaxe, is slightly stronger than the Cobalt Pickaxe.

It can mine any block except Adamantite Ore, Titanium Ore, Chlorophyte Ore, and Lihzahrd Bricks.

Recipes

The Cobalt Repeater is a Hardmode repeater crafted solely using Cobalt Bars. It is one of the first repeaters that can be obtained once entering Hardmode, the other being the Palladium Repeater. It is also the weakest of all repeaters, which is not to say that it cannot be effective.

# 文档：Cobalt Sword

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：cobalt sword
- 来源：wiki/Cobalt_Sword

. Note how the long range allows for the reach of multiple Dummies.]]

The Cobalt Sword is a Hardmode sword crafted solely using Cobalt Bars, and lies at the lowest tier of Hardmode ore swords along with the Palladium Sword. Like other Hardmode ore swords, it is capable of autoswing. It can also swing faster than all of the other Hardmode ore swords.

Crafting
Recipes
Tips
It might be advisable to skip the Cobalt Sword because of its lackluster stats compared to Phasesabers and the pre-Hardmode Night's Edge. Compared to Phasesabers, the Cobalt Sword only has slightly higher knockback, while the only disadvantages the Night's Edge has are its complicated crafting as well as (outside of the ) its slow use and lack of inherent autoswing.

Trivia
The Cobalt Sword bears a strong resemblance to the Muramasa and the Japanese , and fits with the "Feudal Asia" theme of other Cobalt weapons and armor.
The Cobalt Sword has the fastest speed of all Hardmode Ore swords. This may be because it resembles the real-world Katana, a very light, thin blade (which is also in Terraria).

History

---

# 文档：Cobalt Waraxe

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：cobalt waraxe
- 来源：wiki/Cobalt_Waraxe

The Cobalt Waraxe is a Hardmode axe that is obtainable after defeating the Wall of Flesh, and is the axe alternative to the Cobalt Chainsaw. Like all other axes and chainsaws, it is solely used to cut down trees, cacti, and Giant Glowing Mushrooms.

Crafting
Recipes
Notes
Although the Cobalt Waraxe and Cobalt Chainsaw have equal axe powers, the waraxe is faster than the chainsaw at chopping down trees (assuming no buffs or modifiers are at play). It also dishes out more damage and knockback, making this the obvious best choice for players that are new into Hardmode since it can be utilized effectively as both a tool and a weapon. However, once the player obtains and equips accessories such as the Celestial Stone and Celestial Shell, the Cobalt Chainsaw becomes faster than the Cobalt Waraxe at chopping down trees, but still remains far behind in the damage category.
It is one of the earliest available axe tools upon entering Hardmode, along with its slightly stronger alternative, the Palladium Waraxe.

History

# 文档：Code 1

- 类别：weapons
- 标识：code 1
- 来源：wiki/Code_1

The Code 1 is a yoyo purchased from the Traveling Merchant for  once the Eye of Cthulhu has been defeated.

It has a spin duration of 9 seconds, and can reach up to 13.5 tiles.

Notes
The Code 1 is the second strongest pre-Skeletron yoyo (the Hive-Five is the strongest).

Trivia
The name Code 1 comes from one of the many different yoyos sold by One Drop. Their logo is the symbol listed at the bottom of the item tooltips.
CODE1 stands for "Community/One Drop Experiment #1". Its design was created by the YoYoNation community.
